1/* ============================================================================
2 * UltrafastSecp256k1 -- ufsecp C ABI Implementation
3 * ============================================================================
4 * Wraps the C++ UltrafastSecp256k1 library behind the opaque ufsecp_ctx and
5 * the ufsecp_* function surface. All conversions between opaque byte arrays
6 * and internal C++ types happen here -- nothing leaks.
7 *
8 * Build with: -DUFSECP_BUILDING (sets dllexport on Windows)
9 * ============================================================================ */
10
11#ifndef UFSECP_BUILDING
12#define UFSECP_BUILDING
13#endif
14
15#include "ufsecp.h"
16

18#include <cstdint>
19#include <cstdlib>
20#include <algorithm>
21#include <array>
22#include <limits>
23#include <string>
24#include <new>
25#include <vector>

75using Scalar = secp256k1::fast::Scalar;
76using Point = secp256k1::fast::Point;
77using FE = secp256k1::fast::FieldElement;
78
79/* ===========================================================================
80 * Context definition (opaque to callers)
81 * =========================================================================== */
82
83struct ufsecp_ctx {
84 ufsecp_error_t last_err;
85 char last_msg[128];
86 bool selftest_ok;
87};
88
89static void ctx_clear_err(ufsecp_ctx* ctx) {
90 ctx->last_err = UFSECP_OK;
91 ctx->last_msg[0] = '\0';
92}
93
94static ufsecp_error_t ctx_set_err(ufsecp_ctx* ctx, ufsecp_error_t err, const char* msg) {
95 ctx->last_err = err;
96 if (msg) {
97 /* Portable safe copy without MSVC deprecation warning */
98 size_t i = 0;
99 /* cppcheck-suppress arrayIndexOutOfBoundsCond ; i bounded by sizeof(last_msg)-1 */
100 for (; i < sizeof(ctx->last_msg) - 1 && msg[i]; ++i) {
101 ctx->last_msg[i] = msg[i];
102}
103 ctx->last_msg[i] = '\0';
104 } else {
105 ctx->last_msg[0] = '\0';
106 }
107 return err;
108}
109
110/* ===========================================================================
111 * Internal helpers (same pattern as existing c_api, but with error model)
112 * =========================================================================== */
113
114// All scalar parsing uses the strict variants below.
115// Message hashes (32-byte) are handled as raw byte arrays (no scalar reduction).
116
117// Strict parser for secret keys: rejects 0, values >= n. No reduction.
118static inline bool scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(const uint8_t b[32], Scalar& out) {
119 std::array<uint8_t, 32> arr;
120 std::memcpy(arr.data(), b, 32);
121 return Scalar::parse_bytes_strict_nonzero(arr, out);
122}
123
124// Strict parser for tweaks: rejects values >= n, allows 0. No reduction.
125static inline bool scalar_parse_strict(const uint8_t b[32], Scalar& out) {
126 std::array<uint8_t, 32> arr;
127 std::memcpy(arr.data(), b, 32);
128 return Scalar::parse_bytes_strict(arr, out);
129}
130
131static inline void scalar_to_bytes(const Scalar& s, uint8_t out[32]) {
132 auto arr = s.to_bytes();
133 std::memcpy(out, arr.data(), 32);
134}
135
136static inline Point point_from_compressed(const uint8_t pub[33]);
137
138namespace {
139
140constexpr std::size_t kMuSig2KeyAggHeaderLen = 38;
141constexpr std::size_t kMuSig2KeyAggCoeffLen = 32;
142constexpr std::size_t kMuSig2SessionSerializedLen = 98;
143constexpr std::size_t kMuSig2SessionCountOffset = kMuSig2SessionSerializedLen;
144constexpr std::size_t kMuSig2SessionCountLen = 4;
145constexpr uint32_t kMuSig2MinParticipants = 2;
146constexpr uint32_t kMuSig2MaxKeyAggParticipants =
147 static_cast<uint32_t>((UFSECP_MUSIG2_KEYAGG_LEN - kMuSig2KeyAggHeaderLen) / kMuSig2KeyAggCoeffLen);
148
149static_assert(kMuSig2MaxKeyAggParticipants >= kMuSig2MinParticipants,
150 "MuSig2 keyagg blob must encode at least two participants");
151static_assert(kMuSig2SessionCountOffset + kMuSig2SessionCountLen <= UFSECP_MUSIG2_SESSION_LEN,
152 "MuSig2 session blob must have room for participant count metadata");
153
154static ufsecp_error_t parse_musig2_keyagg(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
155 const uint8_t keyagg[UFSECP_MUSIG2_KEYAGG_LEN],
156 secp256k1::MuSig2KeyAggCtx& out) {
157 uint32_t nk = 0;
158 std::memcpy(&nk, keyagg, 4);
159 if (nk < kMuSig2MinParticipants || nk > kMuSig2MaxKeyAggParticipants) {
160 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid keyagg participant count");
161 }
162
163 out.Q_negated = (keyagg[4] != 0);
164 out.Q = point_from_compressed(keyagg + 5);
165 if (out.Q.is_infinity()) {
166 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "invalid aggregated key");
167 }
168
169 auto qc = out.Q.to_compressed();
170 std::memcpy(out.Q_x.data(), qc.data() + 1, 32);
171 out.key_coefficients.clear();
172 out.key_coefficients.reserve(nk);
173 for (uint32_t i = 0; i < nk; ++i) {
174 Scalar coefficient;
175 if (!scalar_parse_strict(keyagg + kMuSig2KeyAggHeaderLen + static_cast<std::size_t>(i) * kMuSig2KeyAggCoeffLen,
176 coefficient)) {
177 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid key coefficient in keyagg");
178 }
179 out.key_coefficients.push_back(coefficient);
180 }
181 return UFSECP_OK;
182}
183
184static ufsecp_error_t parse_musig2_session(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
185 const uint8_t session[UFSECP_MUSIG2_SESSION_LEN],
186 secp256k1::MuSig2Session& out,
187 uint32_t& participant_count_out) {
188 out.R = point_from_compressed(session);
189 if (out.R.is_infinity()) {
190 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid session R point");
191 }
192 if (!scalar_parse_strict(session + 33, out.b)) {
193 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid session scalar b");
194 }
195 if (!scalar_parse_strict(session + 65, out.e)) {
196 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid session scalar e");
197 }
198 out.R_negated = (session[97] != 0);
199
200 std::memcpy(&participant_count_out, session + kMuSig2SessionCountOffset, sizeof(participant_count_out));
201 if (participant_count_out < kMuSig2MinParticipants || participant_count_out > kMuSig2MaxKeyAggParticipants) {
202 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid session participant count");
203 }
204 return UFSECP_OK;
205}
206
207static bool checked_mul_size(std::size_t left, std::size_t right, std::size_t& out) {
208 if (left != 0 && right > std::numeric_limits<std::size_t>::max() / left) {
209 return false;
210 }
211 out = left * right;
212 return true;
213}
214
215static bool checked_add_size(std::size_t left, std::size_t right, std::size_t& out) {
216 if (right > std::numeric_limits<std::size_t>::max() - left) {
217 return false;
218 }
219 out = left + right;
220 return true;
221}
222
223/* Hard upper bound on user-supplied batch/array counts.
224 1 << 20 (~1M) is generous for any legitimate use case and prevents
225 hostile callers from triggering multi-GB allocations. */
226static constexpr std::size_t kMaxBatchN = std::size_t{1} << 20;
227
228} // namespace
229
230/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
231 * Exception-safety macro for extern "C" functions.
232 *
233 * C++ exceptions propagating through an extern "C" boundary are undefined
234 * behaviour. Every function that touches STL containers, std::string, or
235 * any other throwing code must be wrapped.
236 * ---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
237#define UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx_ptr) \
238 catch (const std::bad_alloc&) { \
239 return (ctx_ptr) ? ctx_set_err(ctx_ptr, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, \
240 "allocation failed") \
241 : UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; \
242 } catch (...) { \
243 return (ctx_ptr) ? ctx_set_err(ctx_ptr, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, \
244 "internal error") \
245 : UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; \
246 }
247
248static inline Point point_from_compressed(const uint8_t pub[33]) {
249 // Strict: only accept 0x02/0x03 prefix, reject x >= p
250 if (pub[0] != 0x02 && pub[0] != 0x03) return Point::infinity();
251 FE x;
252 if (!FE::parse_bytes_strict(pub + 1, x)) return Point::infinity();
253
254 /* y^2 = x^3 + 7 */
255 auto x2 = x * x;
256 auto x3 = x2 * x;
257 auto y2 = x3 + FE::from_uint64(7);
258
259 /* sqrt via addition chain for (p+1)/4 */
260 auto t = y2;
261 auto a = t.square() * t;
262 auto b = a.square() * t;
263 auto c = b.square().square().square() * b;
264 auto d = c.square().square().square() * b;
265 auto e = d.square().square() * a;
266 auto f = e;
267 for (int i = 0; i < 11; ++i) f = f.square();
268 f = f * e;
269 auto g = f;
270 for (int i = 0; i < 22; ++i) g = g.square();
271 g = g * f;
272 auto h = g;
273 for (int i = 0; i < 44; ++i) h = h.square();
274 h = h * g;
275 auto j = h;
276 for (int i = 0; i < 88; ++i) j = j.square();
277 j = j * h;
278 auto k = j;
279 for (int i = 0; i < 44; ++i) k = k.square();
280 k = k * g;
281 auto m = k.square().square().square() * b;
282 auto y = m;
283 for (int i = 0; i < 23; ++i) y = y.square();
284 y = y * f;
285 for (int i = 0; i < 6; ++i) y = y.square();
286 y = y * a;
287 y = y.square().square();
288
289 // Verify sqrt: y^2 must equal y2 (reject if x has no valid y on curve)
290 if (y * y != y2) return Point::infinity();
291
292 auto y_bytes = y.to_bytes();
293 bool const y_is_odd = (y_bytes[31] & 1) != 0;
294 bool const want_odd = (pub[0] == 0x03);
295 if (y_is_odd != want_odd) {
296 y = FE::from_uint64(0) - y;
297}
298
299 return Point::from_affine(x, y);
300}
301
302static inline void point_to_compressed(const Point& p, uint8_t out[33]) {
303 auto comp = p.to_compressed();
304 std::memcpy(out, comp.data(), 33);
305}
306
307template <typename T>
308class SecureEraseGuard {
309public:
310 explicit SecureEraseGuard(T* value) noexcept : value_(value) {}
311 SecureEraseGuard(const SecureEraseGuard&) = delete;
312 SecureEraseGuard& operator=(const SecureEraseGuard&) = delete;
313
314 ~SecureEraseGuard() {
315 if (value_ != nullptr) {
316 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(value_, sizeof(T));
317 }
318 }
319
320private:
321 T* value_;
322};
323
324static inline void secure_erase_scalar_vector(std::vector<Scalar>& values) {
325 for (auto& value : values) {
326 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&value, sizeof(value));
327 }
328}
329
330static bool valid_network(int n) {
331 return n == UFSECP_NET_MAINNET || n == UFSECP_NET_TESTNET;
332}
333
334static secp256k1::Network to_network(int n) {
335 return n == UFSECP_NET_TESTNET ? secp256k1::Network::Testnet
336 : secp256k1::Network::Mainnet;
337}
338
339/* ===========================================================================
340 * Version / error (stateless, no ctx needed)
341 * =========================================================================== */
342
343unsigned int ufsecp_version(void) {
344 return UFSECP_VERSION_PACKED;
345}
346
347unsigned int ufsecp_abi_version(void) {
348 return UFSECP_ABI_VERSION;
349}
350
351const char* ufsecp_version_string(void) {
352 return UFSECP_VERSION_STRING;
353}
354
355const char* ufsecp_error_str(ufsecp_error_t err) {
356 switch (err) {
357 case UFSECP_OK: return "OK";
358 case UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG: return "NULL argument";
359 case UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY: return "invalid private key";
360 case UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY: return "invalid public key";
361 case UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG: return "invalid signature";
362 case UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT: return "malformed input";
363 case UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL: return "verification failed";
364 case UFSECP_ERR_ARITH: return "arithmetic error";
365 case UFSECP_ERR_SELFTEST: return "self-test failed";
366 case UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL: return "internal error";
367 case UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL: return "buffer too small";
368 default: return "unknown error";
369 }
370}
371
372/* ===========================================================================
373 * Context lifecycle
374 * =========================================================================== */
375
376ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ctx_create(ufsecp_ctx** ctx_out) {
377 if (!ctx_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
378 *ctx_out = nullptr;
379
380 auto* ctx = static_cast<ufsecp_ctx*>(std::calloc(1, sizeof(ufsecp_ctx)));
381 if (!ctx) return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL;
382
383 ctx->last_err = UFSECP_OK;
384 ctx->last_msg[0] = '\0';
385
386 /* Run selftest once (cached globally by ensure_library_integrity) */
387 ctx->selftest_ok = secp256k1::fast::ensure_library_integrity(false);
388 if (!ctx->selftest_ok) {
389 std::free(ctx);
390 return UFSECP_ERR_SELFTEST;
391 }
392
393 *ctx_out = ctx;
394 return UFSECP_OK;
395}
396
397ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ctx_clone(const ufsecp_ctx* src, ufsecp_ctx** ctx_out) {
398 if (!src || !ctx_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
399 *ctx_out = nullptr;
400
401 auto* dst = static_cast<ufsecp_ctx*>(std::malloc(sizeof(ufsecp_ctx)));
402 if (!dst) return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL;
403
404 std::memcpy(dst, src, sizeof(ufsecp_ctx));
405 ctx_clear_err(dst);
406
407 *ctx_out = dst;
408 return UFSECP_OK;
409}
410
411void ufsecp_ctx_destroy(ufsecp_ctx* ctx) {
412 std::free(ctx); // free(NULL) is a no-op per C standard
413}
414
415ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_last_error(const ufsecp_ctx* ctx) {
416 return ctx ? ctx->last_err : UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
417}
418
419const char* ufsecp_last_error_msg(const ufsecp_ctx* ctx) {
420 if (!ctx) return "NULL context";
421 return ctx->last_msg[0] ? ctx->last_msg : ufsecp_error_str(ctx->last_err);
422}
423
424size_t ufsecp_ctx_size(void) {
425 return sizeof(ufsecp_ctx);
426}
427
428/* ===========================================================================
429 * Private key utilities
430 * =========================================================================== */
431
432ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_seckey_verify(const ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
433 const uint8_t privkey[32]) {
434 if (!ctx || !privkey) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
435 // BIP-340 strict: reject if privkey == 0 or privkey >= n (no reduction)
436 Scalar sk;
437 if (!Scalar::parse_bytes_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
438 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY;
439 }
440 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
441 return UFSECP_OK;
442}
443
444ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_seckey_negate(ufsecp_ctx* ctx, uint8_t privkey[32]) {
445 if (!ctx || !privkey) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
446 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
447 Scalar sk;
448 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
449 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
450 }
451 auto neg = sk.negate();
452 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
453 // negate of valid nonzero scalar is always nonzero
454 scalar_to_bytes(neg, privkey);
455 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&neg, sizeof(neg));
456 return UFSECP_OK;
457}
458
459ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_seckey_tweak_add(ufsecp_ctx* ctx, uint8_t privkey[32],
460 const uint8_t tweak[32]) {
461 if (!ctx || !privkey || !tweak) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
462 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
463 Scalar sk;
464 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
465 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
466 }
467 Scalar tw;
468 if (!scalar_parse_strict(tweak, tw)) {
469 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "tweak >= n");
470 }
471 auto result = sk + tw;
472 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
473 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&tw, sizeof(tw));
474 if (result.is_zero()) {
475 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&result, sizeof(result));
476 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "tweak_add resulted in zero");
477 }
478 scalar_to_bytes(result, privkey);
479 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&result, sizeof(result));
480 return UFSECP_OK;
481}
482
483ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_seckey_tweak_mul(ufsecp_ctx* ctx, uint8_t privkey[32],
484 const uint8_t tweak[32]) {
485 if (!ctx || !privkey || !tweak) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
486 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
487 Scalar sk;
488 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
489 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
490 }
491 Scalar tw;
492 // tweak_mul: reject tweak==0 (result would be zero) and tweak >= n
493 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(tweak, tw)) {
494 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "tweak is zero or >= n");
495 }
496 auto result = sk * tw;
497 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
498 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&tw, sizeof(tw));
499 if (result.is_zero()) {
500 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&result, sizeof(result));
501 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "tweak_mul resulted in zero");
502 }
503 scalar_to_bytes(result, privkey);
504 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&result, sizeof(result));
505 return UFSECP_OK;
506}
507
508/* ===========================================================================
509 * Public key
510 * =========================================================================== */
511
512static ufsecp_error_t pubkey_create_core(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
513 const uint8_t privkey[32],
514 Point& pk_out) {
515 Scalar sk;
516 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
517 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
518 }
519 pk_out = secp256k1::ct::generator_mul(sk);
520 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
521 if (pk_out.is_infinity()) {
522 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "pubkey at infinity");
523 }
524 return UFSECP_OK;
525}
526
527ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pubkey_create(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
528 const uint8_t privkey[32],
529 uint8_t pubkey33_out[33]) {
530 if (!ctx || !privkey || !pubkey33_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
531 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
532 Point pk;
533 const ufsecp_error_t err = pubkey_create_core(ctx, privkey, pk);
534 if (err != UFSECP_OK) return err;
535 point_to_compressed(pk, pubkey33_out);
536 return UFSECP_OK;
537}
538
539ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pubkey_create_uncompressed(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
540 const uint8_t privkey[32],
541 uint8_t pubkey65_out[65]) {
542 if (!ctx || !privkey || !pubkey65_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
543 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
544 Point pk;
545 const ufsecp_error_t err = pubkey_create_core(ctx, privkey, pk);
546 if (err != UFSECP_OK) return err;
547 auto uncomp = pk.to_uncompressed();
548 std::memcpy(pubkey65_out, uncomp.data(), 65);
549 return UFSECP_OK;
550}
551
552ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pubkey_parse(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
553 const uint8_t* input, size_t input_len,
554 uint8_t pubkey33_out[33]) {
555 if (!ctx || !input || !pubkey33_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
556 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
557
558 if (input_len == 33 && (input[0] == 0x02 || input[0] == 0x03)) {
559 auto p = point_from_compressed(input);
560 if (p.is_infinity()) {
561 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "decompression failed");
562}
563 point_to_compressed(p, pubkey33_out);
564 return UFSECP_OK;
565 }
566 if (input_len == 65 && input[0] == 0x04) {
567 std::array<uint8_t, 32> x_bytes, y_bytes;
568 std::memcpy(x_bytes.data(), input + 1, 32);
569 std::memcpy(y_bytes.data(), input + 33, 32);
570 // Strict: reject x >= p or y >= p
571 FE x, y;
572 if (!FE::parse_bytes_strict(x_bytes, x) ||
573 !FE::parse_bytes_strict(y_bytes, y)) {
574 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "coordinate >= p");
575 }
576 // On-curve check: y^2 == x^3 + 7
577 auto lhs = y * y;
578 auto rhs = x * x * x + FE::from_uint64(7);
579 if (lhs != rhs) {
580 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "point not on curve");
581 }
582 auto p = Point::from_affine(x, y);
583 if (p.is_infinity()) {
584 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "point at infinity");
585}
586 point_to_compressed(p, pubkey33_out);
587 return UFSECP_OK;
588 }
589 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "expected 33 or 65 byte pubkey");
590}
591
592ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pubkey_xonly(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
593 const uint8_t privkey[32],
594 uint8_t xonly32_out[32]) {
595 if (!ctx || !privkey || !xonly32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
596 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
597
598 Scalar sk;
599 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
600 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
601 }
602
603 auto xonly = secp256k1::schnorr_pubkey(sk);
604 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
605 std::memcpy(xonly32_out, xonly.data(), 32);
606 return UFSECP_OK;
607}
608
609/* ===========================================================================
610 * ECDSA
611 * =========================================================================== */
612
613ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_sign(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
614 const uint8_t msg32[32],
615 const uint8_t privkey[32],
616 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
617 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !privkey || !sig64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
618 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
619
620 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg;
621 std::memcpy(msg.data(), msg32, 32);
622 Scalar sk;
623 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
624 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
625 }
626
627 auto sig = secp256k1::ct::ecdsa_sign(msg, sk);
628 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
629 // CT path returns already-normalized (low-S) signature
630 auto compact = sig.to_compact();
631 std::memcpy(sig64_out, compact.data(), 64);
632 return UFSECP_OK;
633}
634
635ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_sign_verified(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
636 const uint8_t msg32[32],
637 const uint8_t privkey[32],
638 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
639 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !privkey || !sig64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
640 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
641
642 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg;
643 std::memcpy(msg.data(), msg32, 32);
644 Scalar sk;
645 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
646 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
647 }
648
649 auto sig = secp256k1::ct::ecdsa_sign_verified(msg, sk);
650 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
651 auto compact = sig.to_compact();
652 std::memcpy(sig64_out, compact.data(), 64);
653 return UFSECP_OK;
654}
655
656ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_verify(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
657 const uint8_t msg32[32],
658 const uint8_t sig64[64],
659 const uint8_t pubkey33[33]) {
660 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !sig64 || !pubkey33) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
661 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
662
663 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg;
664 std::memcpy(msg.data(), msg32, 32);
665 std::array<uint8_t, 64> compact;
666 std::memcpy(compact.data(), sig64, 64);
667
668 secp256k1::ECDSASignature ecdsasig;
669 if (!secp256k1::ECDSASignature::parse_compact_strict(compact, ecdsasig)) {
670 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "non-canonical compact sig");
671 }
672 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
673 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
674 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid public key");
675 }
676
677 if (!secp256k1::ecdsa_verify(msg, pk, ecdsasig)) {
678 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "ECDSA verify failed");
679 }
680
681 return UFSECP_OK;
682}
683
684ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_sig_to_der(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
685 const uint8_t sig64[64],
686 uint8_t* der_out, size_t* der_len) {
687 if (!ctx || !sig64 || !der_out || !der_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
688 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
689
690 std::array<uint8_t, 64> compact;
691 std::memcpy(compact.data(), sig64, 64);
692
693 secp256k1::ECDSASignature ecdsasig;
694 if (!secp256k1::ECDSASignature::parse_compact_strict(compact, ecdsasig)) {
695 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "non-canonical compact sig");
696 }
697
698 auto [der, actual_len] = ecdsasig.to_der();
699 if (*der_len < actual_len) {
700 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "DER buffer too small");
701}
702
703 std::memcpy(der_out, der.data(), actual_len);
704 *der_len = actual_len;
705 return UFSECP_OK;
706}
707
708ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_sig_from_der(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
709 const uint8_t* der, size_t der_len,
710 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
711 if (!ctx || !der || !sig64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
712 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
713
714 /* Strict DER parser for ECDSA secp256k1 signatures.
715 * Format: 0x30 <total_len> 0x02 <r_len> <r_bytes...> 0x02 <s_len> <s_bytes...>
716 *
717 * Enforces:
718 * - Single-byte length encoding only (no long form)
719 * - No negative integers (high bit of first data byte must be 0)
720 * - No unnecessary leading zero padding
721 * - Exact total length (no trailing bytes)
722 * - r, s must be in [1, n-1] (canonical, nonzero)
723 * - Max total DER length: 72 bytes */
724
725 /* Max DER ECDSA sig: 2 + 2 + 33 + 2 + 33 = 72 */
726 if (der_len < 8 || der_len > 72 || der[0] != 0x30) {
727 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: missing/oversized SEQUENCE");
728 }
729
730 /* Reject long-form length encoding (bit 7 set = multi-byte length) */
731 if (der[1] & 0x80) {
732 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: long-form length");
733 }
734
735 size_t const seq_len = der[1];
736 if (seq_len + 2 != der_len) {
737 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: length mismatch");
738 }
739
740 size_t pos = 2;
741
742 /* --- Helper lambda: parse one INTEGER component strictly --- */
743 auto parse_int = [&](const char* name, const uint8_t*& out_ptr,
744 size_t& out_len) -> ufsecp_error_t {
745 if (pos >= der_len || der[pos] != 0x02) {
746 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: missing INTEGER");
747 }
748 pos++;
749 if (pos >= der_len) {
750 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: truncated");
751 }
752 /* Reject long-form length for component */
753 if (der[pos] & 0x80) {
754 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: long-form int length");
755 }
756 size_t const int_len = der[pos++];
757 if (int_len == 0 || pos + int_len > der_len) {
758 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: int length out of bounds");
759 }
760 /* Reject negative: high bit set on first data byte means negative in DER */
761 if (der[pos] & 0x80) {
762 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: negative integer");
763 }
764 /* Reject unnecessary leading zero: 0x00 prefix only valid when next byte
765 * has high bit set (positive number needs padding to stay positive).
766 * If next byte has high bit clear, the 0x00 is superfluous padding. */
767 if (int_len > 1 && der[pos] == 0x00 && !(der[pos + 1] & 0x80)) {
768 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: unnecessary leading zero");
769 }
770
771 out_ptr = der + pos;
772 out_len = int_len;
773 /* Strip valid leading zero pad (high bit of next byte is set) */
774 if (out_len > 0 && out_ptr[0] == 0x00) { out_ptr++; out_len--; }
775 pos += int_len;
776 (void)name;
777 return UFSECP_OK;
778 };
779
780 /* Read R */
781 const uint8_t* r_ptr = nullptr;
782 size_t r_data_len = 0;
783 {
784 auto rc = parse_int("R", r_ptr, r_data_len);
785 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) return rc;
786 }
787
788 /* Read S */
789 const uint8_t* s_ptr = nullptr;
790 size_t s_data_len = 0;
791 {
792 auto rc = parse_int("S", s_ptr, s_data_len);
793 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) return rc;
794 }
795
796 /* Reject trailing bytes after S (must consume entire SEQUENCE) */
797 if (pos != der_len) {
798 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: trailing bytes");
799 }
800
801 if (r_data_len > 32 || s_data_len > 32) {
802 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: component > 32 bytes");
803 }
804
805 /* Build compact sig64 (big-endian, right-aligned in 32-byte slots) */
806 std::memset(sig64_out, 0, 64);
807 /* Explicit null checks for static analyzer (r_ptr/s_ptr guaranteed non-null
808 * when *_data_len > 0 by parse_int() success, but SonarCloud can't track it) */
809 if (r_data_len > 0 && r_ptr) {
810 std::memcpy(sig64_out + (32 - r_data_len), r_ptr, r_data_len);
811 }
812 if (s_data_len > 0 && s_ptr) {
813 std::memcpy(sig64_out + 32 + (32 - s_data_len), s_ptr, s_data_len);
814 }
815
816 /* Range check: r and s must be in [1, n-1] (strict nonzero, no reduce) */
817 Scalar r_sc, s_sc;
818 if (!Scalar::parse_bytes_strict_nonzero(sig64_out, r_sc) ||
819 !Scalar::parse_bytes_strict_nonzero(sig64_out + 32, s_sc)) {
820 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "bad DER: r or s out of range [1,n-1]");
821 }
822
823 return UFSECP_OK;
824}
825
826/* -- ECDSA Recovery -------------------------------------------------------- */
827
828ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_sign_recoverable(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
829 const uint8_t msg32[32],
830 const uint8_t privkey[32],
831 uint8_t sig64_out[64],
832 int* recid_out) {
833 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !privkey || !sig64_out || !recid_out) {
834 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
835}
836 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
837
838 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg;
839 std::memcpy(msg.data(), msg32, 32);
840 Scalar sk;
841 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
842 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
843 }
844
845 // ARCH DECISION: No ct::ecdsa_sign_recoverable exists because recovery signing is
846 // inherently non-constant-time — the recid value (0..3) depends on the R point's x
847 // coordinate, leaking timing. We use the FAST path (secp256k1::ecdsa_sign_recoverable)
848 // with explicit zeroization of the private-key scalar immediately after use.
849 // If a future CT recovery path is needed, it must accept a fixed recid hint from the
850 // caller and branch only on public data.
851 auto rsig = secp256k1::ecdsa_sign_recoverable(msg, sk);
852 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
853 auto normalized = rsig.sig.normalize();
854 auto compact = normalized.to_compact();
855 std::memcpy(sig64_out, compact.data(), 64);
856 *recid_out = rsig.recid;
857 return UFSECP_OK;
858}
859
860ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_recover(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
861 const uint8_t msg32[32],
862 const uint8_t sig64[64],
863 int recid,
864 uint8_t pubkey33_out[33]) {
865 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !sig64 || !pubkey33_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
866 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
867
868 if (recid < 0 || recid > 3) {
869 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "recid must be 0..3");
870 }
871
872 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg;
873 std::memcpy(msg.data(), msg32, 32);
874 std::array<uint8_t, 64> compact;
875 std::memcpy(compact.data(), sig64, 64);
876
877 secp256k1::ECDSASignature ecdsasig;
878 if (!secp256k1::ECDSASignature::parse_compact_strict(compact, ecdsasig)) {
879 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "non-canonical compact sig");
880 }
881
882 auto [point, ok] = secp256k1::ecdsa_recover(msg, ecdsasig, recid);
883 if (!ok) {
884 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "recovery failed");
885 }
886
887 point_to_compressed(point, pubkey33_out);
888 return UFSECP_OK;
889}
890
891/* ===========================================================================
892 * Schnorr (BIP-340)
893 * =========================================================================== */
894
895ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_sign(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
896 const uint8_t msg32[32],
897 const uint8_t privkey[32],
898 const uint8_t aux_rand[32],
899 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
900 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !privkey || !aux_rand || !sig64_out) {
901 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
902}
903 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
904
905 Scalar sk;
906 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
907 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
908 }
909
910 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr, aux_arr;
911 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
912 std::memcpy(aux_arr.data(), aux_rand, 32);
913
914 auto kp = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_keypair_create(sk);
915 auto sig = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_sign(kp, msg_arr, aux_arr);
916 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
917 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&kp.d, sizeof(kp.d));
918 auto bytes = sig.to_bytes();
919 std::memcpy(sig64_out, bytes.data(), 64);
920 return UFSECP_OK;
921}
922
923ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_sign_verified(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
924 const uint8_t msg32[32],
925 const uint8_t privkey[32],
926 const uint8_t aux_rand[32],
927 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
928 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !privkey || !aux_rand || !sig64_out) {
929 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
930 }
931 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
932
933 Scalar sk;
934 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
935 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
936 }
937
938 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr, aux_arr;
939 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
940 std::memcpy(aux_arr.data(), aux_rand, 32);
941
942 auto kp = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_keypair_create(sk);
943 auto sig = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_sign_verified(kp, msg_arr, aux_arr);
944 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
945 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&kp.d, sizeof(kp.d));
946 auto bytes = sig.to_bytes();
947 std::memcpy(sig64_out, bytes.data(), 64);
948 return UFSECP_OK;
949}
950
951ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_sign_batch(
952 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
953 size_t count,
954 const uint8_t* msgs32,
955 const uint8_t* privkeys32,
956 uint8_t* sigs64_out)
957{
958 if (!ctx || !msgs32 || !privkeys32 || !sigs64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
959 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
960 if (count > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch count too large");
961 std::size_t total_msg_bytes, total_sig_bytes;
962 if (!checked_mul_size(count, std::size_t{32}, total_msg_bytes)
963 || !checked_mul_size(count, std::size_t{64}, total_sig_bytes))
964 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch size overflow");
965 for (size_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
966 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg;
967 std::memcpy(msg.data(), msgs32 + i * 32, 32);
968 Scalar sk;
969 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkeys32 + i * 32, sk)) {
970 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
971 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY,
972 "privkey[i] is zero or >= n");
973 }
974 auto sig = secp256k1::ct::ecdsa_sign(msg, sk);
975 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
976 auto compact = sig.to_compact();
977 std::memcpy(sigs64_out + i * 64, compact.data(), 64);
978 }
979 return UFSECP_OK;
980}
981
982ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_sign_batch(
983 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
984 size_t count,
985 const uint8_t* msgs32,
986 const uint8_t* privkeys32,
987 const uint8_t* aux_rands32,
988 uint8_t* sigs64_out)
989{
990 if (!ctx || !msgs32 || !privkeys32 || !sigs64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
991 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
992 if (count > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch count too large");
993 std::size_t total_msg_bytes, total_sig_bytes;
994 if (!checked_mul_size(count, std::size_t{32}, total_msg_bytes)
995 || !checked_mul_size(count, std::size_t{64}, total_sig_bytes))
996 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch size overflow");
997
998 static constexpr uint8_t kZeroAux[32] = {};
999
1000 for (size_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
1001 Scalar sk;
1002 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkeys32 + i * 32, sk)) {
1003 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1004 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY,
1005 "privkey[i] is zero or >= n");
1006 }
1007
1008 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr, aux_arr;
1009 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msgs32 + i * 32, 32);
1010 const uint8_t* aux_src = aux_rands32 ? aux_rands32 + i * 32 : kZeroAux;
1011 std::memcpy(aux_arr.data(), aux_src, 32);
1012
1013 auto kp = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_keypair_create(sk);
1014 auto sig = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_sign(kp, msg_arr, aux_arr);
1015 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1016 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&kp.d, sizeof(kp.d));
1017
1018 auto sig_bytes = sig.to_bytes();
1019 std::memcpy(sigs64_out + i * 64, sig_bytes.data(), 64);
1020 }
1021 return UFSECP_OK;
1022}
1023
1024ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_verify(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1025 const uint8_t msg32[32],
1026 const uint8_t sig64[64],
1027 const uint8_t pubkey_x[32]) {
1028 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !sig64 || !pubkey_x) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1029 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1030
1031 // BIP-340 strict parse: reject non-canonical r >= p, s >= n, or s == 0
1032 secp256k1::SchnorrSignature schnorr_sig;
1033 if (!secp256k1::SchnorrSignature::parse_strict(sig64, schnorr_sig)) {
1034 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "Non-canonical Schnorr sig (r>=p or s>=n)");
1035 }
1036
1037 // BIP-340 strict: reject pubkey x >= p
1038 FE pk_fe;
1039 if (!FE::parse_bytes_strict(pubkey_x, pk_fe)) {
1040 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "Non-canonical pubkey (x>=p)");
1041 }
1042
1043 std::array<uint8_t, 32> pk_arr, msg_arr;
1044 std::memcpy(pk_arr.data(), pubkey_x, 32);
1045 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
1046
1047 if (!secp256k1::schnorr_verify(pk_arr, msg_arr, schnorr_sig)) {
1048 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "Schnorr verify failed");
1049}
1050
1051 return UFSECP_OK;
1052}
1053
1054/* ===========================================================================
1055 * ECDH
1056 * =========================================================================== */
1057
1058static ufsecp_error_t ecdh_parse_args(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1059 const uint8_t privkey[32],
1060 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
1061 Scalar& sk, Point& pk) {
1062 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
1063 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
1064 }
1065 pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
1066 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
1067 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1068 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid or infinity pubkey");
1069 }
1070 return UFSECP_OK;
1071}
1072
1073ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdh(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1074 const uint8_t privkey[32],
1075 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
1076 uint8_t secret32_out[32]) {
1077 if (!ctx || !privkey || !pubkey33 || !secret32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1078 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1079 Scalar sk; Point pk;
1080 const ufsecp_error_t err = ecdh_parse_args(ctx, privkey, pubkey33, sk, pk);
1081 if (err != UFSECP_OK) return err;
1082 auto secret = secp256k1::ecdh_compute(sk, pk);
1083 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1084 std::memcpy(secret32_out, secret.data(), 32);
1085 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(secret.data(), 32);
1086 return UFSECP_OK;
1087}
1088
1089ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdh_xonly(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1090 const uint8_t privkey[32],
1091 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
1092 uint8_t secret32_out[32]) {
1093 if (!ctx || !privkey || !pubkey33 || !secret32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1094 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1095 Scalar sk; Point pk;
1096 const ufsecp_error_t err = ecdh_parse_args(ctx, privkey, pubkey33, sk, pk);
1097 if (err != UFSECP_OK) return err;
1098 auto secret = secp256k1::ecdh_compute_xonly(sk, pk);
1099 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1100 std::memcpy(secret32_out, secret.data(), 32);
1101 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(secret.data(), 32);
1102 return UFSECP_OK;
1103}
1104
1105ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdh_raw(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1106 const uint8_t privkey[32],
1107 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
1108 uint8_t secret32_out[32]) {
1109 if (!ctx || !privkey || !pubkey33 || !secret32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1110 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1111 Scalar sk; Point pk;
1112 const ufsecp_error_t err = ecdh_parse_args(ctx, privkey, pubkey33, sk, pk);
1113 if (err != UFSECP_OK) return err;
1114 auto secret = secp256k1::ecdh_compute_raw(sk, pk);
1115 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1116 std::memcpy(secret32_out, secret.data(), 32);
1117 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(secret.data(), 32);
1118 return UFSECP_OK;
1119}
1120
1121/* ===========================================================================
1122 * Hashing (stateless -- no ctx required, but returns error_t for consistency)
1123 * =========================================================================== */
1124
1125ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_sha256(const uint8_t* data, size_t len,
1126 uint8_t digest32_out[32]) {
1127 if (!data || !digest32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1128 secp256k1::SHA256 hasher;
1129 hasher.update(data, len);
1130 auto digest = hasher.finalize();
1131 std::memcpy(digest32_out, digest.data(), 32);
1132 return UFSECP_OK;
1133}
1134
1135ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_hash160(const uint8_t* data, size_t len,
1136 uint8_t digest20_out[20]) {
1137 if (!data || !digest20_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1138 auto h = secp256k1::hash160(data, len);
1139 std::memcpy(digest20_out, h.data(), 20);
1140 return UFSECP_OK;
1141}
1142
1143ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_tagged_hash(const char* tag,
1144 const uint8_t* data, size_t len,
1145 uint8_t digest32_out[32]) {
1146 if (!tag || !data || !digest32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1147 auto h = secp256k1::tagged_hash(tag, data, len);
1148 std::memcpy(digest32_out, h.data(), 32);
1149 return UFSECP_OK;
1150}
1151
1152/* ===========================================================================
1153 * Bitcoin addresses
1154 * =========================================================================== */
1155
1156ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_addr_p2pkh(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1157 const uint8_t pubkey33[33], int network,
1158 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
1159 if (!ctx || !pubkey33 || !addr_out || !addr_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1160 if (!valid_network(network)) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid network");
1161 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1162
1163 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
1164 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
1165 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
1166 }
1167 try {
1168 auto addr = secp256k1::address_p2pkh(pk, to_network(network));
1169 if (addr.empty()) {
1170 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "P2PKH generation failed");
1171}
1172 if (*addr_len < addr.size() + 1) {
1173 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "P2PKH buffer too small");
1174}
1175 std::memcpy(addr_out, addr.c_str(), addr.size() + 1);
1176 *addr_len = addr.size();
1177 return UFSECP_OK;
1178 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
1179}
1180
1181ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_addr_p2wpkh(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1182 const uint8_t pubkey33[33], int network,
1183 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
1184 if (!ctx || !pubkey33 || !addr_out || !addr_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1185 if (!valid_network(network)) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid network");
1186 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1187
1188 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
1189 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
1190 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
1191 }
1192 try {
1193 auto addr = secp256k1::address_p2wpkh(pk, to_network(network));
1194 if (addr.empty()) {
1195 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "P2WPKH generation failed");
1196}
1197 if (*addr_len < addr.size() + 1) {
1198 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "P2WPKH buffer too small");
1199}
1200 std::memcpy(addr_out, addr.c_str(), addr.size() + 1);
1201 *addr_len = addr.size();
1202 return UFSECP_OK;
1203 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
1204}
1205
1206ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_addr_p2tr(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1207 const uint8_t internal_key_x[32], int network,
1208 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
1209 if (!ctx || !internal_key_x || !addr_out || !addr_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1210 if (!valid_network(network)) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid network");
1211 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1212
1213 // Reject all-zero x-only key (not a valid curve point)
1214 {
1215 uint8_t acc = 0;
1216 for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i) acc |= internal_key_x[i];
1217 if (acc == 0) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "zero x-only key");
1218 }
1219
1220 std::array<uint8_t, 32> key_x;
1221 std::memcpy(key_x.data(), internal_key_x, 32);
1222 try {
1223 auto addr = secp256k1::address_p2tr_raw(key_x, to_network(network));
1224 if (addr.empty()) {
1225 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "P2TR generation failed");
1226}
1227 if (*addr_len < addr.size() + 1) {
1228 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "P2TR buffer too small");
1229}
1230 std::memcpy(addr_out, addr.c_str(), addr.size() + 1);
1231 *addr_len = addr.size();
1232 return UFSECP_OK;
1233 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
1234}
1235
1236ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_addr_p2sh(
1237 const uint8_t* redeem_script, size_t redeem_script_len,
1238 int network,
1239 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
1240 if (!redeem_script && redeem_script_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1241 if (!addr_out || !addr_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1242 if (!valid_network(network)) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1243
1244 try {
1245 // hash160 of redeem_script
1246 auto script_hash = secp256k1::hash160(redeem_script, redeem_script_len);
1247 auto addr = secp256k1::address_p2sh(script_hash, to_network(network));
1248 if (addr.empty()) return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL;
1249 if (*addr_len < addr.size() + 1) return UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL;
1250 std::memcpy(addr_out, addr.c_str(), addr.size() + 1);
1251 *addr_len = addr.size();
1252 return UFSECP_OK;
1253 } catch (...) { return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; }
1254}
1255
1256ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_addr_p2sh_p2wpkh(
1257 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1258 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
1259 int network,
1260 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
1261 if (!ctx || !pubkey33 || !addr_out || !addr_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1262 if (!valid_network(network)) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid network");
1263 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1264
1265 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
1266 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
1267 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
1268 }
1269 try {
1270 auto addr = secp256k1::address_p2sh_p2wpkh(pk, to_network(network));
1271 if (addr.empty()) {
1272 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "P2SH-P2WPKH generation failed");
1273 }
1274 if (*addr_len < addr.size() + 1) {
1275 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "P2SH-P2WPKH buffer too small");
1276 }
1277 std::memcpy(addr_out, addr.c_str(), addr.size() + 1);
1278 *addr_len = addr.size();
1279 return UFSECP_OK;
1280 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
1281}
1282
1283/* ===========================================================================
1284 * WIF
1285 * =========================================================================== */
1286
1287ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_wif_encode(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1288 const uint8_t privkey[32],
1289 int compressed, int network,
1290 char* wif_out, size_t* wif_len) {
1291 if (!ctx || !privkey || !wif_out || !wif_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1292 if (!valid_network(network)) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid network");
1293 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1294
1295 Scalar sk;
1296 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
1297 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
1298 }
1299 try {
1300 auto wif = secp256k1::wif_encode(sk, compressed != 0, to_network(network));
1301 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1302 if (wif.empty()) {
1303 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "WIF encode failed");
1304}
1305 if (*wif_len < wif.size() + 1) {
1306 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "WIF buffer too small");
1307}
1308 std::memcpy(wif_out, wif.c_str(), wif.size() + 1);
1309 *wif_len = wif.size();
1310 return UFSECP_OK;
1311 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
1312}
1313
1314ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_wif_decode(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1315 const char* wif,
1316 uint8_t privkey32_out[32],
1317 int* compressed_out,
1318 int* network_out) {
1319 if (!ctx || !wif || !privkey32_out || !compressed_out || !network_out) {
1320 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1321}
1322 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1323
1324 try {
1325 auto result = secp256k1::wif_decode(std::string(wif));
1326 if (!result.valid) {
1327 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid WIF string");
1328}
1329
1330 scalar_to_bytes(result.key, privkey32_out);
1331 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&result.key, sizeof(result.key));
1332 *compressed_out = result.compressed ? 1 : 0;
1333 *network_out = result.network == secp256k1::Network::Testnet
1334 ? UFSECP_NET_TESTNET : UFSECP_NET_MAINNET;
1335 return UFSECP_OK;
1336 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
1337}
1338
1339/* ===========================================================================
1340 * BIP-32
1341 * =========================================================================== */
1342
1343static void extkey_to_uf(const secp256k1::ExtendedKey& ek, ufsecp_bip32_key* out) {
1344 auto serialized = ek.serialize();
1345 std::memcpy(out->data, serialized.data(), 78);
1346 out->is_private = ek.is_private ? 1 : 0;
1347 std::memset(out->_pad, 0, sizeof(out->_pad));
1348}
1349
1350static secp256k1::ExtendedKey extkey_from_uf(const ufsecp_bip32_key* k) {
1351 secp256k1::ExtendedKey ek{};
1352 ek.depth = k->data[4];
1353 std::memcpy(ek.parent_fingerprint.data(), k->data + 5, 4);
1354 ek.child_number = (uint32_t(k->data[9]) << 24) | (uint32_t(k->data[10]) << 16) |
1355 (uint32_t(k->data[11]) << 8) | uint32_t(k->data[12]);
1356 std::memcpy(ek.chain_code.data(), k->data + 13, 32);
1357 std::memcpy(ek.key.data(), k->data + 46, 32);
1358 if (k->is_private) {
1359 ek.is_private = true;
1360 } else {
1361 ek.is_private = false;
1362 ek.pub_prefix = k->data[45];
1363 }
1364 return ek;
1365}
1366
1367static ufsecp_error_t parse_bip32_key(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1368 const ufsecp_bip32_key* key,
1369 secp256k1::ExtendedKey& out) {
1370 if (key->is_private > 1) {
1371 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid BIP-32 key kind");
1372 }
1373 if (key->_pad[0] != 0 || key->_pad[1] != 0 || key->_pad[2] != 0) {
1374 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid BIP-32 reserved bytes");
1375 }
1376
1377 const uint32_t version = (uint32_t(key->data[0]) << 24) |
1378 (uint32_t(key->data[1]) << 16) |
1379 (uint32_t(key->data[2]) << 8) |
1380 uint32_t(key->data[3]);
1381 const uint32_t expected_version = key->is_private ? 0x0488ADE4u : 0x0488B21Eu;
1382 if (version != expected_version) {
1383 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid BIP-32 version");
1384 }
1385
1386 if (key->is_private != 0) {
1387 if (key->data[45] != 0x00) {
1388 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid BIP-32 private marker");
1389 }
1390 Scalar sk;
1391 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(key->data + 46, sk)) {
1392 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "invalid BIP-32 private key");
1393 }
1394 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1395 } else {
1396 if (key->data[45] != 0x02 && key->data[45] != 0x03) {
1397 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid BIP-32 public key prefix");
1398 }
1399 auto pk = point_from_compressed(key->data + 45);
1400 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
1401 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid BIP-32 public key");
1402 }
1403 }
1404
1405 out = extkey_from_uf(key);
1406 return UFSECP_OK;
1407}
1408
1409ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip32_master(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1410 const uint8_t* seed, size_t seed_len,
1411 ufsecp_bip32_key* key_out) {
1412 if (!ctx || !seed || !key_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1413 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1414
1415 if (seed_len < 16 || seed_len > 64) {
1416 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "seed must be 16-64 bytes");
1417}
1418
1419 auto [ek, ok] = secp256k1::bip32_master_key(seed, seed_len);
1420 if (!ok) {
1421 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "BIP-32 master key failed");
1422}
1423
1424 extkey_to_uf(ek, key_out);
1425 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.key.data(), ek.key.size());
1426 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.chain_code.data(), ek.chain_code.size());
1427 return UFSECP_OK;
1428}
1429
1430ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip32_derive(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1431 const ufsecp_bip32_key* parent,
1432 uint32_t index,
1433 ufsecp_bip32_key* child_out) {
1434 if (!ctx || !parent || !child_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1435 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1436
1437 secp256k1::ExtendedKey ek{};
1438 ufsecp_error_t const parse_rc = parse_bip32_key(ctx, parent, ek);
1439 if (parse_rc != UFSECP_OK) {
1440 return parse_rc;
1441 }
1442 auto [child, ok] = ek.derive_child(index);
1443 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.key.data(), ek.key.size());
1444 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.chain_code.data(), ek.chain_code.size());
1445 if (!ok) {
1446 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "BIP-32 derivation failed");
1447}
1448
1449 extkey_to_uf(child, child_out);
1450 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(child.key.data(), child.key.size());
1451 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(child.chain_code.data(), child.chain_code.size());
1452 return UFSECP_OK;
1453}
1454
1455ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip32_derive_path(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1456 const ufsecp_bip32_key* master,
1457 const char* path,
1458 ufsecp_bip32_key* key_out) {
1459 if (!ctx || !master || !path || !key_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1460 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1461
1462 secp256k1::ExtendedKey ek{};
1463 ufsecp_error_t const parse_rc = parse_bip32_key(ctx, master, ek);
1464 if (parse_rc != UFSECP_OK) {
1465 return parse_rc;
1466 }
1467 try {
1468 auto [derived, ok] = secp256k1::bip32_derive_path(ek, std::string(path));
1469 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.key.data(), ek.key.size());
1470 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.chain_code.data(), ek.chain_code.size());
1471 if (!ok) {
1472 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid BIP-32 path");
1473}
1474
1475 extkey_to_uf(derived, key_out);
1476 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(derived.key.data(), derived.key.size());
1477 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(derived.chain_code.data(), derived.chain_code.size());
1478 return UFSECP_OK;
1479 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
1480}
1481
1482ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip32_privkey(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1483 const ufsecp_bip32_key* key,
1484 uint8_t privkey32_out[32]) {
1485 if (!ctx || !key || !privkey32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1486 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1487
1488 if (!key->is_private) {
1489 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "key is public, not private");
1490}
1491
1492 secp256k1::ExtendedKey ek{};
1493 ufsecp_error_t const parse_rc = parse_bip32_key(ctx, key, ek);
1494 if (parse_rc != UFSECP_OK) {
1495 return parse_rc;
1496 }
1497 auto sk = ek.private_key();
1498 scalar_to_bytes(sk, privkey32_out);
1499 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1500 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.key.data(), ek.key.size());
1501 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.chain_code.data(), ek.chain_code.size());
1502 return UFSECP_OK;
1503}
1504
1505ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip32_pubkey(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1506 const ufsecp_bip32_key* key,
1507 uint8_t pubkey33_out[33]) {
1508 if (!ctx || !key || !pubkey33_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1509 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1510
1511 secp256k1::ExtendedKey ek{};
1512 ufsecp_error_t const parse_rc = parse_bip32_key(ctx, key, ek);
1513 if (parse_rc != UFSECP_OK) {
1514 return parse_rc;
1515 }
1516 auto pk = ek.public_key();
1517 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
1518 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.key.data(), ek.key.size());
1519 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.chain_code.data(), ek.chain_code.size());
1520 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid BIP-32 public key");
1521 }
1522 point_to_compressed(pk, pubkey33_out);
1523 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.key.data(), ek.key.size());
1524 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.chain_code.data(), ek.chain_code.size());
1525 return UFSECP_OK;
1526}
1527
1528/* ===========================================================================
1529 * Taproot (BIP-341)
1530 * =========================================================================== */
1531
1532ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_taproot_output_key(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1533 const uint8_t internal_x[32],
1534 const uint8_t* merkle_root,
1535 uint8_t output_x_out[32],
1536 int* parity_out) {
1537 if (!ctx || !internal_x || !output_x_out || !parity_out) {
1538 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1539}
1540 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1541
1542 // Reject all-zero x-only key (not a valid curve point)
1543 {
1544 uint8_t acc = 0;
1545 for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i) acc |= internal_x[i];
1546 if (acc == 0) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "zero x-only key");
1547 }
1548
1549 std::array<uint8_t, 32> ik;
1550 std::memcpy(ik.data(), internal_x, 32);
1551 size_t const mr_len = merkle_root ? 32 : 0;
1552
1553 auto [ok_x, parity] = secp256k1::taproot_output_key(ik, merkle_root, mr_len);
1554 std::memcpy(output_x_out, ok_x.data(), 32);
1555 *parity_out = parity;
1556 return UFSECP_OK;
1557}
1558
1559ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_taproot_tweak_seckey(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1560 const uint8_t privkey[32],
1561 const uint8_t* merkle_root,
1562 uint8_t tweaked32_out[32]) {
1563 if (!ctx || !privkey || !tweaked32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1564 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1565
1566 Scalar sk;
1567 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
1568 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
1569 }
1570 size_t const mr_len = merkle_root ? 32 : 0;
1571
1572 auto tweaked = secp256k1::taproot_tweak_privkey(sk, merkle_root, mr_len);
1573 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
1574 if (tweaked.is_zero()) {
1575 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&tweaked, sizeof(tweaked));
1576 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "taproot tweak resulted in zero");
1577}
1578
1579 scalar_to_bytes(tweaked, tweaked32_out);
1580 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&tweaked, sizeof(tweaked));
1581 return UFSECP_OK;
1582}
1583
1584ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_taproot_verify(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1585 const uint8_t output_x[32], int output_parity,
1586 const uint8_t internal_x[32],
1587 const uint8_t* merkle_root, size_t merkle_root_len) {
1588 if (!ctx || !output_x || !internal_x) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1589 if (!merkle_root && merkle_root_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1590 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1591
1592 std::array<uint8_t, 32> ok_x, ik_x;
1593 std::memcpy(ok_x.data(), output_x, 32);
1594 std::memcpy(ik_x.data(), internal_x, 32);
1595
1596 if (!secp256k1::taproot_verify_commitment(ok_x, output_parity, ik_x,
1597 merkle_root, merkle_root_len)) {
1598 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "taproot commitment invalid");
1599}
1600
1601 return UFSECP_OK;
1602}
1603
1604/* ===========================================================================
1605 * BIP-143: SegWit v0 Sighash
1606 * =========================================================================== */
1607
1608ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip143_sighash(
1609 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1610 uint32_t version,
1611 const uint8_t hash_prevouts[32],
1612 const uint8_t hash_sequence[32],
1613 const uint8_t outpoint_txid[32], uint32_t outpoint_vout,
1614 const uint8_t* script_code, size_t script_code_len,
1615 uint64_t value,
1616 uint32_t sequence,
1617 const uint8_t hash_outputs[32],
1618 uint32_t locktime,
1619 uint32_t sighash_type,
1620 uint8_t sighash_out[32]) {
1621 if (!ctx || !hash_prevouts || !hash_sequence || !outpoint_txid ||
1622 !script_code || !hash_outputs || !sighash_out)
1623 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1624 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1625
1626 secp256k1::Bip143Preimage pre{};
1627 pre.version = version;
1628 std::memcpy(pre.hash_prevouts.data(), hash_prevouts, 32);
1629 std::memcpy(pre.hash_sequence.data(), hash_sequence, 32);
1630 std::memcpy(pre.hash_outputs.data(), hash_outputs, 32);
1631 pre.locktime = locktime;
1632
1633 secp256k1::Outpoint op{};
1634 std::memcpy(op.txid.data(), outpoint_txid, 32);
1635 op.vout = outpoint_vout;
1636
1637 auto h = secp256k1::bip143_sighash(pre, op, script_code, script_code_len,
1638 value, sequence, sighash_type);
1639 std::memcpy(sighash_out, h.data(), 32);
1640 return UFSECP_OK;
1641}
1642
1643ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip143_p2wpkh_script_code(
1644 const uint8_t pubkey_hash[20],
1645 uint8_t script_code_out[25]) {
1646 if (!pubkey_hash || !script_code_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1647
1648 auto sc = secp256k1::bip143_p2wpkh_script_code(pubkey_hash);
1649 std::memcpy(script_code_out, sc.data(), 25);
1650 return UFSECP_OK;
1651}
1652
1653/* ===========================================================================
1654 * BIP-144: Witness Transaction Serialization
1655 * =========================================================================== */
1656
1657// Helper: read Bitcoin CompactSize from buffer; returns 0 on overflow
1658static size_t read_compact_size(const uint8_t* buf, size_t len,
1659 size_t& offset, uint64_t& val) {
1660 if (offset >= len) return 0;
1661 uint8_t first = buf[offset++];
1662 if (first < 0xFD) { val = first; return 1; }
1663 if (first == 0xFD) {
1664 if (offset + 2 > len) return 0;
1665 val = uint64_t(buf[offset]) | (uint64_t(buf[offset+1]) << 8);
1666 offset += 2; return 3;
1667 }
1668 if (first == 0xFE) {
1669 if (offset + 4 > len) return 0;
1670 val = uint64_t(buf[offset]) | (uint64_t(buf[offset+1]) << 8) |
1671 (uint64_t(buf[offset+2]) << 16) | (uint64_t(buf[offset+3]) << 24);
1672 offset += 4; return 5;
1673 }
1674 // 0xFF
1675 if (offset + 8 > len) return 0;
1676 val = 0;
1677 for (int i = 0; i < 8; ++i) val |= uint64_t(buf[offset+i]) << (8*i);
1678 offset += 8; return 9;
1679}
1680
1681// Helper: skip CompactSize-prefixed blob (e.g. scriptSig or scriptPubKey)
1682static bool skip_compact_bytes(const uint8_t* buf, size_t len, size_t& offset) {
1683 uint64_t sz = 0;
1684 if (!read_compact_size(buf, len, offset, sz)) return false;
1685 if (offset + sz > len) return false;
1686 offset += static_cast<size_t>(sz);
1687 return true;
1688}
1689
1690ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip144_txid(
1691 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1692 const uint8_t* raw_tx, size_t raw_tx_len,
1693 uint8_t txid_out[32]) {
1694 if (!ctx || !raw_tx || !txid_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1695 if (raw_tx_len < 10) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1696
1697 // Detect witness flag: version(4) + marker(0x00) + flag(0x01)
1698 bool has_witness = (raw_tx_len > 6 && raw_tx[4] == 0x00 && raw_tx[5] == 0x01);
1699
1700 if (!has_witness) {
1701 // Legacy tx: txid = double-SHA256 of the entire raw bytes
1702 auto h = secp256k1::SHA256::hash256(raw_tx, raw_tx_len);
1703 std::memcpy(txid_out, h.data(), 32);
1704 return UFSECP_OK;
1705 }
1706
1707 // Witness tx: strip marker+flag and witness data
1708 // Legacy = version(4) | inputs | outputs | locktime(4)
1709 secp256k1::SHA256 h1;
1710 // version
1711 h1.update(raw_tx, 4);
1712
1713 // Skip marker+flag, parse inputs+outputs from offset 6
1714 size_t off = 6;
1715 uint64_t n_in = 0;
1716 size_t cs_start = off;
1717 if (!read_compact_size(raw_tx, raw_tx_len, off, n_in)) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1718
1719 // Record start of vin count for hashing
1720 size_t io_start = cs_start;
1721
1722 // Skip all inputs (each: txid(32) + vout(4) + scriptSig + sequence(4))
1723 for (uint64_t i = 0; i < n_in; ++i) {
1724 if (off + 36 > raw_tx_len) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1725 off += 36; // txid + vout
1726 if (!skip_compact_bytes(raw_tx, raw_tx_len, off)) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1727 if (off + 4 > raw_tx_len) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1728 off += 4; // sequence
1729 }
1730
1731 // Parse outputs count
1732 uint64_t n_out = 0;
1733 if (!read_compact_size(raw_tx, raw_tx_len, off, n_out)) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1734
1735 // Skip all outputs (each: value(8) + scriptPubKey)
1736 for (uint64_t i = 0; i < n_out; ++i) {
1737 if (off + 8 > raw_tx_len) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1738 off += 8; // value
1739 if (!skip_compact_bytes(raw_tx, raw_tx_len, off)) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1740 }
1741 size_t io_end = off;
1742
1743 // Hash inputs+outputs section (cs_start..io_end)
1744 h1.update(raw_tx + io_start, io_end - io_start);
1745
1746 // locktime = last 4 bytes
1747 if (raw_tx_len < 4) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1748 h1.update(raw_tx + raw_tx_len - 4, 4);
1749
1750 auto first = h1.finalize();
1751 secp256k1::SHA256 h2;
1752 h2.update(first.data(), 32);
1753 auto txid = h2.finalize();
1754 std::memcpy(txid_out, txid.data(), 32);
1755 return UFSECP_OK;
1756}
1757
1758ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip144_wtxid(
1759 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1760 const uint8_t* raw_tx, size_t raw_tx_len,
1761 uint8_t wtxid_out[32]) {
1762 if (!ctx || !raw_tx || !wtxid_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1763 if (raw_tx_len < 10) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1764
1765 // wtxid = double-SHA256 of the full witness-serialized tx
1766 auto h = secp256k1::SHA256::hash256(raw_tx, raw_tx_len);
1767 std::memcpy(wtxid_out, h.data(), 32);
1768 return UFSECP_OK;
1769}
1770
1771ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip144_witness_commitment(
1772 const uint8_t witness_root[32],
1773 const uint8_t witness_nonce[32],
1774 uint8_t commitment_out[32]) {
1775 if (!witness_root || !witness_nonce || !commitment_out)
1776 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1777
1778 std::array<uint8_t, 32> wr, wn;
1779 std::memcpy(wr.data(), witness_root, 32);
1780 std::memcpy(wn.data(), witness_nonce, 32);
1781
1782 auto c = secp256k1::witness_commitment(wr, wn);
1783 std::memcpy(commitment_out, c.data(), 32);
1784 return UFSECP_OK;
1785}
1786
1787/* ===========================================================================
1788 * BIP-141: Segregated Witness — Witness Programs
1789 * =========================================================================== */
1790
1791int ufsecp_segwit_is_witness_program(
1792 const uint8_t* script, size_t script_len) {
1793 if (!script) return 0;
1794 return secp256k1::is_witness_program(script, script_len) ? 1 : 0;
1795}
1796
1797ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_segwit_parse_program(
1798 const uint8_t* script, size_t script_len,
1799 int* version_out,
1800 uint8_t* program_out, size_t* program_len_out) {
1801 if (!script || !version_out || !program_out || !program_len_out)
1802 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1803
1804 auto wp = secp256k1::parse_witness_program(script, script_len);
1805 if (wp.version < 0) {
1806 *version_out = -1;
1807 *program_len_out = 0;
1808 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1809 }
1810 if (wp.program.size() > 40) return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; /* BIP-141 cap */
1811 *version_out = wp.version;
1812 *program_len_out = wp.program.size();
1813 std::memcpy(program_out, wp.program.data(), wp.program.size());
1814 return UFSECP_OK;
1815}
1816
1817ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_segwit_p2wpkh_spk(
1818 const uint8_t pubkey_hash[20],
1819 uint8_t spk_out[22]) {
1820 if (!pubkey_hash || !spk_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1821
1822 auto spk = secp256k1::segwit_scriptpubkey_p2wpkh(pubkey_hash);
1823 std::memcpy(spk_out, spk.data(), 22);
1824 return UFSECP_OK;
1825}
1826
1827ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_segwit_p2wsh_spk(
1828 const uint8_t script_hash[32],
1829 uint8_t spk_out[34]) {
1830 if (!script_hash || !spk_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1831
1832 auto spk = secp256k1::segwit_scriptpubkey_p2wsh(script_hash);
1833 std::memcpy(spk_out, spk.data(), 34);
1834 return UFSECP_OK;
1835}
1836
1837ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_segwit_p2tr_spk(
1838 const uint8_t output_key[32],
1839 uint8_t spk_out[34]) {
1840 if (!output_key || !spk_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1841
1842 auto spk = secp256k1::segwit_scriptpubkey_p2tr(output_key);
1843 std::memcpy(spk_out, spk.data(), 34);
1844 return UFSECP_OK;
1845}
1846
1847ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_segwit_witness_script_hash(
1848 const uint8_t* script, size_t script_len,
1849 uint8_t hash_out[32]) {
1850 // Allow (nullptr, 0) as a valid empty-script input; only reject null when len > 0
1851 if ((!script && script_len > 0) || !hash_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1852
1853 auto h = secp256k1::witness_script_hash(script, script_len);
1854 std::memcpy(hash_out, h.data(), 32);
1855 return UFSECP_OK;
1856}
1857
1858/* ===========================================================================
1859 * BIP-342: Tapscript Sighash
1860 * =========================================================================== */
1861
1862// Helper: build TapSighashTxData from flat arrays,
1863// converting flattened prevout_txids to array-of-array.
1864static secp256k1::TapSighashTxData build_tap_tx_data(
1865 uint32_t version, uint32_t locktime,
1866 size_t input_count,
1867 const uint8_t* prevout_txids_flat,
1868 const uint32_t* prevout_vouts,
1869 const uint64_t* input_amounts,
1870 const uint32_t* input_sequences,
1871 const uint8_t* const* input_spks,
1872 const size_t* input_spk_lens,
1873 size_t output_count,
1874 const uint64_t* output_values,
1875 const uint8_t* const* output_spks,
1876 const size_t* output_spk_lens,
1877 std::vector<std::array<uint8_t, 32>>& txid_storage) {
1878
1879 // Convert flat txid array to array-of-array
1880 txid_storage.resize(input_count);
1881 for (size_t i = 0; i < input_count; ++i) {
1882 std::memcpy(txid_storage[i].data(), prevout_txids_flat + i * 32, 32);
1883 }
1884
1885 secp256k1::TapSighashTxData td{};
1886 td.version = version;
1887 td.locktime = locktime;
1888 td.input_count = input_count;
1889 td.prevout_txids = txid_storage.data();
1890 td.prevout_vouts = prevout_vouts;
1891 td.input_amounts = input_amounts;
1892 td.input_sequences = input_sequences;
1893 td.input_scriptpubkeys = input_spks;
1894 td.input_scriptpubkey_lens = input_spk_lens;
1895 td.output_count = output_count;
1896 td.output_values = output_values;
1897 td.output_scriptpubkeys = output_spks;
1898 td.output_scriptpubkey_lens = output_spk_lens;
1899 return td;
1900}
1901
1902ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_taproot_keypath_sighash(
1903 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1904 uint32_t version, uint32_t locktime,
1905 size_t input_count,
1906 const uint8_t* prevout_txids,
1907 const uint32_t* prevout_vouts,
1908 const uint64_t* input_amounts,
1909 const uint32_t* input_sequences,
1910 const uint8_t* const* input_spks,
1911 const size_t* input_spk_lens,
1912 size_t output_count,
1913 const uint64_t* output_values,
1914 const uint8_t* const* output_spks,
1915 const size_t* output_spk_lens,
1916 size_t input_index,
1917 uint8_t hash_type,
1918 const uint8_t* annex, size_t annex_len,
1919 uint8_t sighash_out[32]) {
1920 if (!ctx || !prevout_txids || !prevout_vouts || !input_amounts ||
1921 !input_sequences || !input_spks || !input_spk_lens ||
1922 !output_values || !output_spks || !output_spk_lens || !sighash_out)
1923 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1924 if (input_index >= input_count)
1925 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1926 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1927
1928 try {
1929 std::vector<std::array<uint8_t, 32>> txid_storage;
1930 auto td = build_tap_tx_data(version, locktime, input_count,
1931 prevout_txids, prevout_vouts, input_amounts, input_sequences,
1932 input_spks, input_spk_lens, output_count, output_values,
1933 output_spks, output_spk_lens, txid_storage);
1934
1935 auto h = secp256k1::taproot_keypath_sighash(td, input_index, hash_type,
1936 annex, annex_len);
1937 std::memcpy(sighash_out, h.data(), 32);
1938 return UFSECP_OK;
1939 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
1940}
1941
1942ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_tapscript_sighash(
1943 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1944 uint32_t version, uint32_t locktime,
1945 size_t input_count,
1946 const uint8_t* prevout_txids,
1947 const uint32_t* prevout_vouts,
1948 const uint64_t* input_amounts,
1949 const uint32_t* input_sequences,
1950 const uint8_t* const* input_spks,
1951 const size_t* input_spk_lens,
1952 size_t output_count,
1953 const uint64_t* output_values,
1954 const uint8_t* const* output_spks,
1955 const size_t* output_spk_lens,
1956 size_t input_index,
1957 uint8_t hash_type,
1958 const uint8_t tapleaf_hash[32],
1959 uint8_t key_version,
1960 uint32_t code_separator_pos,
1961 const uint8_t* annex, size_t annex_len,
1962 uint8_t sighash_out[32]) {
1963 if (!ctx || !prevout_txids || !prevout_vouts || !input_amounts ||
1964 !input_sequences || !input_spks || !input_spk_lens ||
1965 !output_values || !output_spks || !output_spk_lens ||
1966 !tapleaf_hash || !sighash_out)
1967 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1968 if (input_index >= input_count)
1969 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
1970 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
1971
1972 try {
1973 std::vector<std::array<uint8_t, 32>> txid_storage;
1974 auto td = build_tap_tx_data(version, locktime, input_count,
1975 prevout_txids, prevout_vouts, input_amounts, input_sequences,
1976 input_spks, input_spk_lens, output_count, output_values,
1977 output_spks, output_spk_lens, txid_storage);
1978
1979 std::array<uint8_t, 32> tlh;
1980 std::memcpy(tlh.data(), tapleaf_hash, 32);
1981
1982 auto h = secp256k1::tapscript_sighash(td, input_index, hash_type,
1983 tlh, key_version, code_separator_pos,
1984 annex, annex_len);
1985 std::memcpy(sighash_out, h.data(), 32);
1986 return UFSECP_OK;
1987 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
1988}
1989
1990/* ===========================================================================
1991 * Public key arithmetic
1992 * =========================================================================== */
1993
1994ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pubkey_add(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
1995 const uint8_t a33[33],
1996 const uint8_t b33[33],
1997 uint8_t out33[33]) {
1998 if (!ctx || !a33 || !b33 || !out33) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
1999 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2000 auto pa = point_from_compressed(a33);
2001 if (pa.is_infinity()) {
2002 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey a");
2003 }
2004 auto pb = point_from_compressed(b33);
2005 if (pb.is_infinity()) {
2006 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey b");
2007 }
2008 auto sum = pa.add(pb);
2009 if (sum.is_infinity()) {
2010 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "sum is point at infinity");
2011 }
2012 point_to_compressed(sum, out33);
2013 return UFSECP_OK;
2014}
2015
2016ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pubkey_negate(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2017 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
2018 uint8_t out33[33]) {
2019 if (!ctx || !pubkey33 || !out33) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2020 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2021 auto p = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
2022 if (p.is_infinity()) {
2023 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
2024 }
2025 auto neg = p.negate();
2026 point_to_compressed(neg, out33);
2027 return UFSECP_OK;
2028}
2029
2030ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pubkey_tweak_add(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2031 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
2032 const uint8_t tweak[32],
2033 uint8_t out33[33]) {
2034 if (!ctx || !pubkey33 || !tweak || !out33) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2035 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2036 auto p = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
2037 if (p.is_infinity()) {
2038 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
2039 }
2040 Scalar tw;
2041 if (!scalar_parse_strict(tweak, tw)) {
2042 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "tweak >= n");
2043 }
2044 auto tG = Point::generator().scalar_mul(tw);
2045 auto result = p.add(tG);
2046 if (result.is_infinity()) {
2047 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "tweak_add resulted in infinity");
2048 }
2049 point_to_compressed(result, out33);
2050 return UFSECP_OK;
2051}
2052
2053ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pubkey_tweak_mul(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2054 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
2055 const uint8_t tweak[32],
2056 uint8_t out33[33]) {
2057 if (!ctx || !pubkey33 || !tweak || !out33) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2058 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2059 auto p = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
2060 if (p.is_infinity()) {
2061 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
2062 }
2063 Scalar tw;
2064 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(tweak, tw)) {
2065 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "tweak is zero or >= n");
2066 }
2067 auto result = p.scalar_mul(tw);
2068 if (result.is_infinity()) {
2069 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "tweak_mul resulted in infinity");
2070 }
2071 point_to_compressed(result, out33);
2072 return UFSECP_OK;
2073}
2074
2075ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pubkey_combine(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2076 const uint8_t* pubkeys,
2077 size_t n,
2078 uint8_t out33[33]) {
2079 if (!ctx || !pubkeys || !out33) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2080 if (n == 0) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "need >= 1 pubkey");
2081 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2082 std::size_t total_pubkey_bytes = 0;
2083 if (!checked_mul_size(n, static_cast<std::size_t>(33), total_pubkey_bytes)) {
2084 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "pubkey array length too large");
2085 }
2086 auto acc = point_from_compressed(pubkeys);
2087 if (acc.is_infinity()) {
2088 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey[0]");
2089 }
2090 for (size_t i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
2091 auto pi = point_from_compressed(pubkeys + i * 33);
2092 if (pi.is_infinity()) {
2093 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey in array");
2094 }
2095 acc = acc.add(pi);
2096 }
2097 if (acc.is_infinity()) {
2098 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "combined pubkey is infinity");
2099 }
2100 point_to_compressed(acc, out33);
2101 return UFSECP_OK;
2102}
2103
2104/* ===========================================================================
2105 * BIP-39
2106 * =========================================================================== */
2107
2108ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip39_generate(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2109 size_t entropy_bytes,
2110 const uint8_t* entropy_in,
2111 char* mnemonic_out,
2112 size_t* mnemonic_len) {
2113 if (!ctx || !mnemonic_out || !mnemonic_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2114 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2115 if (entropy_bytes != 16 && entropy_bytes != 20 && entropy_bytes != 24 &&
2116 entropy_bytes != 28 && entropy_bytes != 32) {
2117 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "entropy must be 16/20/24/28/32");
2118 }
2119 try {
2120 auto [mnemonic, ok] = secp256k1::bip39_generate(entropy_bytes, entropy_in);
2121 if (!ok) {
2122 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "BIP-39 generation failed");
2123 }
2124 if (*mnemonic_len < mnemonic.size() + 1) {
2125 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "mnemonic buffer too small");
2126 }
2127 std::memcpy(mnemonic_out, mnemonic.c_str(), mnemonic.size() + 1);
2128 *mnemonic_len = mnemonic.size();
2129 return UFSECP_OK;
2130 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2131}
2132
2133ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip39_validate(const ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2134 const char* mnemonic) {
2135 if (!ctx || !mnemonic) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2136 try {
2137 if (!secp256k1::bip39_validate(std::string(mnemonic))) {
2138 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
2139 }
2140 return UFSECP_OK;
2141 } catch (...) { return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; }
2142}
2143
2144ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip39_to_seed(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2145 const char* mnemonic,
2146 const char* passphrase,
2147 uint8_t seed64_out[64]) {
2148 if (!ctx || !mnemonic || !seed64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2149 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2150 try {
2151 const std::string pass = passphrase ? passphrase : "";
2152 auto [seed, ok] = secp256k1::bip39_mnemonic_to_seed(std::string(mnemonic), pass);
2153 if (!ok) {
2154 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid mnemonic");
2155 }
2156 std::memcpy(seed64_out, seed.data(), 64);
2157 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(seed.data(), seed.size());
2158 return UFSECP_OK;
2159 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2160}
2161
2162ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip39_to_entropy(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2163 const char* mnemonic,
2164 uint8_t* entropy_out,
2165 size_t* entropy_len) {
2166 if (!ctx || !mnemonic || !entropy_out || !entropy_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2167 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2168 try {
2169 auto [ent, ok] = secp256k1::bip39_mnemonic_to_entropy(std::string(mnemonic));
2170 if (!ok) {
2171 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid mnemonic");
2172 }
2173 if (*entropy_len < ent.length) {
2174 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "entropy buffer too small");
2175 }
2176 std::memcpy(entropy_out, ent.data.data(), ent.length);
2177 *entropy_len = ent.length;
2178 return UFSECP_OK;
2179 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2180}
2181
2182/* ===========================================================================
2183 * Batch verification
2184 * =========================================================================== */
2185
2186ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_batch_verify(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2187 const uint8_t* entries, size_t n) {
2188 if (!ctx || !entries) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2189 if (n == 0) return UFSECP_OK;
2190 if (n > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch count too large");
2191 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2192 std::size_t total_bytes = 0;
2193 if (!checked_mul_size(n, std::size_t{128}, total_bytes))
2194 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch size overflow");
2195 try {
2196 /* Each entry: 32-byte xonly pubkey | 32-byte msg | 64-byte sig = 128 bytes */
2197 std::vector<secp256k1::SchnorrBatchEntry> batch(n);
2198 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
2199 const uint8_t* e = entries + i * 128;
2200 // Strict: reject x-only pubkey >= p at ABI gate
2201 FE pk_fe;
2202 if (!FE::parse_bytes_strict(e, pk_fe)) {
2203 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "non-canonical pubkey (x>=p) in batch");
2204 }
2205 std::memcpy(batch[i].pubkey_x.data(), e, 32);
2206 std::memcpy(batch[i].message.data(), e + 32, 32);
2207 if (!secp256k1::SchnorrSignature::parse_strict(e + 64, batch[i].signature)) {
2208 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid Schnorr sig in batch");
2209 }
2210 }
