2211 if (!secp256k1::schnorr_batch_verify(batch)) {
2212 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "batch verify failed");
2213 }
2214 return UFSECP_OK;
2215 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2216}
2217
2218ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_batch_verify(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2219 const uint8_t* entries, size_t n) {
2220 if (!ctx || !entries) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2221 if (n == 0) return UFSECP_OK;
2222 if (n > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch count too large");
2223 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2224 std::size_t total_bytes = 0;
2225 if (!checked_mul_size(n, std::size_t{129}, total_bytes))
2226 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch size overflow");
2227 try {
2228 /* Each entry: 32-byte msg | 33-byte pubkey | 64-byte sig = 129 bytes */
2229 std::vector<secp256k1::ECDSABatchEntry> batch(n);
2230 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
2231 const uint8_t* e = entries + i * 129;
2232 std::memcpy(batch[i].msg_hash.data(), e, 32);
2233 batch[i].public_key = point_from_compressed(e + 32);
2234 if (batch[i].public_key.is_infinity()) {
2235 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey in batch");
2236 }
2237 std::array<uint8_t, 64> compact;
2238 std::memcpy(compact.data(), e + 65, 64);
2239 if (!secp256k1::ECDSASignature::parse_compact_strict(compact, batch[i].signature)) {
2240 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid ECDSA sig in batch");
2241 }
2242 }
2243 if (!secp256k1::ecdsa_batch_verify(batch)) {
2244 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "batch verify failed");
2245 }
2246 return UFSECP_OK;
2247 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2248}
2249
2250ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_batch_identify_invalid(
2251 ufsecp_ctx* ctx, const uint8_t* entries, size_t n,
2252 size_t* invalid_out, size_t* invalid_count) {
2253 if (!ctx || !entries || !invalid_out || !invalid_count) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2254 if (n > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch count too large");
2255 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2256 try {
2257 std::vector<secp256k1::SchnorrBatchEntry> batch(n);
2258 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
2259 const uint8_t* e = entries + i * 128;
2260 FE pk_fe;
2261 if (!FE::parse_bytes_strict(e, pk_fe)) {
2262 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "non-canonical pubkey (x>=p) in batch");
2263 }
2264 std::memcpy(batch[i].pubkey_x.data(), e, 32);
2265 std::memcpy(batch[i].message.data(), e + 32, 32);
2266 if (!secp256k1::SchnorrSignature::parse_strict(e + 64, batch[i].signature)) {
2267 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid Schnorr sig in batch");
2268 }
2269 }
2270 auto invalids = secp256k1::schnorr_batch_identify_invalid(batch.data(), n);
2271 size_t const capacity = *invalid_count;
2272 size_t const count = invalids.size() < capacity ? invalids.size() : capacity;
2273 *invalid_count = invalids.size();
2274 for (size_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
2275 invalid_out[i] = invalids[i];
2276 }
2277 return UFSECP_OK;
2278 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2279}
2280
2281ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_batch_identify_invalid(
2282 ufsecp_ctx* ctx, const uint8_t* entries, size_t n,
2283 size_t* invalid_out, size_t* invalid_count) {
2284 if (!ctx || !entries || !invalid_out || !invalid_count) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2285 if (n > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "batch count too large");
2286 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2287 try {
2288 std::vector<secp256k1::ECDSABatchEntry> batch(n);
2289 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
2290 const uint8_t* e = entries + i * 129;
2291 std::memcpy(batch[i].msg_hash.data(), e, 32);
2292 batch[i].public_key = point_from_compressed(e + 32);
2293 if (batch[i].public_key.is_infinity()) {
2294 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey in batch");
2295 }
2296 std::array<uint8_t, 64> compact;
2297 std::memcpy(compact.data(), e + 65, 64);
2298 if (!secp256k1::ECDSASignature::parse_compact_strict(compact, batch[i].signature)) {
2299 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid ECDSA sig in batch");
2300 }
2301 }
2302 auto invalids = secp256k1::ecdsa_batch_identify_invalid(batch.data(), n);
2303 size_t const capacity = *invalid_count;
2304 size_t const count = invalids.size() < capacity ? invalids.size() : capacity;
2305 *invalid_count = invalids.size();
2306 for (size_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
2307 invalid_out[i] = invalids[i];
2308 }
2309 return UFSECP_OK;
2310 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2311}
2312
2313/* ===========================================================================
2314 * SHA-512
2315 * =========================================================================== */
2316
2317ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_sha512(const uint8_t* data, size_t len,
2318 uint8_t digest64_out[64]) {
2319 if (!data || !digest64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2320 auto hash = secp256k1::SHA512::hash(data, len);
2321 std::memcpy(digest64_out, hash.data(), 64);
2322 return UFSECP_OK;
2323}
2324
2325/* ===========================================================================
2326 * Multi-scalar multiplication
2327 * =========================================================================== */
2328
2329ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_shamir_trick(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2330 const uint8_t a[32], const uint8_t P33[33],
2331 const uint8_t b[32], const uint8_t Q33[33],
2332 uint8_t out33[33]) {
2333 if (!ctx || !a || !P33 || !b || !Q33 || !out33) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2334 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2335 Scalar sa, sb;
2336 if (!scalar_parse_strict(a, sa)) {
2337 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "scalar a >= n");
2338 }
2339 if (!scalar_parse_strict(b, sb)) {
2340 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "scalar b >= n");
2341 }
2342 auto P = point_from_compressed(P33);
2343 if (P.is_infinity()) {
2344 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid point P");
2345 }
2346 auto Q = point_from_compressed(Q33);
2347 if (Q.is_infinity()) {
2348 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid point Q");
2349 }
2350 auto result = secp256k1::shamir_trick(sa, P, sb, Q);
2351 if (result.is_infinity()) {
2352 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "result is infinity");
2353 }
2354 point_to_compressed(result, out33);
2355 return UFSECP_OK;
2356}
2357
2358ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_multi_scalar_mul(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2359 const uint8_t* scalars,
2360 const uint8_t* points,
2361 size_t n,
2362 uint8_t out33[33]) {
2363 if (!ctx || !scalars || !points || !out33) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2364 if (n == 0) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "n must be >= 1");
2365 if (n > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "n too large");
2366 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2367 std::size_t total_scalar_bytes = 0;
2368 std::size_t total_point_bytes = 0;
2369 if (!checked_mul_size(n, static_cast<std::size_t>(32), total_scalar_bytes)
2370 || !checked_mul_size(n, static_cast<std::size_t>(33), total_point_bytes)) {
2371 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "scalar/point array length too large");
2372 }
2373 try {
2374 std::vector<Scalar> svec(n);
2375 std::vector<Point> pvec(n);
2376 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
2377 if (!scalar_parse_strict(scalars + i * 32, svec[i])) {
2378 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "scalar >= n");
2379 }
2380 pvec[i] = point_from_compressed(points + i * 33);
2381 if (pvec[i].is_infinity()) {
2382 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid point in array");
2383 }
2384 }
2385 auto result = secp256k1::multi_scalar_mul(svec, pvec);
2386 if (result.is_infinity()) {
2387 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "MSM result is infinity");
2388 }
2389 point_to_compressed(result, out33);
2390 return UFSECP_OK;
2391 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2392}
2393
2394/* ===========================================================================
2395 * MuSig2 (BIP-327)
2396 * =========================================================================== */
2397
2398ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_musig2_key_agg(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2399 const uint8_t* pubkeys, size_t n,
2400 uint8_t keyagg_out[UFSECP_MUSIG2_KEYAGG_LEN],
2401 uint8_t agg_pubkey32_out[32]) {
2402 if (!ctx || !pubkeys || !keyagg_out || !agg_pubkey32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2403 std::memset(keyagg_out, 0, UFSECP_MUSIG2_KEYAGG_LEN);
2404 std::memset(agg_pubkey32_out, 0, 32);
2405 if (n < 2) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "need >= 2 pubkeys");
2406 if (n > kMuSig2MaxKeyAggParticipants) {
2407 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "too many pubkeys for keyagg blob");
2408 }
2409 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2410 try {
2411 std::vector<std::array<uint8_t, 32>> pks(n);
2412 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
2413 std::memcpy(pks[i].data(), pubkeys + i * 32, 32);
2414 }
2415 auto kagg = secp256k1::musig2_key_agg(pks);
2416 std::memcpy(agg_pubkey32_out, kagg.Q_x.data(), 32);
2417 /* Serialize key agg ctx: n(4) | Q_negated(1) | Q_compressed(33) | coefficients(n*32) */
2418 std::memset(keyagg_out, 0, UFSECP_MUSIG2_KEYAGG_LEN);
2419 const auto nk = static_cast<uint32_t>(kagg.key_coefficients.size());
2420 std::memcpy(keyagg_out, &nk, 4);
2421 keyagg_out[4] = kagg.Q_negated ? 1 : 0;
2422 point_to_compressed(kagg.Q, keyagg_out + 5);
2423 for (uint32_t i = 0; i < nk && (38u + (i+1)*32u <= UFSECP_MUSIG2_KEYAGG_LEN); ++i) {
2424 scalar_to_bytes(kagg.key_coefficients[i], keyagg_out + 38 + static_cast<size_t>(i) * 32);
2425 }
2426 return UFSECP_OK;
2427 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2428}
2429
2430ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_musig2_nonce_gen(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2431 const uint8_t privkey[32],
2432 const uint8_t pubkey32[32],
2433 const uint8_t agg_pubkey32[32],
2434 const uint8_t msg32[32],
2435 const uint8_t extra_in[32],
2436 uint8_t secnonce_out[UFSECP_MUSIG2_SECNONCE_LEN],
2437 uint8_t pubnonce_out[UFSECP_MUSIG2_PUBNONCE_LEN]) {
2438 if (!ctx || !privkey || !pubkey32 || !agg_pubkey32 || !msg32 ||
2439 !secnonce_out || !pubnonce_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2440 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2441 Scalar sk;
2442 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
2443 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
2444 }
2445 std::array<uint8_t, 32> pk_arr, agg_arr, msg_arr;
2446 std::memcpy(pk_arr.data(), pubkey32, 32);
2447 std::memcpy(agg_arr.data(), agg_pubkey32, 32);
2448 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
2449 auto [sec, pub] = secp256k1::musig2_nonce_gen(sk, pk_arr, agg_arr, msg_arr, extra_in);
2450 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
2451 /* Secret nonce: k1 || k2 */
2452 auto k1_bytes = sec.k1.to_bytes();
2453 auto k2_bytes = sec.k2.to_bytes();
2454 std::memcpy(secnonce_out, k1_bytes.data(), 32);
2455 std::memcpy(secnonce_out + 32, k2_bytes.data(), 32);
2456 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(k1_bytes.data(), k1_bytes.size());
2457 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(k2_bytes.data(), k2_bytes.size());
2458 /* Public nonce: R1(33) || R2(33) */
2459 auto pn = pub.serialize();
2460 std::memcpy(pubnonce_out, pn.data(), 66);
2461 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sec, sizeof(sec));
2462 return UFSECP_OK;
2463}
2464
2465ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_musig2_nonce_agg(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2466 const uint8_t* pubnonces, size_t n,
2467 uint8_t aggnonce_out[UFSECP_MUSIG2_AGGNONCE_LEN]) {
2468 if (!ctx || !pubnonces || !aggnonce_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2469 std::memset(aggnonce_out, 0, UFSECP_MUSIG2_AGGNONCE_LEN);
2470 if (n < 2) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "need >= 2 nonces");
2471 if (n > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "nonce count too large");
2472 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2473 std::size_t total_bytes = 0;
2474 if (!checked_mul_size(n, std::size_t{66}, total_bytes))
2475 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "nonce array size overflow");
2476 try {
2477 std::vector<secp256k1::MuSig2PubNonce> pns(n);
2478 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
2479 if (point_from_compressed(pubnonces + i * 66).is_infinity()) {
2480 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid pubnonce R1");
2481 }
2482 if (point_from_compressed(pubnonces + i * 66 + 33).is_infinity()) {
2483 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid pubnonce R2");
2484 }
2485 std::array<uint8_t, 66> buf;
2486 std::memcpy(buf.data(), pubnonces + i * 66, 66);
2487 pns[i] = secp256k1::MuSig2PubNonce::deserialize(buf);
2488 }
2489 auto agg = secp256k1::musig2_nonce_agg(pns);
2490 /* Serialize: R1(33) || R2(33) */
2491 auto r1 = agg.R1.to_compressed();
2492 auto r2 = agg.R2.to_compressed();
2493 std::memcpy(aggnonce_out, r1.data(), 33);
2494 std::memcpy(aggnonce_out + 33, r2.data(), 33);
2495 return UFSECP_OK;
2496 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2497}
2498
2499ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_musig2_start_sign_session(
2500 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2501 const uint8_t aggnonce[UFSECP_MUSIG2_AGGNONCE_LEN],
2502 const uint8_t keyagg[UFSECP_MUSIG2_KEYAGG_LEN],
2503 const uint8_t msg32[32],
2504 uint8_t session_out[UFSECP_MUSIG2_SESSION_LEN]) {
2505 if (!ctx || !aggnonce || !keyagg || !msg32 || !session_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2506 std::memset(session_out, 0, UFSECP_MUSIG2_SESSION_LEN);
2507 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2508 /* Deserialize agg nonce */
2509 secp256k1::MuSig2AggNonce an;
2510 an.R1 = point_from_compressed(aggnonce);
2511 if (an.R1.is_infinity()) {
2512 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid agg nonce R1");
2513 }
2514 an.R2 = point_from_compressed(aggnonce + 33);
2515 if (an.R2.is_infinity()) {
2516 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid agg nonce R2");
2517 }
2518 /* Deserialize key agg context */
2519 secp256k1::MuSig2KeyAggCtx kagg;
2520 {
2521 const ufsecp_error_t rc = parse_musig2_keyagg(ctx, keyagg, kagg);
2522 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) {
2523 return rc;
2524 }
2525 }
2526 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
2527 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
2528 auto sess = secp256k1::musig2_start_sign_session(an, kagg, msg_arr);
2529 /* Serialize session: R(33) | b(32) | e(32) | R_negated(1) = 98 bytes */
2530 std::memset(session_out, 0, UFSECP_MUSIG2_SESSION_LEN);
2531 point_to_compressed(sess.R, session_out);
2532 scalar_to_bytes(sess.b, session_out + 33);
2533 scalar_to_bytes(sess.e, session_out + 65);
2534 session_out[97] = sess.R_negated ? 1 : 0;
2535 const uint32_t participant_count = static_cast<uint32_t>(kagg.key_coefficients.size());
2536 std::memcpy(session_out + kMuSig2SessionCountOffset, &participant_count, sizeof(participant_count));
2537 return UFSECP_OK;
2538}
2539
2540ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_musig2_partial_sign(
2541 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2542 uint8_t secnonce[UFSECP_MUSIG2_SECNONCE_LEN],
2543 const uint8_t privkey[32],
2544 const uint8_t keyagg[UFSECP_MUSIG2_KEYAGG_LEN],
2545 const uint8_t session[UFSECP_MUSIG2_SESSION_LEN],
2546 size_t signer_index,
2547 uint8_t partial_sig32_out[32]) {
2548 if (!ctx || !secnonce || !privkey || !keyagg || !session || !partial_sig32_out) {
2549 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2550 }
2551 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2552 Scalar sk;
2553 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
2554 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
2555 }
2556 secp256k1::MuSig2SecNonce sn;
2557 Scalar k1, k2;
2558 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(secnonce, k1)) {
2559 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid secnonce k1");
2560 }
2561 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(secnonce + 32, k2)) {
2562 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid secnonce k2");
2563 }
2564 sn.k1 = k1;
2565 sn.k2 = k2;
2566 secp256k1::MuSig2KeyAggCtx kagg;
2567 {
2568 const ufsecp_error_t rc = parse_musig2_keyagg(ctx, keyagg, kagg);
2569 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) {
2570 return rc;
2571 }
2572 }
2573 if (signer_index >= kagg.key_coefficients.size()) {
2574 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "signer_index out of range");
2575 }
2576 secp256k1::MuSig2Session sess;
2577 uint32_t session_participant_count = 0;
2578 {
2579 const ufsecp_error_t rc = parse_musig2_session(ctx, session, sess, session_participant_count);
2580 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) {
2581 return rc;
2582 }
2583 }
2584 if (session_participant_count != kagg.key_coefficients.size()) {
2585 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "session participant count does not match keyagg");
2586 }
2587 auto psig = secp256k1::musig2_partial_sign(sn, sk, kagg, sess, signer_index);
2588 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
2589 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sn, sizeof(sn));
2590 // Consume caller's secnonce to prevent catastrophic nonce reuse
2591 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(secnonce, UFSECP_MUSIG2_SECNONCE_LEN);
2592 scalar_to_bytes(psig, partial_sig32_out);
2593 return UFSECP_OK;
2594}
2595
2596ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_musig2_partial_verify(
2597 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2598 const uint8_t partial_sig32[32],
2599 const uint8_t pubnonce[UFSECP_MUSIG2_PUBNONCE_LEN],
2600 const uint8_t pubkey32[32],
2601 const uint8_t keyagg[UFSECP_MUSIG2_KEYAGG_LEN],
2602 const uint8_t session[UFSECP_MUSIG2_SESSION_LEN],
2603 size_t signer_index) {
2604 if (!ctx || !partial_sig32 || !pubnonce || !pubkey32 || !keyagg || !session) {
2605 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2606 }
2607 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2608 Scalar psig;
2609 if (!scalar_parse_strict(partial_sig32, psig)) {
2610 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "partial sig >= n");
2611 }
2612 std::array<uint8_t, 66> pn_buf;
2613 std::memcpy(pn_buf.data(), pubnonce, 66);
2614 auto pn = secp256k1::MuSig2PubNonce::deserialize(pn_buf);
2615 std::array<uint8_t, 32> pk_arr;
2616 std::memcpy(pk_arr.data(), pubkey32, 32);
2617 secp256k1::MuSig2KeyAggCtx kagg;
2618 {
2619 const ufsecp_error_t rc = parse_musig2_keyagg(ctx, keyagg, kagg);
2620 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) {
2621 return rc;
2622 }
2623 }
2624 if (signer_index >= kagg.key_coefficients.size()) {
2625 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "signer_index out of range");
2626 }
2627 secp256k1::MuSig2Session sess;
2628 uint32_t session_participant_count = 0;
2629 {
2630 const ufsecp_error_t rc = parse_musig2_session(ctx, session, sess, session_participant_count);
2631 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) {
2632 return rc;
2633 }
2634 }
2635 if (session_participant_count != kagg.key_coefficients.size()) {
2636 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "session participant count does not match keyagg");
2637 }
2638 if (!secp256k1::musig2_partial_verify(psig, pn, pk_arr, kagg, sess, signer_index)) {
2639 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "partial sig verify failed");
2640 }
2641 return UFSECP_OK;
2642}
2643
2644ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_musig2_partial_sig_agg(
2645 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2646 const uint8_t* partial_sigs, size_t n,
2647 const uint8_t session[UFSECP_MUSIG2_SESSION_LEN],
2648 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
2649 if (!ctx || !partial_sigs || !session || !sig64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2650 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2651 if (n == 0) {
2652 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "partial_sigs must be non-empty");
2653 }
2654 if (n > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "partial sig count too large");
2655 std::size_t total_bytes = 0;
2656 if (!checked_mul_size(n, std::size_t{32}, total_bytes))
2657 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "partial sig size overflow");
2658 try {
2659 std::vector<Scalar> psigs(n);
2660 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
2661 if (!scalar_parse_strict(partial_sigs + i * 32, psigs[i])) {
2662 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "partial sig >= n");
2663 }
2664 }
2665 secp256k1::MuSig2Session sess;
2666 uint32_t session_participant_count = 0;
2667 {
2668 const ufsecp_error_t rc = parse_musig2_session(ctx, session, sess, session_participant_count);
2669 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) {
2670 return rc;
2671 }
2672 }
2673 if (n != session_participant_count) {
2674 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "partial_sigs count does not match session participant count");
2675 }
2676 auto final_sig = secp256k1::musig2_partial_sig_agg(psigs, sess);
2677 std::memcpy(sig64_out, final_sig.data(), 64);
2678 return UFSECP_OK;
2679 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2680}
2681
2682/* ===========================================================================
2683 * FROST (threshold signatures)
2684 * =========================================================================== */
2685
2686ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_frost_keygen_begin(
2687 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2688 uint32_t participant_id, uint32_t threshold, uint32_t num_participants,
2689 const uint8_t seed[32],
2690 uint8_t* commits_out, size_t* commits_len,
2691 uint8_t* shares_out, size_t* shares_len) {
2692 if (!ctx || !seed || !commits_out || !commits_len || !shares_out || !shares_len) {
2693 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2694 }
2695 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2696 if (threshold < 2 || threshold > num_participants) {
2697 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid threshold");
2698 }
2699 if (participant_id == 0 || participant_id > num_participants) {
2700 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid participant_id");
2701 }
2702 if (num_participants > kMaxBatchN) {
2703 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "num_participants too large");
2704 }
2705 try {
2706 std::size_t required_commit_coeff_bytes = 0;
2707 std::size_t required_commits = 0;
2708 std::size_t required_shares = 0;
2709 if (!checked_mul_size(static_cast<std::size_t>(threshold), static_cast<std::size_t>(33), required_commit_coeff_bytes)
2710 || !checked_add_size(static_cast<std::size_t>(8), required_commit_coeff_bytes, required_commits)
2711 || !checked_mul_size(static_cast<std::size_t>(num_participants), static_cast<std::size_t>(UFSECP_FROST_SHARE_LEN), required_shares)) {
2712 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "FROST cardinality too large");
2713 }
2714 if (*commits_len < required_commits) {
2715 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "commits buffer too small");
2716 }
2717 if (*shares_len < required_shares) {
2718 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "shares buffer too small");
2719 }
2720 std::array<uint8_t, 32> seed_arr;
2721 std::memcpy(seed_arr.data(), seed, 32);
2722 auto [commit, shares] = secp256k1::frost_keygen_begin(
2723 participant_id, threshold, num_participants, seed_arr);
2724 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(seed_arr.data(), 32);
2725 auto erase_shares = [&]() {
2726 for (auto& share : shares) {
2727 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&share.value, sizeof(share.value));
2728 }
2729 };
2730 /* Serialize commitment: coeff count(4) + from(4) + coeffs(33 each) */
2731 const size_t coeff_count = commit.coeffs.size();
2732 const size_t needed_commits = 8 + coeff_count * 33;
2733 if (*commits_len < needed_commits) {
2734 erase_shares();
2735 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "commits buffer too small");
2736 }
2737 const auto cc32 = static_cast<uint32_t>(coeff_count);
2738 std::memcpy(commits_out, &cc32, 4);
2739 std::memcpy(commits_out + 4, &commit.from, 4);
2740 for (size_t i = 0; i < coeff_count; ++i) {
2741 point_to_compressed(commit.coeffs[i], commits_out + 8 + i * 33);
2742
2743 }
2744 *commits_len = 8 + coeff_count * 33;
2745 /* Serialize shares */
2746 const size_t needed_shares = shares.size() * UFSECP_FROST_SHARE_LEN;
2747 if (*shares_len < needed_shares) {
2748 erase_shares();
2749 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "shares buffer too small");
2750 }
2751 for (size_t i = 0; i < shares.size(); ++i) {
2752 uint8_t* s = shares_out + i * UFSECP_FROST_SHARE_LEN;
2753 std::memcpy(s, &shares[i].from, 4);
2754 scalar_to_bytes(shares[i].value, s + 4);
2755 }
2756 *shares_len = needed_shares;
2757 erase_shares();
2758 return UFSECP_OK;
2759 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2760}
2761
2762ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_frost_keygen_finalize(
2763 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2764 uint32_t participant_id,
2765 const uint8_t* all_commits, size_t commits_len,
2766 const uint8_t* received_shares, size_t shares_len,
2767 uint32_t threshold, uint32_t num_participants,
2768 uint8_t keypkg_out[UFSECP_FROST_KEYPKG_LEN]) {
2769 if (!ctx || !all_commits || !received_shares || !keypkg_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2770 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2771 if (threshold < 2 || threshold > num_participants) {
2772 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid threshold");
2773 }
2774 if (participant_id == 0 || participant_id > num_participants) {
2775 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid participant_id");
2776 }
2777 if (num_participants > kMaxBatchN) {
2778 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "num_participants too large");
2779 }
2780 try {
2781 std::size_t expected_commit_coeff_bytes = 0;
2782 std::size_t expected_commit_record_len = 0;
2783 std::size_t expected_commits_len = 0;
2784 std::size_t expected_shares_len = 0;
2785 if (!checked_mul_size(static_cast<std::size_t>(threshold), static_cast<std::size_t>(33), expected_commit_coeff_bytes)
2786 || !checked_add_size(static_cast<std::size_t>(8), expected_commit_coeff_bytes, expected_commit_record_len)
2787 || !checked_mul_size(static_cast<std::size_t>(num_participants), expected_commit_record_len, expected_commits_len)
2788 || !checked_mul_size(static_cast<std::size_t>(num_participants), static_cast<std::size_t>(UFSECP_FROST_SHARE_LEN), expected_shares_len)) {
2789 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "FROST cardinality too large");
2790 }
2791 if (commits_len != expected_commits_len) {
2792 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "all_commits length does not match threshold and num_participants");
2793 }
2794 if (shares_len != expected_shares_len) {
2795 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "received_shares length does not match num_participants");
2796 }
2797 /* Deserialize commitments */
2798 std::vector<secp256k1::FrostCommitment> commits;
2799 std::vector<uint8_t> seen_commit_from(static_cast<size_t>(num_participants) + 1, 0);
2800 size_t pos = 0;
2801 while (pos < commits_len) {
2802 secp256k1::FrostCommitment fc;
2803 uint32_t cc = 0;
2804 if (pos + 8 > commits_len) {
2805 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "truncated commit header");
2806 }
2807 std::memcpy(&cc, all_commits + pos, 4); pos += 4;
2808 std::memcpy(&fc.from, all_commits + pos, 4); pos += 4;
2809 if (cc != threshold) {
2810 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid commitment coefficient count");
2811 }
2812 if (fc.from == 0 || fc.from > num_participants) {
2813 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid commitment sender");
2814 }
2815 if (seen_commit_from[fc.from] != 0) {
2816 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "duplicate commitment sender");
2817 }
2818 seen_commit_from[fc.from] = 1;
2819 if (pos + static_cast<size_t>(cc) * 33 > commits_len) {
2820 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "truncated commit coefficients");
2821 }
2822 for (uint32_t j = 0; j < cc; ++j) {
2823 auto pt = point_from_compressed(all_commits + pos);
2824 if (pt.is_infinity()) {
2825 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid commitment coefficient");
2826 }
2827 fc.coeffs.push_back(pt);
2828 pos += 33;
2829 }
2830 commits.push_back(std::move(fc));
2831 }
2832 if (commits.size() != num_participants) {
2833 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid commitment count");
2834 }
2835 /* Deserialize shares */
2836 if (shares_len == 0 || (shares_len % UFSECP_FROST_SHARE_LEN) != 0) {
2837 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid share blob length");
2838 }
2839 const size_t n_shares = shares_len / UFSECP_FROST_SHARE_LEN;
2840 if (n_shares != num_participants) {
2841 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid share count");
2842 }
2843 std::vector<secp256k1::FrostShare> shares(n_shares);
2844 auto erase_shares = [&]() {
2845 for (auto& share : shares) {
2846 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&share.value, sizeof(share.value));
2847 }
2848 };
2849 std::vector<uint8_t> seen_share_from(static_cast<size_t>(num_participants) + 1, 0);
2850 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_shares; ++i) {
2851 const uint8_t* s = received_shares + i * UFSECP_FROST_SHARE_LEN;
2852 std::memcpy(&shares[i].from, s, 4);
2853 if (shares[i].from == 0 || shares[i].from > num_participants) {
2854 erase_shares();
2855 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid share sender");
2856 }
2857 if (seen_share_from[shares[i].from] != 0) {
2858 erase_shares();
2859 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "duplicate share sender");
2860 }
2861 seen_share_from[shares[i].from] = 1;
2862 shares[i].id = participant_id;
2863 Scalar v;
2864 if (!scalar_parse_strict(s + 4, v)) {
2865 erase_shares();
2866 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid share scalar");
2867 }
2868 shares[i].value = v;
2869 }
2870 auto [kp, ok] = secp256k1::frost_keygen_finalize(
2871 participant_id, commits, shares, threshold, num_participants);
2872 if (!ok) {
2873 erase_shares();
2874 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "FROST keygen finalize failed");
2875 }
2876 erase_shares();
2877 /* Serialize FrostKeyPackage: id(4) | threshold(4) | num_participants(4) |
2878 signing_share(32) | verification_share(33) | group_public_key(33) = 110 bytes */
2879 std::memset(keypkg_out, 0, UFSECP_FROST_KEYPKG_LEN);
2880 std::memcpy(keypkg_out, &kp.id, 4);
2881 std::memcpy(keypkg_out + 4, &kp.threshold, 4);
2882 std::memcpy(keypkg_out + 8, &kp.num_participants, 4);
2883 scalar_to_bytes(kp.signing_share, keypkg_out + 12);
2884 point_to_compressed(kp.verification_share, keypkg_out + 44);
2885 point_to_compressed(kp.group_public_key, keypkg_out + 77);
2886 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&kp.signing_share, sizeof(kp.signing_share));
2887 return UFSECP_OK;
2888 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
2889}
2890
2891ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_frost_sign_nonce_gen(
2892 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2893 uint32_t participant_id,
2894 const uint8_t nonce_seed[32],
2895 uint8_t nonce_out[UFSECP_FROST_NONCE_LEN],
2896 uint8_t nonce_commit_out[UFSECP_FROST_NONCE_COMMIT_LEN]) {
2897 if (!ctx || !nonce_seed || !nonce_out || !nonce_commit_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2898 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2899 if (participant_id == 0) {
2900 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid participant_id");
2901 }
2902 std::array<uint8_t, 32> seed_arr;
2903 std::memcpy(seed_arr.data(), nonce_seed, 32);
2904 auto [nonce, commit] = secp256k1::frost_sign_nonce_gen(participant_id, seed_arr);
2905 auto h_bytes = nonce.hiding_nonce.to_bytes();
2906 auto b_bytes = nonce.binding_nonce.to_bytes();
2907 std::memcpy(nonce_out, h_bytes.data(), 32);
2908 std::memcpy(nonce_out + 32, b_bytes.data(), 32);
2909 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(seed_arr.data(), 32);
2910 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&nonce.hiding_nonce, sizeof(nonce.hiding_nonce));
2911 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&nonce.binding_nonce, sizeof(nonce.binding_nonce));
2912 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(h_bytes.data(), 32);
2913 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(b_bytes.data(), 32);
2914 std::memcpy(nonce_commit_out, &commit.id, 4);
2915 auto hp = commit.hiding_point.to_compressed();
2916 auto bp = commit.binding_point.to_compressed();
2917 std::memcpy(nonce_commit_out + 4, hp.data(), 33);
2918 std::memcpy(nonce_commit_out + 37, bp.data(), 33);
2919 return UFSECP_OK;
2920}
2921
2937ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_frost_sign(
2938 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
2939 const uint8_t keypkg[UFSECP_FROST_KEYPKG_LEN],
2940 const uint8_t nonce[UFSECP_FROST_NONCE_LEN],
2941 const uint8_t msg32[32],
2942 const uint8_t* nonce_commits, size_t n_signers,
2943 uint8_t partial_sig_out[36]) {
2944 if (!ctx || !keypkg || !nonce || !msg32 || !nonce_commits || !partial_sig_out) {
2945 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
2946 }
2947 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
2948 if (n_signers == 0) {
2949 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "n_signers must be non-zero");
2950 }
2951 if (n_signers > kMaxBatchN) {
2952 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "n_signers too large");
2953 }
2954 std::size_t nc_total = 0;
2955 if (!checked_mul_size(n_signers, std::size_t{UFSECP_FROST_NONCE_COMMIT_LEN}, nc_total))
2956 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "nonce commit size overflow");
2957 try {
2958 secp256k1::FrostKeyPackage kp;
2959 std::memcpy(&kp.id, keypkg, 4);
2960 std::memcpy(&kp.threshold, keypkg + 4, 4);
2961 std::memcpy(&kp.num_participants, keypkg + 8, 4);
2962 if (kp.num_participants == 0 || kp.id == 0 || kp.id > kp.num_participants) {
2963 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "invalid key package participant metadata");
2964 }
2965 if (kp.threshold < 2 || kp.threshold > kp.num_participants) {
2966 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "invalid key package threshold");
2967 }
2968 if (n_signers > kp.num_participants) {
2969 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid signer count");
2970 }
2971 if (!scalar_parse_strict(keypkg + 12, kp.signing_share)) {
2972 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "invalid signing share in keypkg");
2973 }
2974 kp.verification_share = point_from_compressed(keypkg + 44);
2975 if (kp.verification_share.is_infinity()) {
2976 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "invalid verification share");
2977 }
2978 kp.group_public_key = point_from_compressed(keypkg + 77);
2979 if (kp.group_public_key.is_infinity()) {
2980 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "invalid group public key");
2981 }
2982 secp256k1::FrostNonce fn;
2983 Scalar h, b;
2984 if (!scalar_parse_strict(nonce, h)) {
2985 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid hiding nonce");
2986 }
2987 if (!scalar_parse_strict(nonce + 32, b)) {
2988 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid binding nonce");
2989 }
2990 fn.hiding_nonce = h;
2991 fn.binding_nonce = b;
2992 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
2993 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
2994 std::vector<secp256k1::FrostNonceCommitment> ncs(n_signers);
2995 size_t self_commitment_count = 0;
2996 ufsecp_error_t nc_err = UFSECP_OK;
2997 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_signers; ++i) {
2998 const uint8_t* nc = nonce_commits + i * UFSECP_FROST_NONCE_COMMIT_LEN;
2999 std::memcpy(&ncs[i].id, nc, 4);
3000 if (ncs[i].id == 0 || ncs[i].id > kp.num_participants) {
3001 nc_err = ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid nonce commitment signer");
3002 break;
3003 }
3004 for (size_t j = 0; j < i; ++j) {
3005 if (ncs[j].id == ncs[i].id) {
3006 nc_err = ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "duplicate nonce commitment signer");
3007 break;
3008 }
3009 }
3010 if (nc_err != UFSECP_OK) break;
3011 if (ncs[i].id == kp.id) {
3012 ++self_commitment_count;
3013 }
3014 ncs[i].hiding_point = point_from_compressed(nc + 4);
3015 if (ncs[i].hiding_point.is_infinity()) {
3016 nc_err = ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid hiding nonce point");
3017 break;
3018 }
3019 ncs[i].binding_point = point_from_compressed(nc + 37);
3020 if (ncs[i].binding_point.is_infinity()) {
3021 nc_err = ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid binding nonce point");
3022 break;
3023 }
3024 }
3025 if (nc_err == UFSECP_OK && self_commitment_count != 1) {
3026 nc_err = ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "missing signer nonce commitment");
3027 }
3028 if (nc_err != UFSECP_OK) {
3029 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&kp.signing_share, sizeof(kp.signing_share));
3030 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&fn.hiding_nonce, sizeof(fn.hiding_nonce));
3031 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&fn.binding_nonce, sizeof(fn.binding_nonce));
3032 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&h, sizeof(h));
3033 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&b, sizeof(b));
3034 return nc_err;
3035 }
3036 auto psig = secp256k1::frost_sign(kp, fn, msg_arr, ncs);
3037 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&kp.signing_share, sizeof(kp.signing_share));
3038 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&fn.hiding_nonce, sizeof(fn.hiding_nonce));
3039 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&fn.binding_nonce, sizeof(fn.binding_nonce));
3040 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&h, sizeof(h));
3041 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&b, sizeof(b));
3042 std::memcpy(partial_sig_out, &psig.id, 4);
3043 scalar_to_bytes(psig.z_i, partial_sig_out + 4);
3044 return UFSECP_OK;
3045 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
3046}
3047
3048ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_frost_verify_partial(
3049 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3050 const uint8_t partial_sig[36],
3051 const uint8_t verification_share33[33],
3052 const uint8_t* nonce_commits, size_t n_signers,
3053 const uint8_t msg32[32],
3054 const uint8_t group_pubkey33[33]) {
3055 if (!ctx || !partial_sig || !verification_share33 || !nonce_commits || !msg32 || !group_pubkey33) {
3056 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3057 }
3058 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3059 if (n_signers == 0) {
3060 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "n_signers must be non-zero");
3061 }
3062 if (n_signers > kMaxBatchN) {
3063 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "n_signers too large");
3064 }
3065 std::size_t nc_total = 0;
3066 if (!checked_mul_size(n_signers, std::size_t{UFSECP_FROST_NONCE_COMMIT_LEN}, nc_total))
3067 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "nonce commit size overflow");
3068 try {
3069 secp256k1::FrostPartialSig psig;
3070 std::memcpy(&psig.id, partial_sig, 4);
3071 if (psig.id == 0) {
3072 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "partial_sig.id must be non-zero");
3073 }
3074 Scalar z;
3075 if (!scalar_parse_strict(partial_sig + 4, z)) {
3076 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid partial sig scalar");
3077 }
3078 psig.z_i = z;
3079 auto vs = point_from_compressed(verification_share33);
3080 if (vs.is_infinity()) {
3081 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid verification share");
3082 }
3083 std::vector<secp256k1::FrostNonceCommitment> ncs(n_signers);
3084 secp256k1::FrostNonceCommitment signer_commit{};
3085 size_t signer_matches = 0;
3086 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_signers; ++i) {
3087 const uint8_t* nc = nonce_commits + i * UFSECP_FROST_NONCE_COMMIT_LEN;
3088 std::memcpy(&ncs[i].id, nc, 4);
3089 if (ncs[i].id == 0) {
3090 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "nonce commitment signer IDs must be non-zero");
3091 }
3092 for (size_t j = 0; j < i; ++j) {
3093 if (ncs[j].id == ncs[i].id) {
3094 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "duplicate nonce commitment signer IDs");
3095 }
3096 }
3097 ncs[i].hiding_point = point_from_compressed(nc + 4);
3098 if (ncs[i].hiding_point.is_infinity()) {
3099 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid hiding nonce point");
3100 }
3101 ncs[i].binding_point = point_from_compressed(nc + 37);
3102 if (ncs[i].binding_point.is_infinity()) {
3103 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid binding nonce point");
3104 }
3105 if (ncs[i].id == psig.id) {
3106 signer_commit = ncs[i];
3107 ++signer_matches;
3108 }
3109 }
3110 if (signer_matches != 1) {
3111 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT,
3112 signer_matches == 0 ? "partial_sig.id not found in nonce_commits"
3113 : "partial_sig.id must appear exactly once in nonce_commits");
3114 }
3115 auto gp = point_from_compressed(group_pubkey33);
3116 if (gp.is_infinity()) {
3117 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid group public key");
3118 }
3119 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
3120 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
3121 const bool ok = secp256k1::frost_verify_partial(psig, signer_commit, vs, msg_arr, ncs, gp);
3122 if (!ok) {
3123 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "FROST partial signature verification failed");
3124 }
3125 return UFSECP_OK;
3126 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
3127}
3128
3129ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_frost_aggregate(
3130 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3131 const uint8_t* partial_sigs, size_t n,
3132 const uint8_t* nonce_commits, size_t n_signers,
3133 const uint8_t group_pubkey33[33],
3134 const uint8_t msg32[32],
3135 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
3136 if (!ctx || !partial_sigs || !nonce_commits || !group_pubkey33 || !msg32 || !sig64_out) {
3137 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3138 }
3139 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3140 if (n == 0) {
3141 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "partial_sigs must be non-empty");
3142 }
3143 if (n_signers == 0) {
3144 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "n_signers must be non-zero");
3145 }
3146 if (n != n_signers) {
3147 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "partial/nonces signer count mismatch");
3148 }
3149 if (n > kMaxBatchN) {
3150 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "signer count too large");
3151 }
3152 std::size_t psig_total = 0, nc_total = 0;
3153 if (!checked_mul_size(n, std::size_t{36}, psig_total)
3154 || !checked_mul_size(n_signers, std::size_t{UFSECP_FROST_NONCE_COMMIT_LEN}, nc_total))
3155 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "frost aggregate size overflow");
3156 try {
3157 std::vector<secp256k1::FrostPartialSig> psigs(n);
3158 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
3159 const uint8_t* ps = partial_sigs + i * 36;
3160 std::memcpy(&psigs[i].id, ps, 4);
3161 if (psigs[i].id == 0) {
3162 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid partial sig signer");
3163 }
3164 for (size_t j = 0; j < i; ++j) {
3165 if (psigs[j].id == psigs[i].id) {
3166 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "duplicate partial sig signer");
3167 }
3168 }
3169 Scalar z;
3170 if (!scalar_parse_strict(ps + 4, z)) {
3171 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid partial sig scalar");
3172 }
3173 psigs[i].z_i = z;
3174 }
3175 std::vector<secp256k1::FrostNonceCommitment> ncs(n_signers);
3176 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_signers; ++i) {
3177 const uint8_t* nc = nonce_commits + i * UFSECP_FROST_NONCE_COMMIT_LEN;
3178 std::memcpy(&ncs[i].id, nc, 4);
3179 if (ncs[i].id == 0) {
3180 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid nonce commitment signer");
3181 }
3182 for (size_t j = 0; j < i; ++j) {
3183 if (ncs[j].id == ncs[i].id) {
3184 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "duplicate nonce commitment signer");
3185 }
3186 }
3187 ncs[i].hiding_point = point_from_compressed(nc + 4);
3188 if (ncs[i].hiding_point.is_infinity()) {
3189 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid hiding nonce point");
3190 }
3191 ncs[i].binding_point = point_from_compressed(nc + 37);
3192 if (ncs[i].binding_point.is_infinity()) {
3193 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid binding nonce point");
3194 }
3195 }
3196 for (const auto& psig : psigs) {
3197 bool found = false;
3198 for (const auto& nc : ncs) {
3199 if (nc.id == psig.id) {
3200 found = true;
3201 break;
3202 }
3203 }
3204 if (!found) {
3205 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "partial sig signer missing from nonce commitments");
3206 }
3207 }
3208 auto gp = point_from_compressed(group_pubkey33);
3209 if (gp.is_infinity()) {
3210 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "invalid group public key");
3211 }
3212 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
3213 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
3214 auto sig = secp256k1::frost_aggregate(psigs, ncs, gp, msg_arr);
3215 auto bytes = sig.to_bytes();
3216 std::memcpy(sig64_out, bytes.data(), 64);
3217 return UFSECP_OK;
3218 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
3219}
3220
3221/* ===========================================================================
3222 * Adaptor signatures
3223 * =========================================================================== */
3224
3225ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_adaptor_sign(
3226 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3227 const uint8_t privkey[32],
3228 const uint8_t msg32[32],
3229 const uint8_t adaptor_point33[33],
3230 const uint8_t aux_rand[32],
3231 uint8_t pre_sig_out[UFSECP_SCHNORR_ADAPTOR_SIG_LEN]) {
3232 if (!ctx || !privkey || !msg32 || !adaptor_point33 || !aux_rand || !pre_sig_out) {
3233 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3234 }
3235 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3236 Scalar sk;
3237 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
3238 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
3239 }
3240 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr, aux_arr;
3241 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
3242 std::memcpy(aux_arr.data(), aux_rand, 32);
3243 auto ap = point_from_compressed(adaptor_point33);
3244 if (ap.is_infinity()) {
3245 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid adaptor point");
3246 }
3247 auto pre = secp256k1::schnorr_adaptor_sign(sk, msg_arr, ap, aux_arr);
3248 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
3249 auto rhat = pre.R_hat.to_compressed();
3250 auto shat = pre.s_hat.to_bytes();
3251 std::memcpy(pre_sig_out, rhat.data(), 33);
3252 std::memcpy(pre_sig_out + 33, shat.data(), 32);
3253 /* Serialize needs_negation as a 32-byte flag for completeness */
3254 std::memset(pre_sig_out + 65, 0, 32);
3255 pre_sig_out[65] = pre.needs_negation ? 1 : 0;
3256 return UFSECP_OK;
3257}
3258
3259ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_adaptor_verify(
3260 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3261 const uint8_t pre_sig[UFSECP_SCHNORR_ADAPTOR_SIG_LEN],
3262 const uint8_t pubkey_x[32],
3263 const uint8_t msg32[32],
3264 const uint8_t adaptor_point33[33]) {
3265 if (!ctx || !pre_sig || !pubkey_x || !msg32 || !adaptor_point33) {
3266 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3267 }
3268 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3269 secp256k1::SchnorrAdaptorSig as;
3270 as.R_hat = point_from_compressed(pre_sig);
3271 if (as.R_hat.is_infinity()) {
3272 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor R_hat");
3273 }
3274 Scalar shat;
3275 if (!scalar_parse_strict(pre_sig + 33, shat)) {
3276 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor sig scalar");
3277 }
3278 as.s_hat = shat;
3279 as.needs_negation = (pre_sig[65] != 0);
3280 // Strict: reject x-only pubkey >= p at ABI gate
3281 FE pk_fe;
3282 if (!FE::parse_bytes_strict(pubkey_x, pk_fe)) {
3283 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "non-canonical pubkey (x>=p)");
3284 }
3285 std::array<uint8_t, 32> pk_arr, msg_arr;
3286 std::memcpy(pk_arr.data(), pubkey_x, 32);
3287 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
3288 auto ap = point_from_compressed(adaptor_point33);
3289 if (ap.is_infinity()) {
3290 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid adaptor point");
3291 }
3292 if (!secp256k1::schnorr_adaptor_verify(as, pk_arr, msg_arr, ap)) {
3293 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "adaptor verify failed");
3294 }
3295 return UFSECP_OK;
3296}
3297
3298ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_adaptor_adapt(
3299 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3300 const uint8_t pre_sig[UFSECP_SCHNORR_ADAPTOR_SIG_LEN],
3301 const uint8_t adaptor_secret[32],
3302 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
3303 if (!ctx || !pre_sig || !adaptor_secret || !sig64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3304 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3305 secp256k1::SchnorrAdaptorSig as;
3306 as.R_hat = point_from_compressed(pre_sig);
3307 if (as.R_hat.is_infinity()) {
3308 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor R_hat");
3309 }
3310 Scalar shat;
3311 if (!scalar_parse_strict(pre_sig + 33, shat)) {
3312 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor sig scalar");
3313 }
3314 as.s_hat = shat;
3315 as.needs_negation = (pre_sig[65] != 0);
3316 Scalar secret;
3317 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(adaptor_secret, secret)) {
3318 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "adaptor secret is zero or >= n");
3319 }
3320 auto sig = secp256k1::schnorr_adaptor_adapt(as, secret);
3321 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&secret, sizeof(secret));
3322 auto bytes = sig.to_bytes();
3323 std::memcpy(sig64_out, bytes.data(), 64);
3324 return UFSECP_OK;
3325}
3326
3327ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_adaptor_extract(
3328 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3329 const uint8_t pre_sig[UFSECP_SCHNORR_ADAPTOR_SIG_LEN],
3330 const uint8_t sig64[64],
3331 uint8_t secret32_out[32]) {
3332 if (!ctx || !pre_sig || !sig64 || !secret32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3333 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3334 secp256k1::SchnorrAdaptorSig as;
3335 as.R_hat = point_from_compressed(pre_sig);
3336 if (as.R_hat.is_infinity()) {
3337 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor R_hat");
3338 }
3339 Scalar shat;
3340 if (!scalar_parse_strict(pre_sig + 33, shat)) {
3341 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor sig scalar");
3342 }
3343 as.s_hat = shat;
3344 as.needs_negation = (pre_sig[65] != 0);
3345 secp256k1::SchnorrSignature sig;
3346 if (!secp256k1::SchnorrSignature::parse_strict(sig64, sig)) {
3347 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid schnorr signature");
3348 }
3349 auto [secret, ok] = secp256k1::schnorr_adaptor_extract(as, sig);
3350 if (!ok) {
3351 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "adaptor extract failed");
3352 }
3353 scalar_to_bytes(secret, secret32_out);
3354 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&secret, sizeof(secret));
3355 return UFSECP_OK;
3356}
3357
3358ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_adaptor_sign(
3359 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3360 const uint8_t privkey[32],
3361 const uint8_t msg32[32],
3362 const uint8_t adaptor_point33[33],
3363 uint8_t pre_sig_out[UFSECP_ECDSA_ADAPTOR_SIG_LEN]) {
3364 if (!ctx || !privkey || !msg32 || !adaptor_point33 || !pre_sig_out) {
3365 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3366 }
3367 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3368 Scalar sk;
3369 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
3370 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
3371 }
3372 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
3373 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
3374 auto ap = point_from_compressed(adaptor_point33);
3375 if (ap.is_infinity()) {
3376 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid adaptor point");
3377 }
3378 auto pre = secp256k1::ecdsa_adaptor_sign(sk, msg_arr, ap);
3379 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
3380 auto rhat = pre.R_hat.to_compressed();
3381 auto shat = pre.s_hat.to_bytes();
3382 auto r_bytes = pre.r.to_bytes();
3383 std::memcpy(pre_sig_out, rhat.data(), 33);
3384 std::memcpy(pre_sig_out + 33, shat.data(), 32);
3385 std::memcpy(pre_sig_out + 65, r_bytes.data(), 32);
3386 /* zero-pad remainder */
3387 std::memset(pre_sig_out + 97, 0, UFSECP_ECDSA_ADAPTOR_SIG_LEN - 97);
3388 return UFSECP_OK;
3389}
3390
3391ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_adaptor_verify(
3392 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3393 const uint8_t pre_sig[UFSECP_ECDSA_ADAPTOR_SIG_LEN],
3394 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
3395 const uint8_t msg32[32],
3396 const uint8_t adaptor_point33[33]) {
3397 if (!ctx || !pre_sig || !pubkey33 || !msg32 || !adaptor_point33) {
3398 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3399 }
3400 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3401 secp256k1::ECDSAAdaptorSig as;
3402 as.R_hat = point_from_compressed(pre_sig);
3403 if (as.R_hat.is_infinity()) {
3404 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor R_hat");
3405 }
3406 Scalar shat;
3407 if (!scalar_parse_strict(pre_sig + 33, shat)) {
3408 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor sig scalar");
3409 }
3410 as.s_hat = shat;
3411 if (!scalar_parse_strict(pre_sig + 65, as.r)) {
3412 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor sig r");
3413 }
3414 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
3415 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
3416 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
3417 }
3418 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
3419 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
3420 auto ap = point_from_compressed(adaptor_point33);
3421 if (ap.is_infinity()) {
3422 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid adaptor point");
3423 }
3424 if (!secp256k1::ecdsa_adaptor_verify(as, pk, msg_arr, ap)) {
3425 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "ECDSA adaptor verify failed");
3426 }
3427 return UFSECP_OK;
3428}
3429
3430ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_adaptor_adapt(
3431 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3432 const uint8_t pre_sig[UFSECP_ECDSA_ADAPTOR_SIG_LEN],
3433 const uint8_t adaptor_secret[32],
3434 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
3435 if (!ctx || !pre_sig || !adaptor_secret || !sig64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3436 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3437 secp256k1::ECDSAAdaptorSig as;
3438 as.R_hat = point_from_compressed(pre_sig);
3439 if (as.R_hat.is_infinity()) {
3440 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor R_hat");
3441 }
3442 Scalar shat;
3443 if (!scalar_parse_strict(pre_sig + 33, shat)) {
3444 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor sig scalar");
3445 }
3446 as.s_hat = shat;
3447 if (!scalar_parse_strict(pre_sig + 65, as.r)) {
3448 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor sig r");
3449 }
3450 Scalar secret;
3451 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(adaptor_secret, secret)) {
3452 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "adaptor secret is zero or >= n");
3453 }
3454 auto sig = secp256k1::ecdsa_adaptor_adapt(as, secret);
3455 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&secret, sizeof(secret));
3456 auto compact = sig.to_compact();
3457 std::memcpy(sig64_out, compact.data(), 64);
3458 return UFSECP_OK;
3459}
3460
3461ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecdsa_adaptor_extract(
3462 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3463 const uint8_t pre_sig[UFSECP_ECDSA_ADAPTOR_SIG_LEN],
3464 const uint8_t sig64[64],
3465 uint8_t secret32_out[32]) {
3466 if (!ctx || !pre_sig || !sig64 || !secret32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3467 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3468 secp256k1::ECDSAAdaptorSig as;
3469 as.R_hat = point_from_compressed(pre_sig);
3470 if (as.R_hat.is_infinity()) {
3471 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor R_hat");
3472 }
3473 Scalar shat;
3474 if (!scalar_parse_strict(pre_sig + 33, shat)) {
3475 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor sig scalar");
3476 }
3477 as.s_hat = shat;
3478 if (!scalar_parse_strict(pre_sig + 65, as.r)) {
3479 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid adaptor sig r");
3480 }
3481 std::array<uint8_t, 64> compact;
3482 std::memcpy(compact.data(), sig64, 64);
3483 secp256k1::ECDSASignature ecdsasig;
3484 if (!secp256k1::ECDSASignature::parse_compact_strict(compact, ecdsasig)) {
3485 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid ECDSA sig");
3486 }
3487 auto [secret, ok] = secp256k1::ecdsa_adaptor_extract(as, ecdsasig);
3488 if (!ok) {
3489 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "ECDSA adaptor extract failed");
3490 }
3491 scalar_to_bytes(secret, secret32_out);
3492 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&secret, sizeof(secret));
3493 return UFSECP_OK;
3494}
3495
3496/* ===========================================================================
3497 * Pedersen commitments
3498 * =========================================================================== */
3499
3500ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pedersen_commit(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3501 const uint8_t value[32],
3502 const uint8_t blinding[32],
3503 uint8_t commitment33_out[33]) {
3504 if (!ctx || !value || !blinding || !commitment33_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3505 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3506 Scalar v, b;
3507 if (!scalar_parse_strict(value, v)) {
3508 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "value >= n");
3509 }
3510 if (!scalar_parse_strict(blinding, b)) {
3511 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "blinding >= n");
3512 }
3513 auto c = secp256k1::pedersen_commit(v, b);
3514 auto comp = c.point.to_compressed();
3515 std::memcpy(commitment33_out, comp.data(), 33);
3516 return UFSECP_OK;
3517}
3518
3519ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pedersen_verify(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3520 const uint8_t commitment33[33],
3521 const uint8_t value[32],
3522 const uint8_t blinding[32]) {
3523 if (!ctx || !commitment33 || !value || !blinding) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3524 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3525 Scalar v, b;
3526 if (!scalar_parse_strict(value, v)) {
3527 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "value >= n");
3528 }
3529 if (!scalar_parse_strict(blinding, b)) {
3530 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "blinding >= n");
3531 }
3532 auto commit_pt = point_from_compressed(commitment33);
3533 if (commit_pt.is_infinity()) {
3534 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid commitment point");
3535 }
3536 if (!secp256k1::pedersen_verify(secp256k1::PedersenCommitment{commit_pt}, v, b)) {
3537 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "Pedersen verify failed");
3538 }
3539 return UFSECP_OK;
3540}
3541
3542ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pedersen_verify_sum(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3543 const uint8_t* pos, size_t n_pos,
3544 const uint8_t* neg, size_t n_neg) {
3545 if (!ctx || (!pos && n_pos > 0) || (!neg && n_neg > 0)) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3546 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3547 if (n_pos > kMaxBatchN || n_neg > kMaxBatchN)
3548 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "commitment count too large");
3549 std::size_t pos_bytes = 0, neg_bytes = 0;
3550 if (!checked_mul_size(n_pos, std::size_t{33}, pos_bytes)
3551 || !checked_mul_size(n_neg, std::size_t{33}, neg_bytes))
3552 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "commitment array size overflow");
3553 try {
3554 std::vector<secp256k1::PedersenCommitment> pcs(n_pos), ncs(n_neg);
3555 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_pos; ++i) {
3556 auto p = point_from_compressed(pos + i * 33);
3557 if (p.is_infinity()) {
3558 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid positive commitment");
3559 }
3560 pcs[i] = secp256k1::PedersenCommitment{p};
3561 }
3562 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_neg; ++i) {
3563 auto p = point_from_compressed(neg + i * 33);
3564 if (p.is_infinity()) {
3565 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid negative commitment");
3566 }
3567 ncs[i] = secp256k1::PedersenCommitment{p};
3568 }
3569 if (!secp256k1::pedersen_verify_sum(pcs.data(), n_pos, ncs.data(), n_neg)) {
3570 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "Pedersen sum verify failed");
3571 }
3572 return UFSECP_OK;
3573 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
3574}
3575
3576ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pedersen_blind_sum(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3577 const uint8_t* blinds_in, size_t n_in,
3578 const uint8_t* blinds_out, size_t n_out,
3579 uint8_t sum32_out[32]) {
3580 if (!ctx || (!blinds_in && n_in > 0) || (!blinds_out && n_out > 0) || !sum32_out) {
3581 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3582 }
3583 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3584 if (n_in > kMaxBatchN || n_out > kMaxBatchN)
3585 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "blind count too large");
3586 std::size_t in_bytes = 0, out_bytes = 0;
3587 if (!checked_mul_size(n_in, std::size_t{32}, in_bytes)
3588 || !checked_mul_size(n_out, std::size_t{32}, out_bytes))
3589 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "blind array size overflow");
3590 try {
3591 std::vector<Scalar> ins(n_in), outs(n_out);
3592 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_in; ++i) {
3593 if (!scalar_parse_strict(blinds_in + i * 32, ins[i])) {
3594 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid input blind");
3595 }
3596 }
3597 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_out; ++i) {
3598 if (!scalar_parse_strict(blinds_out + i * 32, outs[i])) {
3599 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid output blind");
3600 }
3601 }
3602 auto sum = secp256k1::pedersen_blind_sum(ins.data(), n_in, outs.data(), n_out);
3603 scalar_to_bytes(sum, sum32_out);
3604 return UFSECP_OK;
3605 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
3606}
3607
3608ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_pedersen_switch_commit(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3609 const uint8_t value[32],
3610 const uint8_t blinding[32],
3611 const uint8_t switch_blind[32],
3612 uint8_t commitment33_out[33]) {
3613 if (!ctx || !value || !blinding || !switch_blind || !commitment33_out) {
3614 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3615 }
3616 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3617 Scalar v, b, sb;
3618 if (!scalar_parse_strict(value, v)) {
3619 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "value >= n");
3620 }
3621 if (!scalar_parse_strict(blinding, b)) {
3622 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "blinding >= n");
3623 }
3624 if (!scalar_parse_strict(switch_blind, sb)) {
3625 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "switch_blind >= n");
3626 }
3627 auto c = secp256k1::pedersen_switch_commit(v, b, sb);
3628 auto comp = c.point.to_compressed();
3629 std::memcpy(commitment33_out, comp.data(), 33);
3630 return UFSECP_OK;
3631}
3632
3633/* ===========================================================================
3634 * Zero-knowledge proofs
3635 * =========================================================================== */
3636
3637ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_zk_knowledge_prove(
3638 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3639 const uint8_t secret[32],
3640 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
3641 const uint8_t msg32[32],
3642 const uint8_t aux_rand[32],
3643 uint8_t proof_out[UFSECP_ZK_KNOWLEDGE_PROOF_LEN]) {
3644 if (!ctx || !secret || !pubkey33 || !msg32 || !aux_rand || !proof_out) {
3645 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3646 }
3647 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3648 Scalar s;
3649 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(secret, s)) {
3650 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "secret is zero or >= n");
3651 }
3652 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
3653 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
3654 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
3655 }
3656 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr, aux_arr;
3657 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
3658 std::memcpy(aux_arr.data(), aux_rand, 32);
3659 auto proof = secp256k1::zk::knowledge_prove(s, pk, msg_arr, aux_arr);
3660 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&s, sizeof(s));
3661 auto ser = proof.serialize();
3662 std::memcpy(proof_out, ser.data(), UFSECP_ZK_KNOWLEDGE_PROOF_LEN);
3663 return UFSECP_OK;
3664}
3665
3666ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_zk_knowledge_verify(
3667 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3668 const uint8_t proof[UFSECP_ZK_KNOWLEDGE_PROOF_LEN],
3669 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
3670 const uint8_t msg32[32]) {
3671 if (!ctx || !proof || !pubkey33 || !msg32) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3672 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3673 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
3674 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
3675 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
3676 }
3677 secp256k1::zk::KnowledgeProof kp;
3678 if (!secp256k1::zk::KnowledgeProof::deserialize(proof, kp)) {
3679 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid knowledge proof");
3680 }
3681 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
3682 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg32, 32);
3683 if (!secp256k1::zk::knowledge_verify(kp, pk, msg_arr)) {
3684 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "knowledge proof failed");
3685 }
3686 return UFSECP_OK;
3687}
3688
3689ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_zk_dleq_prove(
3690 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3691 const uint8_t secret[32],
3692 const uint8_t G33[33], const uint8_t H33[33],
3693 const uint8_t P33[33], const uint8_t Q33[33],
3694 const uint8_t aux_rand[32],
3695 uint8_t proof_out[UFSECP_ZK_DLEQ_PROOF_LEN]) {
3696 if (!ctx || !secret || !G33 || !H33 || !P33 || !Q33 || !aux_rand || !proof_out) {
3697 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3698 }
3699 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3700 Scalar s;
3701 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(secret, s)) {
3702 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "secret is zero or >= n");
3703 }
3704 auto G = point_from_compressed(G33);
3705 auto H = point_from_compressed(H33);
3706 auto P = point_from_compressed(P33);
3707 auto Q = point_from_compressed(Q33);
3708 if (G.is_infinity() || H.is_infinity() || P.is_infinity() || Q.is_infinity()) {
3709 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid DLEQ point");
3710 }
3711 std::array<uint8_t, 32> aux_arr;
3712 std::memcpy(aux_arr.data(), aux_rand, 32);
3713 auto proof = secp256k1::zk::dleq_prove(s, G, H, P, Q, aux_arr);
3714 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&s, sizeof(s));
3715 auto ser = proof.serialize();
3716 std::memcpy(proof_out, ser.data(), UFSECP_ZK_DLEQ_PROOF_LEN);
3717 return UFSECP_OK;
3718}
3719
3720ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_zk_dleq_verify(
3721 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3722 const uint8_t proof[UFSECP_ZK_DLEQ_PROOF_LEN],
3723 const uint8_t G33[33], const uint8_t H33[33],
3724 const uint8_t P33[33], const uint8_t Q33[33]) {
3725 if (!ctx || !proof || !G33 || !H33 || !P33 || !Q33) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3726 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3727 auto G = point_from_compressed(G33);
3728 auto H = point_from_compressed(H33);
3729 auto P = point_from_compressed(P33);
3730 auto Q = point_from_compressed(Q33);
3731 if (G.is_infinity() || H.is_infinity() || P.is_infinity() || Q.is_infinity()) {
3732 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid DLEQ point");
3733 }
3734 secp256k1::zk::DLEQProof dp;
3735 if (!secp256k1::zk::DLEQProof::deserialize(proof, dp)) {
3736 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid DLEQ proof");
3737 }
3738 if (!secp256k1::zk::dleq_verify(dp, G, H, P, Q)) {
3739 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "DLEQ proof failed");
3740 }
3741 return UFSECP_OK;
3742}
3743
3744ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_zk_range_prove(
3745 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3746 uint64_t value,
3747 const uint8_t blinding[32],
3748 const uint8_t commitment33[33],
3749 const uint8_t aux_rand[32],
3750 uint8_t* proof_out, size_t* proof_len) {
3751 if (!ctx || !blinding || !commitment33 || !aux_rand || !proof_out || !proof_len) {
3752 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3753 }
3754 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3755 Scalar b;
3756 if (!scalar_parse_strict(blinding, b)) {
3757 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "blinding >= n");
3758 }
3759 auto commit_pt = point_from_compressed(commitment33);
3760 if (commit_pt.is_infinity()) {
3761 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid commitment point");
3762 }
3763 auto commit = secp256k1::PedersenCommitment{commit_pt};
3764 std::array<uint8_t, 32> aux_arr;
3765 std::memcpy(aux_arr.data(), aux_rand, 32);
3766 auto rp = secp256k1::zk::range_prove(value, b, commit, aux_arr);
3767 /* Serialize range proof: A(33)+S(33)+T1(33)+T2(33)+tau_x(32)+mu(32)+t_hat(32)+L[6]*33+R[6]*33+a(32)+b(32) */
3768 const size_t needed = 33*4 + 32*3 + 6*33 + 6*33 + 32*2;
3769 if (*proof_len < needed) {
3770 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "range proof buffer too small");
3771 }
3772 size_t off = 0;
3773 auto write_point = [&](const Point& p) {
3774 auto c = p.to_compressed();
3775 std::memcpy(proof_out + off, c.data(), 33);
3776 off += 33;
3777 };
3778 auto write_scalar = [&](const Scalar& s) {
3779 scalar_to_bytes(s, proof_out + off);
3780 off += 32;
3781 };
3782 write_point(rp.A); write_point(rp.S);
3783 write_point(rp.T1); write_point(rp.T2);
3784 write_scalar(rp.tau_x); write_scalar(rp.mu); write_scalar(rp.t_hat);
3785 for (int i = 0; i < 6; ++i) write_point(rp.L[i]);
3786 for (int i = 0; i < 6; ++i) write_point(rp.R[i]);
3787 write_scalar(rp.a); write_scalar(rp.b);
3788 *proof_len = off;
3789 return UFSECP_OK;
3790}
3791
3792ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_zk_range_verify(
3793 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3794 const uint8_t commitment33[33],
3795 const uint8_t* proof, size_t proof_len) {
3796 if (!ctx || !commitment33 || !proof) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3797 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3798 /* Deserialize range proof */
3799 const size_t expected = 33*4 + 32*3 + 6*33 + 6*33 + 32*2;
3800 if (proof_len < expected) {
3801 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "range proof too short");
3802 }
3803 if (proof_len != expected) {
3804 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "range proof length mismatch");
3805 }
3806 secp256k1::zk::RangeProof rp;
3807 size_t off = 0;
3808 bool point_ok = true;
3809 auto read_point = [&]() -> Point {
3810 auto p = point_from_compressed(proof + off);
3811 if (p.is_infinity()) point_ok = false;
3812 off += 33;
3813 return p;
3814 };
3815 bool scalar_ok = true;
3816 auto read_scalar = [&]() -> Scalar {
3817 Scalar s;
3818 if (!scalar_parse_strict(proof + off, s)) {
3819 scalar_ok = false;
3820 }
3821 off += 32;
3822 return s;
3823 };
3824 rp.A = read_point(); rp.S = read_point();
3825 rp.T1 = read_point(); rp.T2 = read_point();
3826 rp.tau_x = read_scalar(); rp.mu = read_scalar(); rp.t_hat = read_scalar();
3827 for (int i = 0; i < 6; ++i) rp.L[i] = read_point();
3828 for (int i = 0; i < 6; ++i) rp.R[i] = read_point();
3829 rp.a = read_scalar(); rp.b = read_scalar();
3830 if (!point_ok) {
3831 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid point in range proof");
3832 }
3833 if (!scalar_ok) {
3834 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid scalar in range proof");
3835 }
3836 auto commit_pt = point_from_compressed(commitment33);
3837 if (commit_pt.is_infinity()) {
3838 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid commitment point");
3839 }
3840 auto commit = secp256k1::PedersenCommitment{commit_pt};
3841 if (!secp256k1::zk::range_verify(commit, rp)) {
3842 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "range proof failed");
3843 }
3844 return UFSECP_OK;
3845}
3846
3847/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
3848 * ECDSA foreign-field SNARK witness (eprint 2025/695)
3849 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
3850
3851ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_zk_ecdsa_snark_witness(
3852 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3853 const uint8_t msg_hash32[32],
3854 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
3855 const uint8_t sig64[64],
3856 ufsecp_ecdsa_snark_witness_t* out)
3857{
3858 if (!ctx || !msg_hash32 || !pubkey33 || !sig64 || !out)
3859 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3860 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3861
3862 /* Parse public key from compressed 33-byte encoding */
3863 auto pubkey = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
3864 if (pubkey.is_infinity())
3865 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid pubkey");
3866
3867 /* Parse r and s from compact 64-byte signature */
3868 std::array<uint8_t, 32> r_bytes{};
3869 std::array<uint8_t, 32> s_bytes{};
3870 std::memcpy(r_bytes.data(), sig64, 32);
3871 std::memcpy(s_bytes.data(), sig64 + 32, 32);
3872
3873 Scalar sig_r, sig_s;
3874 if (!scalar_parse_strict(r_bytes.data(), sig_r) || sig_r.is_zero())
3875 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid sig r");
3876 if (!scalar_parse_strict(s_bytes.data(), sig_s) || sig_s.is_zero())
3877 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid sig s");
3878
3879 /* Compute witness via C++ layer */
3880 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr{};
3881 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg_hash32, 32);
3882 auto w = secp256k1::zk::ecdsa_snark_witness(msg_arr, pubkey, sig_r, sig_s);
3883
3884 /* ── public inputs ──────────────────────────────────────────────── */
3885 std::memcpy(out->msg, msg_hash32, 32);
3886 std::memcpy(out->sig_r, r_bytes.data(), 32);
3887 std::memcpy(out->sig_s, s_bytes.data(), 32);
3888
3889 auto px_bytes = pubkey.x().to_bytes();
3890 auto py_bytes = pubkey.y().to_bytes();
3891 std::memcpy(out->pub_x, px_bytes.data(), 32);
3892 std::memcpy(out->pub_y, py_bytes.data(), 32);
3893
3894 /* ── private witness bytes ──────────────────────────────────────── */
3895 std::memcpy(out->s_inv, w.bytes_s_inv.data(), 32);
3896 std::memcpy(out->u1, w.bytes_u1.data(), 32);
3897 std::memcpy(out->u2, w.bytes_u2.data(), 32);
3898 std::memcpy(out->result_x, w.bytes_result_x.data(), 32);
3899 std::memcpy(out->result_y, w.bytes_result_y.data(), 32);
3900 std::memcpy(out->result_x_mod_n, w.bytes_result_x_mod_n.data(), 32);
3901
3902 /* ── 5×52-bit limb decompositions ───────────────────────────────── */
3903 static_assert(sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t) == sizeof(secp256k1::zk::ForeignFieldLimbs),
3904 "limb struct size mismatch");
3905 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_sig_r, &w.sig_r, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3906 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_sig_s, &w.sig_s, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3907 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_pub_x, &w.pub_x, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3908 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_pub_y, &w.pub_y, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3909 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_s_inv, &w.s_inv, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3910 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_u1, &w.u1, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3911 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_u2, &w.u2, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3912 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_result_x, &w.result_x, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3913 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_result_y, &w.result_y, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3914 std::memcpy(&out->lmb_result_x_mod_n, &w.result_x_mod_n, sizeof(ufsecp_ff_limbs_t));
3915
3916 out->valid = w.valid ? 1 : 0;
3917 return UFSECP_OK;
3918}
3919
3920/* ===========================================================================
3921 * Multi-coin wallet infrastructure
3922 * =========================================================================== */
3923
3924static const secp256k1::coins::CoinParams* find_coin(uint32_t coin_type) {
3925 return secp256k1::coins::find_by_coin_type(coin_type);
3926}
3927
3928ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_coin_address(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3929 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
3930 uint32_t coin_type, int testnet,
3931 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
3932 if (!ctx || !pubkey33 || !addr_out || !addr_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3933 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3934 auto coin = find_coin(coin_type);
3935 if (!coin) {
3936 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "unknown coin type");
3937 }
3938 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
3939 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
3940 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
3941 }
3942 try {
3943 auto addr = secp256k1::coins::coin_address(pk, *coin, testnet != 0);
3944 if (addr.empty()) {
3945 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "address generation failed");
3946 }
3947 if (*addr_len < addr.size() + 1) {
3948 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "address buffer too small");
3949 }
3950 std::memcpy(addr_out, addr.c_str(), addr.size() + 1);
3951 *addr_len = addr.size();
3952 return UFSECP_OK;
3953 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
3954}
3955
3956ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_coin_derive_from_seed(
3957 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
3958 const uint8_t* seed, size_t seed_len,
3959 uint32_t coin_type, uint32_t account, int change, uint32_t index,
3960 int testnet,
3961 uint8_t* privkey32_out,
3962 uint8_t* pubkey33_out,
3963 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
3964 if (!ctx || !seed) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
3965 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
3966 if ((addr_out == nullptr) != (addr_len == nullptr)) {
3967 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG,
3968 "addr_out and addr_len must both be null or both be non-null");
3969 }
3970 if (seed_len < 16 || seed_len > 64) {
3971 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "seed must be 16-64 bytes");
3972 }
3973 auto coin = find_coin(coin_type);
3974 if (!coin) {
3975 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "unknown coin type");
3976 }
3977 try {
3978 /* BIP-32 master */
3979 auto bip32_result = secp256k1::bip32_master_key(seed, seed_len);
3980 if (!bip32_result.second) {
3981 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "BIP-32 master key failed");
3982 }
3983 auto master = bip32_result.first;
3984 const auto cleanup_keys = [&]() {
3985 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(master.key.data(), master.key.size());
3986 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(master.chain_code.data(), master.chain_code.size());
3987 };
3988 /* Derive coin key */
3989 auto derived = secp256k1::coins::coin_derive_key(
3990 master, *coin, account, change != 0, index);
3991 auto key = derived.first;
3992 bool const d_ok = derived.second;
3993 if (!d_ok) {
3994 cleanup_keys();
3995 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "coin key derivation failed");
3996 }
3997 const auto cleanup_derived_key = [&]() {
3998 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(key.key.data(), key.key.size());
3999 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(key.chain_code.data(), key.chain_code.size());
4000 };
4001 if (privkey32_out) {
4002 auto sk = key.private_key();
4003 scalar_to_bytes(sk, privkey32_out);
4004 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4005 }
4006 auto pk = key.public_key();
4007 cleanup_keys();
4008 cleanup_derived_key();
4009 if (pubkey33_out) {
4010 point_to_compressed(pk, pubkey33_out);
4011 }
4012 if (addr_out && addr_len) {
4013 auto addr = secp256k1::coins::coin_address(pk, *coin, testnet != 0);
4014 if (*addr_len < addr.size() + 1) {
4015 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "address buffer too small");
4016 }
4017 std::memcpy(addr_out, addr.c_str(), addr.size() + 1);
4018 *addr_len = addr.size();
4019 }
4020 return UFSECP_OK;
4021 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4022}
4023
4024ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_coin_wif_encode(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4025 const uint8_t privkey[32],
4026 uint32_t coin_type, int testnet,
4027 char* wif_out, size_t* wif_len) {
4028 if (!ctx || !privkey || !wif_out || !wif_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4029 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4030 auto coin = find_coin(coin_type);
4031 if (!coin) {
4032 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "unknown coin type");
4033 }
4034 Scalar sk;
4035 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
4036 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
4037 }
4038 try {
4039 auto wif = secp256k1::coins::coin_wif_encode(sk, *coin, true, testnet != 0);
4040 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4041 if (wif.empty()) {
4042 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "WIF encode failed");
4043 }
4044 if (*wif_len < wif.size() + 1) {
4045 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "WIF buffer too small");
4046 }
4047 std::memcpy(wif_out, wif.c_str(), wif.size() + 1);
4048 *wif_len = wif.size();
4049 return UFSECP_OK;
4050 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4051}
4052
4053ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_btc_message_sign(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4054 const uint8_t* msg, size_t msg_len,
4055 const uint8_t privkey[32],
4056 char* base64_out, size_t* base64_len) {
4057 if (!ctx || !msg || !privkey || !base64_out || !base64_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4058 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4059 Scalar sk;
4060 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
4061 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
4062 }
4063 try {
4064 auto rsig = secp256k1::coins::bitcoin_sign_message(msg, msg_len, sk);
4065 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4066 auto b64 = secp256k1::coins::bitcoin_sig_to_base64(rsig);
4067 if (*base64_len < b64.size() + 1) {
4068 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "base64 buffer too small");
4069 }
4070 std::memcpy(base64_out, b64.c_str(), b64.size() + 1);
4071 *base64_len = b64.size();
4072 return UFSECP_OK;
4073 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4074}
4075
4076ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_btc_message_verify(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4077 const uint8_t* msg, size_t msg_len,
4078 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
4079 const char* base64_sig) {
4080 if (!ctx || !msg || !pubkey33 || !base64_sig) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4081 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4082 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
4083 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
4084 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
4085 }
4086 try {
4087 auto dec = secp256k1::coins::bitcoin_sig_from_base64(std::string(base64_sig));
4088 if (!dec.valid) {
4089 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "invalid base64 signature");
4090 }
4091 if (!secp256k1::coins::bitcoin_verify_message(msg, msg_len, pk, dec.sig)) {
4092 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "BTC message verify failed");
4093 }
4094 return UFSECP_OK;
4095 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4096}
4097
4098ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_btc_message_hash(const uint8_t* msg, size_t msg_len,
4099 uint8_t digest32_out[32]) {
4100 if (!msg || !digest32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4101 auto h = secp256k1::coins::bitcoin_message_hash(msg, msg_len);
4102 std::memcpy(digest32_out, h.data(), 32);
4103 return UFSECP_OK;
4104}
4105
4106/* ===========================================================================
4107 * BIP-352 Silent Payments
4108 * =========================================================================== */
4109
4110ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_silent_payment_address(
4111 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4112 const uint8_t scan_privkey[32],
4113 const uint8_t spend_privkey[32],
4114 uint8_t scan_pubkey33_out[33],
4115 uint8_t spend_pubkey33_out[33],
4116 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
4117 if (!ctx || !scan_privkey || !spend_privkey || !scan_pubkey33_out ||
4118 !spend_pubkey33_out || !addr_out || !addr_len) {
4119 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4120 }
4121 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4122
4123 Scalar scan_sk, spend_sk;
4124 auto cleanup = [&]() {
4125 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&scan_sk, sizeof(scan_sk));
4126 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&spend_sk, sizeof(spend_sk));
4127 };
4128 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(scan_privkey, scan_sk)) {
4129 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "scan privkey is zero or >= n");
4130 }
4131 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(spend_privkey, spend_sk)) {
4132 cleanup();
4133 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "spend privkey is zero or >= n");
4134 }
4135
4136 auto spa = secp256k1::silent_payment_address(scan_sk, spend_sk);
4137 auto scan_comp = spa.scan_pubkey.to_compressed();
4138 auto spend_comp = spa.spend_pubkey.to_compressed();
4139 std::memcpy(scan_pubkey33_out, scan_comp.data(), 33);
4140 std::memcpy(spend_pubkey33_out, spend_comp.data(), 33);
4141
4142 auto addr_str = spa.encode();
4143 if (addr_str.size() >= *addr_len) {
4144 cleanup();
4145 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "address buffer too small");
4146 }
4147 std::memcpy(addr_out, addr_str.c_str(), addr_str.size() + 1);
4148 *addr_len = addr_str.size();
4149
4150 cleanup();
