4151 return UFSECP_OK;
4152}
4153
4154ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_silent_payment_create_output(
4155 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4156 const uint8_t* input_privkeys, size_t n_inputs,
4157 const uint8_t scan_pubkey33[33],
4158 const uint8_t spend_pubkey33[33],
4159 uint32_t k,
4160 uint8_t output_pubkey33_out[33],
4161 uint8_t* tweak32_out) {
4162 if (!ctx || !input_privkeys || n_inputs == 0 || !scan_pubkey33 ||
4163 !spend_pubkey33 || !output_pubkey33_out) {
4164 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4165 }
4166 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4167 if (n_inputs > kMaxBatchN) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "input count too large");
4168 std::size_t total = 0;
4169 if (!checked_mul_size(n_inputs, std::size_t{32}, total))
4170 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "input privkey array size overflow");
4171 try {
4172
4173 // Parse input private keys
4174 std::vector<Scalar> privkeys;
4175 auto cleanup_privkeys = [&]() {
4176 for (auto& sk : privkeys) {
4177 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4178 }
4179 };
4180 privkeys.reserve(n_inputs);
4181 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_inputs; ++i) {
4182 Scalar sk;
4183 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(input_privkeys + i * 32, sk)) {
4184 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4185 cleanup_privkeys();
4186 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "input privkey is zero or >= n");
4187 }
4188 privkeys.push_back(sk);
4189 }
4190
4191 // Parse recipient address
4192 secp256k1::SilentPaymentAddress recipient;
4193 recipient.scan_pubkey = point_from_compressed(scan_pubkey33);
4194 recipient.spend_pubkey = point_from_compressed(spend_pubkey33);
4195 if (recipient.scan_pubkey.is_infinity() || recipient.spend_pubkey.is_infinity()) {
4196 cleanup_privkeys();
4197 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid recipient pubkey");
4198 }
4199
4200 auto [output_point, tweak] = secp256k1::silent_payment_create_output(privkeys, recipient, k);
4201 if (output_point.is_infinity()) {
4202 cleanup_privkeys();
4203 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_ARITH, "output point is infinity");
4204 }
4205
4206 auto out_comp = output_point.to_compressed();
4207 std::memcpy(output_pubkey33_out, out_comp.data(), 33);
4208
4209 if (tweak32_out) {
4210 auto tweak_bytes = tweak.to_bytes();
4211 std::memcpy(tweak32_out, tweak_bytes.data(), 32);
4212 }
4213
4214 cleanup_privkeys();
4215 return UFSECP_OK;
4216 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4217}
4218
4219ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_silent_payment_scan(
4220 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4221 const uint8_t scan_privkey[32],
4222 const uint8_t spend_privkey[32],
4223 const uint8_t* input_pubkeys33, size_t n_input_pubkeys,
4224 const uint8_t* output_xonly32, size_t n_outputs,
4225 uint32_t* found_indices_out,
4226 uint8_t* found_privkeys_out,
4227 size_t* n_found) {
4228 if (!ctx || !scan_privkey || !spend_privkey || !input_pubkeys33 ||
4229 !output_xonly32 || !n_found) {
4230 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4231 }
4232 if (n_input_pubkeys == 0 || n_outputs == 0) {
4233 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
4234 }
4235 if (n_input_pubkeys > kMaxBatchN || n_outputs > kMaxBatchN)
4236 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "input/output count too large");
4237 std::size_t pk_bytes = 0, out_bytes = 0;
4238 if (!checked_mul_size(n_input_pubkeys, std::size_t{33}, pk_bytes)
4239 || !checked_mul_size(n_outputs, std::size_t{32}, out_bytes))
4240 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "silent payment array size overflow");
4241 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4242 try {
4243
4244 Scalar scan_sk, spend_sk;
4245 auto cleanup = [&]() {
4246 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&scan_sk, sizeof(scan_sk));
4247 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&spend_sk, sizeof(spend_sk));
4248 };
4249 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(scan_privkey, scan_sk)) {
4250 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "scan privkey is zero or >= n");
4251 }
4252 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(spend_privkey, spend_sk)) {
4253 cleanup();
4254 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "spend privkey is zero or >= n");
4255 }
4256
4257 // Parse input pubkeys
4258 std::vector<Point> input_pks;
4259 input_pks.reserve(n_input_pubkeys);
4260 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_input_pubkeys; ++i) {
4261 auto pk = point_from_compressed(input_pubkeys33 + i * 33);
4262 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
4263 cleanup();
4264 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid input pubkey");
4265 }
4266 input_pks.push_back(pk);
4267 }
4268
4269 // Parse output x-only pubkeys
4270 std::vector<std::array<uint8_t, 32>> outputs;
4271 outputs.reserve(n_outputs);
4272 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_outputs; ++i) {
4273 std::array<uint8_t, 32> x;
4274 std::memcpy(x.data(), output_xonly32 + i * 32, 32);
4275 outputs.push_back(x);
4276 }
4277
4278 auto results = secp256k1::silent_payment_scan(scan_sk, spend_sk, input_pks, outputs);
4279
4280 size_t const capacity = *n_found;
4281 size_t const count = results.size() < capacity ? results.size() : capacity;
4282 *n_found = results.size();
4283
4284 for (size_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
4285 if (found_indices_out) found_indices_out[i] = results[i].first;
4286 if (found_privkeys_out) {
4287 auto key_bytes = results[i].second.to_bytes();
4288 std::memcpy(found_privkeys_out + i * 32, key_bytes.data(), 32);
4289 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(key_bytes.data(), key_bytes.size());
4290 }
4291 }
4292
4293 // Erase result private keys from heap before vector destruction
4294 for (auto& r : results) {
4295 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&r.second, sizeof(r.second));
4296 }
4297
4298 cleanup();
4299 return UFSECP_OK;
4300 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4301}
4302
4303/* ===========================================================================
4304 * ECIES (Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme)
4305 * =========================================================================== */
4306
4307ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecies_encrypt(
4308 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4309 const uint8_t recipient_pubkey33[33],
4310 const uint8_t* plaintext, size_t plaintext_len,
4311 uint8_t* envelope_out, size_t* envelope_len) {
4312 if (!ctx || !recipient_pubkey33 || !plaintext || !envelope_out || !envelope_len) {
4313 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4314 }
4315 if (plaintext_len == 0) {
4316 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
4317 }
4318 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4319
4320 if (plaintext_len > SIZE_MAX - UFSECP_ECIES_OVERHEAD) {
4321 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "plaintext_len too large");
4322 }
4323 size_t const needed = plaintext_len + UFSECP_ECIES_OVERHEAD;
4324 if (*envelope_len < needed) {
4325 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "envelope buffer too small");
4326 }
4327
4328 auto pk = point_from_compressed(recipient_pubkey33);
4329 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
4330 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid recipient pubkey");
4331 }
4332 try {
4333 auto envelope = secp256k1::ecies_encrypt(pk, plaintext, plaintext_len);
4334 if (envelope.empty()) {
4335 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "ECIES encryption failed");
4336 }
4337
4338 std::memcpy(envelope_out, envelope.data(), envelope.size());
4339 *envelope_len = envelope.size();
4340 return UFSECP_OK;
4341 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4342}
4343
4344ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ecies_decrypt(
4345 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4346 const uint8_t privkey[32],
4347 const uint8_t* envelope, size_t envelope_len,
4348 uint8_t* plaintext_out, size_t* plaintext_len) {
4349 if (!ctx || !privkey || !envelope || !plaintext_out || !plaintext_len) {
4350 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4351 }
4352 if (envelope_len < 82) { // min: 33 + 16 + 1 + 32
4353 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
4354 }
4355 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4356
4357 size_t const expected_pt_len = envelope_len - UFSECP_ECIES_OVERHEAD;
4358 if (*plaintext_len < expected_pt_len) {
4359 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "plaintext buffer too small");
4360 }
4361
4362 Scalar sk;
4363 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
4364 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
4365 }
4366 try {
4367 auto pt = secp256k1::ecies_decrypt(sk, envelope, envelope_len);
4368 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4369
4370 if (pt.empty()) {
4371 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "ECIES decryption failed (bad key or tampered)");
4372 }
4373
4374 std::memcpy(plaintext_out, pt.data(), pt.size());
4375 *plaintext_len = pt.size();
4376 return UFSECP_OK;
4377 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4378}
4379
4380/* ===========================================================================
4381 * BIP-324: Version 2 P2P Encrypted Transport (conditional: SECP256K1_BIP324)
4382 * =========================================================================== */
4383
4384#if defined(SECP256K1_BIP324)
4385
4386struct ufsecp_bip324_session {
4387 secp256k1::Bip324Session* cpp_session;
4388};
4389
4390ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip324_create(
4391 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4392 int initiator,
4393 ufsecp_bip324_session** session_out,
4394 uint8_t ellswift64_out[64]) {
4395 if (!ctx || !session_out || !ellswift64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4396 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4397 *session_out = nullptr;
4398 if (initiator != 0 && initiator != 1) {
4399 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "initiator must be 0 or 1");
4400 }
4401
4402 auto* sess = new (std::nothrow) ufsecp_bip324_session;
4403 if (!sess) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "allocation failed");
4404 sess->cpp_session = nullptr;
4405
4406 sess->cpp_session = new (std::nothrow) secp256k1::Bip324Session(initiator == 1);
4407 if (!sess->cpp_session) {
4408 delete sess;
4409 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "allocation failed");
4410 }
4411
4412 auto& enc = sess->cpp_session->our_ellswift_encoding();
4413 std::memcpy(ellswift64_out, enc.data(), 64);
4414
4415 *session_out = sess;
4416 return UFSECP_OK;
4417}
4418
4419ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip324_handshake(
4420 ufsecp_bip324_session* session,
4421 const uint8_t peer_ellswift64[64],
4422 uint8_t session_id32_out[32]) {
4423 if (!session || !session->cpp_session || !peer_ellswift64) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4424
4425 if (!session->cpp_session->complete_handshake(peer_ellswift64)) {
4426 return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL;
4427 }
4428
4429 if (session_id32_out) {
4430 auto& sid = session->cpp_session->session_id();
4431 std::memcpy(session_id32_out, sid.data(), 32);
4432 }
4433 return UFSECP_OK;
4434}
4435
4436ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip324_encrypt(
4437 ufsecp_bip324_session* session,
4438 const uint8_t* plaintext, size_t plaintext_len,
4439 uint8_t* out, size_t* out_len) {
4440 if (!session || !session->cpp_session || !out || !out_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4441 if (!plaintext && plaintext_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4442 if (plaintext_len > SIZE_MAX - 19) return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
4443
4444 size_t const needed = plaintext_len + 19; // 3 (length) + payload + 16 (tag)
4445 if (*out_len < needed) return UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL;
4446 try {
4447 auto enc = session->cpp_session->encrypt(plaintext, plaintext_len);
4448 if (enc.empty()) return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL;
4449
4450 std::memcpy(out, enc.data(), enc.size());
4451 *out_len = enc.size();
4452 return UFSECP_OK;
4453 } catch (...) { return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; }
4454}
4455
4456ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip324_decrypt(
4457 ufsecp_bip324_session* session,
4458 const uint8_t* encrypted, size_t encrypted_len,
4459 uint8_t* plaintext_out, size_t* plaintext_len) {
4460 if (!session || !session->cpp_session || !encrypted || !plaintext_out || !plaintext_len)
4461 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4462
4463 // encrypted = [3B header][payload][16B tag], minimum length 19
4464 if (encrypted_len < 19) return UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL;
4465
4466 const uint8_t* header = encrypted;
4467 const uint8_t* payload_tag = encrypted + 3;
4468 const size_t payload_tag_len = encrypted_len - 3;
4469 try {
4470 std::vector<uint8_t> dec;
4471 if (!session->cpp_session->decrypt(header, payload_tag, payload_tag_len, dec)) {
4472 return UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL;
4473 }
4474
4475 if (*plaintext_len < dec.size()) return UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL;
4476 std::memcpy(plaintext_out, dec.data(), dec.size());
4477 *plaintext_len = dec.size();
4478 return UFSECP_OK;
4479 } catch (...) { return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; }
4480}
4481
4482void ufsecp_bip324_destroy(ufsecp_bip324_session* session) {
4483 if (session) {
4484 if (session->cpp_session) {
4485 delete session->cpp_session;
4486 }
4487 delete session;
4488 }
4489}
4490
4491ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_aead_chacha20_poly1305_encrypt(
4492 const uint8_t key[32], const uint8_t nonce[12],
4493 const uint8_t* aad, size_t aad_len,
4494 const uint8_t* plaintext, size_t plaintext_len,
4495 uint8_t* out, uint8_t tag[16]) {
4496 if (!key || !nonce || !out || !tag) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4497 if (!plaintext && plaintext_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4498 if (!aad && aad_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4499
4500 secp256k1::aead_chacha20_poly1305_encrypt(
4501 key, nonce, aad, aad_len, plaintext, plaintext_len, out, tag);
4502 return UFSECP_OK;
4503}
4504
4505ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_aead_chacha20_poly1305_decrypt(
4506 const uint8_t key[32], const uint8_t nonce[12],
4507 const uint8_t* aad, size_t aad_len,
4508 const uint8_t* ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len,
4509 const uint8_t tag[16], uint8_t* out) {
4510 if (!key || !nonce || !tag || !out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4511 if (!ciphertext && ciphertext_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4512 if (!aad && aad_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4513
4514 bool ok = secp256k1::aead_chacha20_poly1305_decrypt(
4515 key, nonce, aad, aad_len, ciphertext, ciphertext_len, tag, out);
4516 return ok ? UFSECP_OK : UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL;
4517}
4518
4519ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ellswift_create(
4520 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4521 const uint8_t privkey[32],
4522 uint8_t encoding64_out[64]) {
4523 if (!ctx || !privkey || !encoding64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4524 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4525
4526 Scalar sk;
4527 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
4528 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
4529 }
4530
4531 auto enc = secp256k1::ellswift_create(sk);
4532 std::memcpy(encoding64_out, enc.data(), 64);
4533
4534 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4535 return UFSECP_OK;
4536}
4537
4538ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_ellswift_xdh(
4539 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4540 const uint8_t ell_a64[64],
4541 const uint8_t ell_b64[64],
4542 const uint8_t our_privkey[32],
4543 int initiating,
4544 uint8_t secret32_out[32]) {
4545 if (!ctx || !ell_a64 || !ell_b64 || !our_privkey || !secret32_out)
4546 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4547 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4548
4549 Scalar sk;
4550 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(our_privkey, sk)) {
4551 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
4552 }
4553
4554 auto secret = secp256k1::ellswift_xdh(ell_a64, ell_b64, sk, initiating != 0);
4555 std::memcpy(secret32_out, secret.data(), 32);
4556
4557 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4558 return UFSECP_OK;
4559}
4560
4561#endif /* SECP256K1_BIP324 */
4562
4563/* ===========================================================================
4564 * Ethereum (conditional: SECP256K1_BUILD_ETHEREUM)
4565 * =========================================================================== */
4566
4567#if defined(SECP256K1_BUILD_ETHEREUM)
4568
4569ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_keccak256(const uint8_t* data, size_t len,
4570 uint8_t digest32_out[32]) {
4571 if (!data && len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4572 if (!digest32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4573
4574 auto hash = secp256k1::coins::keccak256(data, len);
4575 std::memcpy(digest32_out, hash.data(), 32);
4576 return UFSECP_OK;
4577}
4578
4579ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_eth_address(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4580 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
4581 uint8_t addr20_out[20]) {
4582 if (!ctx || !pubkey33 || !addr20_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4583 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4584
4585 const Point pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
4586 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
4587 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid compressed pubkey");
4588 }
4589
4590 auto addr = secp256k1::coins::ethereum_address_bytes(pk);
4591 std::memcpy(addr20_out, addr.data(), 20);
4592 return UFSECP_OK;
4593}
4594
4595ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_eth_address_checksummed(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4596 const uint8_t pubkey33[33],
4597 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
4598 if (!ctx || !pubkey33 || !addr_out || !addr_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4599 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4600
4601 if (*addr_len < 43) {
4602 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "need >= 43 bytes for ETH address");
4603 }
4604
4605 const Point pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey33);
4606 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
4607 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid compressed pubkey");
4608 }
4609
4610 try {
4611 const std::string addr_str = secp256k1::coins::ethereum_address(pk);
4612 std::memcpy(addr_out, addr_str.c_str(), addr_str.size());
4613 addr_out[addr_str.size()] = '\0';
4614 *addr_len = addr_str.size();
4615 return UFSECP_OK;
4616 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4617}
4618
4619ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_eth_personal_hash(const uint8_t* msg, size_t msg_len,
4620 uint8_t digest32_out[32]) {
4621 if (!msg && msg_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4622 if (!digest32_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4623
4624 auto hash = secp256k1::coins::eip191_hash(msg, msg_len);
4625 std::memcpy(digest32_out, hash.data(), 32);
4626 return UFSECP_OK;
4627}
4628
4629ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_eth_sign(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4630 const uint8_t msg32[32],
4631 const uint8_t privkey[32],
4632 uint8_t r_out[32],
4633 uint8_t s_out[32],
4634 uint64_t* v_out,
4635 uint64_t chain_id) {
4636 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !privkey || !r_out || !s_out || !v_out) {
4637 return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4638 }
4639 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4640
4641 Scalar sk;
4642 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
4643 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
4644 }
4645
4646 std::array<uint8_t, 32> hash;
4647 std::memcpy(hash.data(), msg32, 32);
4648
4649 auto esig = secp256k1::coins::eth_sign_hash(hash, sk, chain_id);
4650 std::memcpy(r_out, esig.r.data(), 32);
4651 std::memcpy(s_out, esig.s.data(), 32);
4652 *v_out = esig.v;
4653
4654 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4655 return UFSECP_OK;
4656}
4657
4658ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_eth_ecrecover(ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4659 const uint8_t msg32[32],
4660 const uint8_t r[32],
4661 const uint8_t s[32],
4662 uint64_t v,
4663 uint8_t addr20_out[20]) {
4664 if (!ctx || !msg32 || !r || !s || !addr20_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4665 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4666
4667 std::array<uint8_t, 32> hash, r_arr, s_arr;
4668 std::memcpy(hash.data(), msg32, 32);
4669 std::memcpy(r_arr.data(), r, 32);
4670 std::memcpy(s_arr.data(), s, 32);
4671
4672 auto [addr, ok] = secp256k1::coins::ecrecover(hash, r_arr, s_arr, v);
4673 if (!ok) {
4674 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "ecrecover failed");
4675 }
4676
4677 std::memcpy(addr20_out, addr.data(), 20);
4678 return UFSECP_OK;
4679}
4680
4681#endif /* SECP256K1_BUILD_ETHEREUM */
4682
4683/* ===========================================================================
4684 * BIP-85 — Deterministic Entropy from BIP-32 Keychains
4685 * =========================================================================== */
4686
4687ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip85_entropy(
4688 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4689 const ufsecp_bip32_key* master_xprv,
4690 const char* path,
4691 uint8_t* entropy_out, size_t entropy_len) {
4692 if (!ctx || !master_xprv || !path || !entropy_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4693 if (entropy_len == 0 || entropy_len > 32) {
4694 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "entropy_len must be 1-32");
4695 }
4696 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4697
4698 if (!master_xprv->is_private) {
4699 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "BIP-85 requires xprv (private key)");
4700 }
4701
4702 secp256k1::ExtendedKey ek{};
4703 ufsecp_error_t const parse_rc = parse_bip32_key(ctx, master_xprv, ek);
4704 if (parse_rc != UFSECP_OK) return parse_rc;
4705
4706 try {
4707 // Derive at the given path (all components must be hardened per BIP-85)
4708 auto [derived, ok] = secp256k1::bip32_derive_path(ek, std::string(path));
4709 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.key.data(), ek.key.size());
4710 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(ek.chain_code.data(), ek.chain_code.size());
4711 if (!ok) {
4712 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "BIP-85 path derivation failed");
4713 }
4714
4715 // Get the derived private key
4716 auto child_privkey = derived.private_key().to_bytes();
4717 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(derived.key.data(), derived.key.size());
4718 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(derived.chain_code.data(), derived.chain_code.size());
4719
4720 // HMAC-SHA512(key="bip-85", data=child_privkey)
4721 static const uint8_t BIP85_KEY[] = {'b','i','p','-','8','5'};
4722 auto hmac = secp256k1::hmac_sha512(BIP85_KEY, 6, child_privkey.data(), 32);
4723 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(child_privkey.data(), child_privkey.size());
4724
4725 std::memcpy(entropy_out, hmac.data(), entropy_len);
4726 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(hmac.data(), hmac.size());
4727 return UFSECP_OK;
4728 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4729}
4730
4731ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip85_bip39(
4732 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4733 const ufsecp_bip32_key* master_xprv,
4734 uint32_t words, uint32_t language_index, uint32_t index,
4735 char* mnemonic_out, size_t* mnemonic_len) {
4736 if (!ctx || !master_xprv || !mnemonic_out || !mnemonic_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4737 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4738
4739 if (words != 12 && words != 18 && words != 24) {
4740 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "words must be 12, 18, or 24");
4741 }
4742 if (language_index != 0) {
4743 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "only language_index=0 (English) supported");
4744 }
4745
4746 // BIP-85 path for BIP-39: m/83696968'/39'/<language>'/<words>'/<index>'
4747 char path[128];
4748 std::snprintf(path, sizeof(path),
4749 "m/83696968'/39'/%u'/%u'/%u'",
4750 language_index, words, index);
4751
4752 // entropy_len = (words / 3) * 4
4753 size_t const entropy_len = (static_cast<size_t>(words) / 3) * 4;
4754 uint8_t entropy[32]{};
4755
4756 ufsecp_error_t rc = ufsecp_bip85_entropy(ctx, master_xprv, path, entropy, entropy_len);
4757 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) {
4758 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(entropy, sizeof(entropy));
4759 return rc;
4760 }
4761
4762 try {
4763 auto [mnemonic, ok] = secp256k1::bip39_generate(entropy_len, entropy);
4764 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(entropy, sizeof(entropy));
4765 if (!ok || mnemonic.empty()) {
4766 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL, "BIP-85 BIP-39 mnemonic generation failed");
4767 }
4768 if (*mnemonic_len < mnemonic.size() + 1) {
4769 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "mnemonic buffer too small");
4770 }
4771 std::memcpy(mnemonic_out, mnemonic.c_str(), mnemonic.size() + 1);
4772 *mnemonic_len = mnemonic.size();
4773 return UFSECP_OK;
4774 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4775}
4776
4777/* ===========================================================================
4778 * BIP-340 Variable-Length Schnorr
4779 * =========================================================================== */
4780
4781ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_sign_msg(
4782 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4783 const uint8_t privkey[32],
4784 const uint8_t* msg, size_t msg_len,
4785 const uint8_t* aux_rand32,
4786 uint8_t sig64_out[64]) {
4787 if (!ctx || !privkey || !sig64_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4788 if (!msg && msg_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4789 if (!aux_rand32) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4790 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4791
4792 // Hash the message with BIP-340 tagged hash "BIP0340/msg"
4793 static const uint8_t tag_data[] = "BIP0340/msg";
4794 uint8_t msg_hash[32];
4795 {
4796 // tagged_hash("BIP0340/msg", msg) = SHA256(SHA256(tag)||SHA256(tag)||msg)
4797 auto tag_hash = secp256k1::SHA256::hash(tag_data, sizeof(tag_data) - 1);
4798 secp256k1::SHA256 h;
4799 h.update(tag_hash.data(), 32);
4800 h.update(tag_hash.data(), 32);
4801 if (msg && msg_len > 0) h.update(msg, msg_len);
4802 auto digest = h.finalize();
4803 std::memcpy(msg_hash, digest.data(), 32);
4804 }
4805
4806 // Use the fixed 32-byte sign API
4807 uint8_t aux_arr[32];
4808 std::memcpy(aux_arr, aux_rand32, 32);
4809 return ufsecp_schnorr_sign(ctx, msg_hash, privkey, aux_arr, sig64_out);
4810}
4811
4812ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_schnorr_verify_msg(
4813 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4814 const uint8_t pubkey_x[32],
4815 const uint8_t* msg, size_t msg_len,
4816 const uint8_t sig64[64]) {
4817 if (!ctx || !pubkey_x || !sig64) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4818 if (!msg && msg_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4819 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4820
4821 static const uint8_t tag_data[] = "BIP0340/msg";
4822 uint8_t msg_hash[32];
4823 {
4824 auto tag_hash = secp256k1::SHA256::hash(tag_data, sizeof(tag_data) - 1);
4825 secp256k1::SHA256 h;
4826 h.update(tag_hash.data(), 32);
4827 h.update(tag_hash.data(), 32);
4828 if (msg && msg_len > 0) h.update(msg, msg_len);
4829 auto digest = h.finalize();
4830 std::memcpy(msg_hash, digest.data(), 32);
4831 }
4832
4833 return ufsecp_schnorr_verify(ctx, msg_hash, sig64, pubkey_x);
4834}
4835
4836/* ===========================================================================
4837 * BIP-322 — Generic Message Signing
4838 * =========================================================================== */
4839
4840ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip322_sign(
4841 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4842 const uint8_t privkey[32],
4843 ufsecp_bip322_addr_type addr_type,
4844 const uint8_t* msg, size_t msg_len,
4845 uint8_t* sig_out, size_t* sig_len) {
4846 if (!ctx || !privkey || !sig_out || !sig_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4847 if (!msg && msg_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4848 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4849
4850 // BIP-322 message hash: tagged_hash("BIP0322-signed-message", msg)
4851 static const uint8_t bip322_tag[] = "BIP0322-signed-message";
4852 uint8_t msg_hash[32];
4853 {
4854 auto tag_hash = secp256k1::SHA256::hash(bip322_tag, sizeof(bip322_tag) - 1);
4855 secp256k1::SHA256 h;
4856 h.update(tag_hash.data(), 32);
4857 h.update(tag_hash.data(), 32);
4858 if (msg && msg_len > 0) h.update(msg, msg_len);
4859 auto digest = h.finalize();
4860 std::memcpy(msg_hash, digest.data(), 32);
4861 }
4862
4863 Scalar sk;
4864 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
4865 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
4866 }
4867
4868 try {
4869 if (addr_type == UFSECP_BIP322_ADDR_P2TR) {
4870 // P2TR: Schnorr sign
4871 if (*sig_len < 64) {
4872 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "P2TR sig buffer too small (need 64)");
4873 }
4874 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr{}, aux_arr{};
4875 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg_hash, 32);
4876 auto kp = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_keypair_create(sk);
4877 auto sig = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_sign(kp, msg_arr, aux_arr);
4878 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4879 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&kp.d, sizeof(kp.d));
4880 auto bytes = sig.to_bytes();
4881 std::memcpy(sig_out, bytes.data(), 64);
4882 *sig_len = 64;
4883 } else {
4884 // P2PKH, P2WPKH, P2SH-P2WPKH: ECDSA sign
4885 if (*sig_len < 65) {
4886 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4887 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "ECDSA sig buffer too small (need 65)");
4888 }
4889 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
4890 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg_hash, 32);
4891 auto sig = secp256k1::ecdsa_sign(msg_arr, sk);
4892 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
4893 auto compact = sig.to_compact();
4894 std::memcpy(sig_out, compact.data(), 32);
4895 std::memcpy(sig_out + 32, compact.data() + 32, 32);
4896 sig_out[64] = 0x01; // SIGHASH_ALL type byte
4897 *sig_len = 65;
4898 }
4899 return UFSECP_OK;
4900 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4901}
4902
4903ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_bip322_verify(
4904 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
4905 const uint8_t* pubkey, size_t pubkey_len,
4906 ufsecp_bip322_addr_type addr_type,
4907 const uint8_t* msg, size_t msg_len,
4908 const uint8_t* sig, size_t sig_len) {
4909 if (!ctx || !pubkey || !sig) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4910 if (!msg && msg_len > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
4911 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
4912
4913 static const uint8_t bip322_tag[] = "BIP0322-signed-message";
4914 uint8_t msg_hash[32];
4915 {
4916 auto tag_hash = secp256k1::SHA256::hash(bip322_tag, sizeof(bip322_tag) - 1);
4917 secp256k1::SHA256 h;
4918 h.update(tag_hash.data(), 32);
4919 h.update(tag_hash.data(), 32);
4920 if (msg && msg_len > 0) h.update(msg, msg_len);
4921 auto digest = h.finalize();
4922 std::memcpy(msg_hash, digest.data(), 32);
4923 }
4924
4925 try {
4926 if (addr_type == UFSECP_BIP322_ADDR_P2TR) {
4927 if (pubkey_len != 32) {
4928 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "P2TR requires 32-byte x-only pubkey");
4929 }
4930 if (sig_len < 64) {
4931 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "P2TR sig must be at least 64 bytes");
4932 }
4933 return ufsecp_schnorr_verify(ctx, msg_hash, sig, pubkey);
4934 } else {
4935 if (pubkey_len != 33) {
4936 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "requires 33-byte compressed pubkey");
4937 }
4938 if (sig_len < 64) {
4939 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_SIG, "sig must be at least 64 bytes");
4940 }
4941 auto pk = point_from_compressed(pubkey);
4942 if (pk.is_infinity()) {
4943 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_PUBKEY, "invalid pubkey");
4944 }
4945 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
4946 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), msg_hash, 32);
4947 // Build compact sig from first 64 bytes
4948 std::array<uint8_t, 64> compact64;
4949 std::memcpy(compact64.data(), sig, 64);
4950 auto esig = secp256k1::ECDSASignature::from_compact(compact64);
4951 bool ok = secp256k1::ecdsa_verify(msg_arr, pk, esig);
4952 if (!ok) return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_VERIFY_FAIL, "BIP-322 ECDSA verify failed");
4953 return UFSECP_OK;
4954 }
4955 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
4956}
4957
4958/* ===========================================================================
4959 * BIP-157/158 — Compact Block Filters (Golomb-Coded Set)
4960 * =========================================================================== */
4961
4962// SipHash-2-4 implementation (for GCS)
4963namespace {
4964
4965/* Portable 64×64→high-64 multiply.
4966 * Provided by secp256k1::detail::mulhi64 (arith64.hpp).
4967 * Alias it into the anonymous namespace used by the GCS code below. */
4968using secp256k1::detail::mulhi64;
4969
4970static inline uint64_t siphash_rotl64(uint64_t x, int b) {
4971 return (x << b) | (x >> (64 - b));
4972}
4973
4974static uint64_t siphash24(const uint8_t key[16], const uint8_t* data, size_t len) {
4975 uint64_t k0, k1;
4976 std::memcpy(&k0, key, 8);
4977 std::memcpy(&k1, key + 8, 8);
4978
4979 uint64_t v0 = k0 ^ 0x736f6d6570736575ULL;
4980 uint64_t v1 = k1 ^ 0x646f72616e646f6dULL;
4981 uint64_t v2 = k0 ^ 0x6c7967656e657261ULL;
4982 uint64_t v3 = k1 ^ 0x7465646279746573ULL;
4983
4984 auto sipround = [&]() {
4985 v0 += v1; v1 = siphash_rotl64(v1, 13); v1 ^= v0; v0 = siphash_rotl64(v0, 32);
4986 v2 += v3; v3 = siphash_rotl64(v3, 16); v3 ^= v2;
4987 v0 += v3; v3 = siphash_rotl64(v3, 21); v3 ^= v0;
4988 v2 += v1; v1 = siphash_rotl64(v1, 17); v1 ^= v2; v2 = siphash_rotl64(v2, 32);
4989 };
4990
4991 size_t i = 0;
4992 for (; i + 8 <= len; i += 8) {
4993 uint64_t m;
4994 std::memcpy(&m, data + i, 8);
4995 v3 ^= m;
4996 sipround(); sipround();
4997 v0 ^= m;
4998 }
4999 // Remaining bytes + length byte
5000 uint64_t last = static_cast<uint64_t>(len & 0xff) << 56;
5001 size_t rem = len - i;
5002 for (size_t j = 0; j < rem; ++j) last |= static_cast<uint64_t>(data[i + j]) << (j * 8);
5003 v3 ^= last;
5004 sipround(); sipround();
5005 v0 ^= last;
5006 v2 ^= 0xff;
5007 sipround(); sipround(); sipround(); sipround();
5008 return v0 ^ v1 ^ v2 ^ v3;
5009}
5010
5011} // anonymous namespace
5012
5013static constexpr uint64_t GCS_P = 19;
5014static constexpr uint64_t GCS_M = 784931ULL;
5015
5016// Encode a GCS filter: sort, delta-encode, Golomb-Rice encode with P=19
5017static bool gcs_encode(const std::vector<uint64_t>& values,
5018 uint8_t* out, size_t* out_len) {
5019 std::vector<uint64_t> sorted = values;
5020 std::sort(sorted.begin(), sorted.end());
5021
5022 // Golomb-Rice encode: quotient (unary) + P-bit remainder
5023 // Pack bits into bytes
5024 std::vector<uint8_t> bits;
5025 bits.reserve(sorted.size() * 4);
5026
5027 uint64_t prev = 0;
5028 for (uint64_t v : sorted) {
5029 uint64_t delta = v - prev;
5030 prev = v;
5031 uint64_t q = delta >> GCS_P;
5032 uint64_t r = delta & ((1ULL << GCS_P) - 1);
5033 // Write q ones then a zero (unary)
5034 for (uint64_t i = 0; i < q; ++i) bits.push_back(1);
5035 bits.push_back(0);
5036 // Write P-bit remainder, MSB first
5037 for (int i = static_cast<int>(GCS_P) - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
5038 bits.push_back(static_cast<uint8_t>((r >> i) & 1));
5039 }
5040 }
5041
5042 // Pack bits into bytes
5043 size_t nbytes = (bits.size() + 7) / 8;
5044 if (*out_len < nbytes) return false;
5045 std::memset(out, 0, nbytes);
5046 for (size_t i = 0; i < bits.size(); ++i) {
5047 if (bits[i]) out[i / 8] |= static_cast<uint8_t>(0x80u >> (i % 8));
5048 }
5049 *out_len = nbytes;
5050 return true;
5051}
5052
5053static bool gcs_decode(const uint8_t* filter, size_t filter_len, size_t n_items,
5054 std::vector<uint64_t>& out) {
5055 out.clear();
5056 out.reserve(n_items);
5057
5058 // Unpack bits
5059 size_t bit_pos = 0;
5060 auto read_bit = [&]() -> int {
5061 if (bit_pos / 8 >= filter_len) return -1;
5062 return (filter[bit_pos / 8] >> (7 - (bit_pos % 8))) & 1;
5063 };
5064
5065 uint64_t prev = 0;
5066 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_items; ++i) {
5067 // Read unary quotient
5068 uint64_t q = 0;
5069 while (true) {
5070 int b = read_bit();
5071 if (b < 0) return false;
5072 ++bit_pos;
5073 if (b == 0) break;
5074 ++q;
5075 }
5076 // Read P-bit remainder
5077 uint64_t r = 0;
5078 for (size_t j = 0; j < GCS_P; ++j) {
5079 int b = read_bit();
5080 if (b < 0) return false;
5081 ++bit_pos;
5082 r = (r << 1) | static_cast<uint64_t>(b);
5083 }
5084 uint64_t delta = (q << GCS_P) | r;
5085 prev += delta;
5086 out.push_back(prev);
5087 }
5088 return true;
5089}
5090
5091ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_gcs_build(
5092 const uint8_t key[16],
5093 const uint8_t** data, const size_t* data_sizes, size_t count,
5094 uint8_t* filter_out, size_t* filter_len) {
5095 if (!key || !filter_out || !filter_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5096 if (!data && count > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5097 if (!data_sizes && count > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5098
5099 try {
5100 uint64_t const modulus = static_cast<uint64_t>(count) * GCS_M;
5101 std::vector<uint64_t> hashed;
5102 hashed.reserve(count);
5103
5104 for (size_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
5105 uint64_t h = siphash24(key, data[i], data_sizes[i]);
5106 // Reduce modulo N*M using multiplication technique to avoid bias
5107 hashed.push_back(mulhi64(h, modulus));
5108 }
5109
5110 if (!gcs_encode(hashed, filter_out, filter_len)) {
5111 return UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL;
5112 }
5113 return UFSECP_OK;
5114 } catch (...) { return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; }
5115}
5116
5117ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_gcs_match(
5118 const uint8_t key[16],
5119 const uint8_t* filter, size_t filter_len,
5120 size_t n_items,
5121 const uint8_t* item, size_t item_len) {
5122 if (!key || !filter || !item) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5123
5124 try {
5125 std::vector<uint64_t> decoded;
5126 if (!gcs_decode(filter, filter_len, n_items, decoded)) {
5127 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
5128 }
5129
5130 uint64_t const modulus = static_cast<uint64_t>(n_items) * GCS_M;
5131 uint64_t h = siphash24(key, item, item_len);
5132 uint64_t target = mulhi64(h, modulus);
5133
5134 for (uint64_t v : decoded) {
5135 if (v == target) return UFSECP_OK;
5136 if (v > target) break;
5137 }
5138 return UFSECP_ERR_NOT_FOUND;
5139 } catch (...) { return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; }
5140}
5141
5142ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_gcs_match_any(
5143 const uint8_t key[16],
5144 const uint8_t* filter, size_t filter_len,
5145 size_t n_items,
5146 const uint8_t** query, const size_t* query_sizes, size_t query_count) {
5147 if (!key || !filter) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5148 if (!query && query_count > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5149 if (!query_sizes && query_count > 0) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5150
5151 try {
5152 std::vector<uint64_t> decoded;
5153 if (!gcs_decode(filter, filter_len, n_items, decoded)) {
5154 return UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT;
5155 }
5156
5157 uint64_t const modulus = static_cast<uint64_t>(n_items) * GCS_M;
5158 for (size_t qi = 0; qi < query_count; ++qi) {
5159 uint64_t h = siphash24(key, query[qi], query_sizes[qi]);
5160 uint64_t target = mulhi64(h, modulus);
5161 for (uint64_t v : decoded) {
5162 if (v == target) return UFSECP_OK;
5163 if (v > target) break;
5164 }
5165 }
5166 return UFSECP_ERR_NOT_FOUND;
5167 } catch (...) { return UFSECP_ERR_INTERNAL; }
5168}
5169
5170/* ===========================================================================
5171 * BIP-174/370 — PSBT Signing Helpers
5172 * =========================================================================== */
5173
5174ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_psbt_sign_legacy(
5175 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
5176 const uint8_t sighash32[32],
5177 const uint8_t privkey[32],
5178 uint8_t sighash_type,
5179 uint8_t* sig_out, size_t* sig_len) {
5180 if (!ctx || !sighash32 || !privkey || !sig_out || !sig_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5181 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
5182
5183 Scalar sk;
5184 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
5185 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
5186 }
5187
5188 if (*sig_len < 73) {
5189 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "legacy sig buffer too small (need 73)");
5190 }
5191
5192 try {
5193 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
5194 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), sighash32, 32);
5195 auto sig = secp256k1::ecdsa_sign(msg_arr, sk);
5196 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
5197
5198 // DER encode: extract r and s from compact encoding
5199 auto compact = sig.to_compact();
5200 std::array<uint8_t, 32> r_arr, s_arr;
5201 std::memcpy(r_arr.data(), compact.data(), 32);
5202 std::memcpy(s_arr.data(), compact.data() + 32, 32);
5203 // Build DER manually
5204 uint8_t der[72];
5205 size_t der_len = 0;
5206 // r: add 0x00 prefix if high bit set
5207 uint8_t r_data[33], s_data[33];
5208 size_t r_len = 0, s_len = 0;
5209 // Strip leading zeros from r
5210 size_t r_start = 0;
5211 while (r_start < 31 && r_arr[r_start] == 0) ++r_start;
5212 if (r_arr[r_start] & 0x80) { r_data[0] = 0x00; ++r_len; }
5213 for (size_t i = r_start; i < 32; ++i) r_data[r_len++] = r_arr[i];
5214 // Strip leading zeros from s
5215 size_t s_start = 0;
5216 while (s_start < 31 && s_arr[s_start] == 0) ++s_start;
5217 if (s_arr[s_start] & 0x80) { s_data[0] = 0x00; ++s_len; }
5218 for (size_t i = s_start; i < 32; ++i) s_data[s_len++] = s_arr[i];
5219
5220 size_t total = 2 + r_len + 2 + s_len;
5221 der[der_len++] = 0x30;
5222 der[der_len++] = static_cast<uint8_t>(total);
5223 der[der_len++] = 0x02;
5224 der[der_len++] = static_cast<uint8_t>(r_len);
5225 std::memcpy(der + der_len, r_data, r_len); der_len += r_len;
5226 der[der_len++] = 0x02;
5227 der[der_len++] = static_cast<uint8_t>(s_len);
5228 std::memcpy(der + der_len, s_data, s_len); der_len += s_len;
5229
5230 if (*sig_len < der_len + 1) {
5231 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "legacy sig buffer too small");
5232 }
5233 std::memcpy(sig_out, der, der_len);
5234 sig_out[der_len] = sighash_type;
5235 *sig_len = der_len + 1;
5236 return UFSECP_OK;
5237 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
5238}
5239
5240ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_psbt_sign_segwit(
5241 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
5242 const uint8_t sighash32[32],
5243 const uint8_t privkey[32],
5244 uint8_t sighash_type,
5245 uint8_t* sig_out, size_t* sig_len) {
5246 if (!ctx || !sighash32 || !privkey || !sig_out || !sig_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5247 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
5248
5249 if (*sig_len < 65) {
5250 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "segwit sig buffer too small (need 65)");
5251 }
5252
5253 Scalar sk;
5254 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
5255 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
5256 }
5257
5258 try {
5259 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
5260 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), sighash32, 32);
5261 auto sig = secp256k1::ecdsa_sign(msg_arr, sk);
5262 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
5263 auto compact = sig.to_compact();
5264 std::memcpy(sig_out, compact.data(), 32);
5265 std::memcpy(sig_out + 32, compact.data() + 32, 32);
5266 sig_out[64] = sighash_type;
5267 *sig_len = 65;
5268 return UFSECP_OK;
5269 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
5270}
5271
5272ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_psbt_sign_taproot(
5273 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
5274 const uint8_t sighash32[32],
5275 const uint8_t privkey[32],
5276 uint8_t sighash_type,
5277 const uint8_t* aux_rand32,
5278 uint8_t* sig_out, size_t* sig_len) {
5279 if (!ctx || !sighash32 || !privkey || !sig_out || !sig_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5280 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
5281
5282 size_t const expected_len = (sighash_type == UFSECP_SIGHASH_DEFAULT) ? 64 : 65;
5283 if (*sig_len < expected_len) {
5284 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BUF_TOO_SMALL, "taproot sig buffer too small");
5285 }
5286
5287 Scalar sk;
5288 if (!scalar_parse_strict_nonzero(privkey, sk)) {
5289 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY, "privkey is zero or >= n");
5290 }
5291
5292 try {
5293 std::array<uint8_t, 32> msg_arr;
5294 std::array<uint8_t, 32> aux_arr{};
5295 std::memcpy(msg_arr.data(), sighash32, 32);
5296 if (aux_rand32) std::memcpy(aux_arr.data(), aux_rand32, 32);
5297
5298 auto kp = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_keypair_create(sk);
5299 auto sig = secp256k1::ct::schnorr_sign(kp, msg_arr, aux_arr);
5300 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&sk, sizeof(sk));
5301 secp256k1::detail::secure_erase(&kp.d, sizeof(kp.d));
5302
5303 auto bytes = sig.to_bytes();
5304 std::memcpy(sig_out, bytes.data(), 64);
5305 if (sighash_type != UFSECP_SIGHASH_DEFAULT) {
5306 sig_out[64] = sighash_type;
5307 *sig_len = 65;
5308 } else {
5309 *sig_len = 64;
5310 }
5311 return UFSECP_OK;
5312 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
5313}
5314
5315ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_psbt_derive_key(
5316 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
5317 const ufsecp_bip32_key* master_xprv,
5318 const char* key_path,
5319 uint8_t privkey_out[32]) {
5320 if (!ctx || !master_xprv || !key_path || !privkey_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5321 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
5322
5323 ufsecp_bip32_key derived{};
5324 ufsecp_error_t rc = ufsecp_bip32_derive_path(ctx, master_xprv, key_path, &derived);
5325 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) return rc;
5326
5327 return ufsecp_bip32_privkey(ctx, &derived, privkey_out);
5328}
5329
5330/* ===========================================================================
5331 * BIP-380..386 — Output Descriptors (key expression parser)
5332 * =========================================================================== */
5333
5334ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_descriptor_parse(
5335 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
5336 const char* descriptor,
5337 uint32_t index,
5338 ufsecp_desc_key* key_out,
5339 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
5340 if (!ctx || !descriptor || !key_out) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5341 if (addr_out && !addr_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5342 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
5343
5344 std::string desc(descriptor);
5345
5346 try {
5347 // Determine descriptor type by prefix
5348 ufsecp_desc_type dtype = UFSECP_DESC_WPKH;
5349 std::string inner;
5350 std::string outer_func;
5351
5352 auto strip_func = [](const std::string& s, const std::string& fname, std::string& inner_out) -> bool {
5353 if (s.size() <= fname.size() + 2) return false;
5354 if (s.substr(0, fname.size() + 1) != fname + "(") return false;
5355 if (s.back() != ')') return false;
5356 inner_out = s.substr(fname.size() + 1, s.size() - fname.size() - 2);
5357 return true;
5358 };
5359
5360 if (strip_func(desc, "wpkh", inner)) {
5361 dtype = UFSECP_DESC_WPKH;
5362 } else if (desc.substr(0, 8) == "sh(wpkh(" && desc.back() == ')' &&
5363 desc[desc.size()-2] == ')') {
5364 dtype = UFSECP_DESC_SH_WPKH;
5365 inner = desc.substr(8, desc.size() - 10); // strip sh(wpkh( and ))
5366 } else if (strip_func(desc, "tr", inner)) {
5367 dtype = UFSECP_DESC_TR;
5368 } else if (strip_func(desc, "pkh", inner)) {
5369 dtype = UFSECP_DESC_PKH;
5370 } else if (strip_func(desc, "pk", inner)) {
5371 dtype = UFSECP_DESC_PK;
5372 } else {
5373 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "unsupported descriptor type");
5374 }
5375
5376 // Validate no extra ')' in inner
5377 // Find path suffix: inner may be "xpub.../path" or "xpub.../path/*"
5378 // or just a raw hex pubkey
5379 key_out->type = dtype;
5380 key_out->network = UFSECP_NET_MAINNET;
5381 key_out->path[0] = '\0';
5382
5383 // Find the key part: if it starts with xpub/xprv, parse BIP-32
5384 std::string key_str, path_suffix;
5385 auto slash_pos = inner.find('/');
5386 if (slash_pos != std::string::npos) {
5387 key_str = inner.substr(0, slash_pos);
5388 path_suffix = inner.substr(slash_pos);
5389 } else {
5390 key_str = inner;
5391 }
5392
5393 // Validate path_suffix - reject non-numeric characters besides / and '*
5394 for (char c : path_suffix) {
5395 if (c != '/' && c != '*' && c != '\'' && !std::isdigit((unsigned char)c) && c != ';' && c != '<' && c != '>') {
5396 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid path characters");
5397 }
5398 }
5399
5400 uint8_t pubkey33[33]{};
5401 int net = UFSECP_NET_MAINNET;
5402
5403 if (key_str.size() == 111 || key_str.size() == 112 || // xpub
5404 (key_str.substr(0,4) == "xpub" || key_str.substr(0,4) == "xprv" ||
5405 key_str.substr(0,4) == "tpub" || key_str.substr(0,4) == "tprv")) {
5406 // BIP-32 extended key
5407 if (key_str[0] == 't') net = UFSECP_NET_TESTNET;
5408
5409 // Decode the xpub/xprv using base58check
5410 auto [data, valid] = secp256k1::base58check_decode(key_str);
5411 if (!valid || data.size() < 78) {
5412 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid xpub/xprv in descriptor");
5413 }
5414
5415 // Build ufsecp_bip32_key
5416 ufsecp_bip32_key bip32k{};
5417 std::memcpy(bip32k.data, data.data(), 78);
5418 // Determine if private
5419 uint32_t ver = (uint32_t(data[0]) << 24) | (uint32_t(data[1]) << 16) |
5420 (uint32_t(data[2]) << 8) | uint32_t(data[3]);
5421 bip32k.is_private = (ver == 0x0488ADE4u || ver == 0x04358394u) ? 1 : 0;
5422
5423 // Derive path + index
5424 std::string derive_path = "m";
5425 if (!path_suffix.empty()) {
5426 // Replace '*' with index, handle <a;b> range as index selection
5427 std::string ps = path_suffix;
5428 // Handle <0;1> style
5429 auto ab = ps.find('<');
5430 if (ab != std::string::npos) {
5431 auto ae = ps.find('>', ab);
5432 if (ae != std::string::npos) ps.replace(ab, ae - ab + 1, "0");
5433 }
5434 // Replace * with index
5435 auto star = ps.find('*');
5436 if (star != std::string::npos) {
5437 ps.replace(star, 1, std::to_string(index));
5438 }
5439 derive_path += ps;
5440 }
5441
5442 ufsecp_bip32_key derived{};
5443 if (derive_path != "m") {
5444 ufsecp_ctx* dummy = ctx;
5445 ufsecp_error_t rc = ufsecp_bip32_derive_path(dummy, &bip32k, derive_path.c_str(), &derived);
5446 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) return rc;
5447 } else {
5448 derived = bip32k;
5449 }
5450
5451 // Extract pubkey
5452 ufsecp_error_t rc = ufsecp_bip32_pubkey(ctx, &derived, pubkey33);
5453 if (rc != UFSECP_OK) return rc;
5454 } else if (key_str.size() == 64 || key_str.size() == 66) {
5455 // Raw hex pubkey
5456 if (key_str.size() % 2 != 0) {
5457 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "odd hex length in descriptor");
5458 }
5459 size_t expected_bytes = key_str.size() / 2;
5460 if (expected_bytes != 33 && expected_bytes != 32) {
5461 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "pubkey must be 33 or 32 bytes");
5462 }
5463 for (size_t i = 0; i < key_str.size(); i += 2) {
5464 unsigned int byte_val = 0;
5465 // Manual hex parse
5466 auto hexchar = [](char c) -> int {
5467 if (c >= '0' && c <= '9') return c - '0';
5468 if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f') return c - 'a' + 10;
5469 if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F') return c - 'A' + 10;
5470 return -1;
5471 };
5472 int hi = hexchar(key_str[i]), lo = hexchar(key_str[i+1]);
5473 if (hi < 0 || lo < 0) {
5474 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "invalid hex in descriptor key");
5475 }
5476 byte_val = static_cast<unsigned int>(hi * 16 + lo);
5477 pubkey33[i / 2] = static_cast<uint8_t>(byte_val);
5478 }
5479 } else {
5480 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "unrecognized key format in descriptor");
5481 }
5482
5483 // Fill key_out
5484 if (dtype == UFSECP_DESC_TR) {
5485 // x-only pubkey (skip prefix byte if 33 bytes)
5486 if (pubkey33[0] == 0x02 || pubkey33[0] == 0x03) {
5487 std::memcpy(key_out->pubkey, pubkey33 + 1, 32);
5488 key_out->pubkey_len = 32;
5489 } else {
5490 std::memcpy(key_out->pubkey, pubkey33, 32);
5491 key_out->pubkey_len = 32;
5492 }
5493 } else {
5494 std::memcpy(key_out->pubkey, pubkey33, 33);
5495 key_out->pubkey_len = 33;
5496 }
5497 key_out->network = net;
5498 if (path_suffix.size() < sizeof(key_out->path)) {
5499 std::memcpy(key_out->path, path_suffix.c_str(), path_suffix.size() + 1);
5500 } else {
5501 key_out->path[0] = '\0';
5502 }
5503
5504 // Generate address if requested
5505 if (addr_out && addr_len) {
5506 switch (dtype) {
5507 case UFSECP_DESC_WPKH:
5508 return ufsecp_addr_p2wpkh(ctx, pubkey33, net, addr_out, addr_len);
5509 case UFSECP_DESC_PKH:
5510 case UFSECP_DESC_PK:
5511 return ufsecp_addr_p2pkh(ctx, pubkey33, net, addr_out, addr_len);
5512 case UFSECP_DESC_TR: {
5513 uint8_t xonly[32];
5514 if (pubkey33[0] == 0x02 || pubkey33[0] == 0x03) {
5515 std::memcpy(xonly, pubkey33 + 1, 32);
5516 } else {
5517 std::memcpy(xonly, pubkey33, 32);
5518 }
5519 return ufsecp_addr_p2tr(ctx, xonly, net, addr_out, addr_len);
5520 }
5521 case UFSECP_DESC_SH_WPKH:
5522 return ufsecp_addr_p2sh_p2wpkh(ctx, pubkey33, net, addr_out, addr_len);
5523 default:
5524 return ctx_set_err(ctx, UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT, "unknown descriptor type for address");
5525 }
5526 }
5527
5528 return UFSECP_OK;
5529 } UFSECP_CATCH_RETURN(ctx)
5530}
5531
5532ufsecp_error_t ufsecp_descriptor_address(
5533 ufsecp_ctx* ctx,
5534 const char* descriptor,
5535 uint32_t index,
5536 char* addr_out, size_t* addr_len) {
5537 if (!ctx || !descriptor || !addr_out || !addr_len) return UFSECP_ERR_NULL_ARG;
5538 ctx_clear_err(ctx);
5539
5540 ufsecp_desc_key key_out{};
5541 return ufsecp_descriptor_parse(ctx, descriptor, index, &key_out, addr_out, addr_len);
5542}
